name: Shepherd House

name: Iqta' Land Grant

search: Iqta' Land Grant The Iqta' Land Grant is the Muslim equivalent to the Manorial Demesne, but with a twist: Unlike the European feudal system, the Iqta' was not hereditary. This discouraged investment and maintenance, so there is a 10% (Minus Ruler Administration) chance per turn that an Iqta' Land Grant degrades to a less productive domain (EVENT). name: Fallow Fields
search: Fallow Fields Letting the earth rest regularly was a common practice at this time, as otherwise the soil would become poor after a few harvests. Fallow Fields are free of upkeep costs and have a base 1/15 chance per turn to be removed and immediately produce 75 food (EVENT), although the presence of farm fields will slightly improve the odds (making the Fallow Fields more efficient). name: Poacher
search:
Poacher Event Building.

The common Poacher will hunt local game without regard for the forestry laws or the general population. In addition to the use of the appropriate Regional Decision, they can be countered with the presence of the Sworn Forester (or a Sheriff, if you are in England), a tier I Stewardship building.

EVENT: Rarely, they can turn out to be benevolent, helping feed the local populace. 41 ID_STRUC_EVT_POACHER $ID_STRUC_EVT_POACHER 1 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural         Food_Output Income_Output  0 0 0

name: Noble Poacher

Noble Poacher Event Building.

This altruistic Poacher and his vivacious companions have pledged to help the local poor in their daily struggle for sustenance. They will share what they manage to catch in the forest, be it a deer or wealthy travellers' purses … although Sworn Foresters will also look to catch them in return. 42 ID_STRUC_EVT_NOBLE_POACHER $ID_STRUC_EVT_NOBLE_POACHER 1 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural         Food_Output Income_Output  0 0 0 Loyalty Bonus: 5

search: Iqta' Land Grant (Major) The Iqta' Land Grant is the Muslim equivalent to the Manorial Demesne, but with a twist: Unlike the European feudal system, the Iqta' was not hereditary. This discouraged investment and maintenance, so there is a 10% chance per turn that an Iqta' Land Grant degrades to a less productive domain. But once in a while, with a change of owner, investments will be made and productivity will be restored. This event will also happen when a new ruler appears in the nation, as many Iqta's will be reshuffled to different grantees. (EVENT: 10% chance per turn to degrade down to subvariant III. If not then 5% to be fully restored. search: Iqta' Land Grant M. (Poor) The Iqta' Land Grant is the Muslim equivalent to the Manorial Demesne, but with a twist: Unlike the European feudal system, the Iqta' was not hereditary. This discouraged investment and maintenance, so there is a 10% chance per turn that an Iqta' Land Grant degrades to a less productive domain. But once in a while, with a change of owner, investments will be made and productivity will be restored. This event will also happen when a new ruler appears in the nation, as many Iqta's will be reshuffled to different grantees. (EVENT: 10% chance per turn to degrade down to subvariant III. If not then 5% to be fully restored. name: Rustic Water Mill
search:
Rustic Water Mill This early water mill needs significant maintenance in Wood, as the water wheel breaks often, but is still incomparable when it comes to the grinding of vast quantities of Grain. Mills are often the property of the local lord, who will lend the usage to the peasantry, thus bolstering their income and authority while improving the productivity of their lands.

The flow of the river easily replaces the several mules or dozens of ancient Roman slaves. It can be improved into a more technologically advanced Water Mill, a tier II Infrastructure building. 60 ID_STRUC_RUSTIC_WATER_MILL $ID_STRUC_RUSTIC_WATER_MILL 2 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural  Wood       Food_Output Income_Output Steward_Output  1 1 0

name: Cotton Fields
search: Cotton Fields Cotton will be used to make clothes, as Wool does, using a Spinning Mill, a Tier II Commerce building. It can lead to much higher value garments though, using Dyes. Originally cultivated only by India, its cultivation spread over time to many hot climates in Europe. Its seed can be made into consumable oil. 61 ID_STRUC_COTTON_FIELD $ID_STRUC_COTTON_FIELD 2 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural   Cotton      Food_Output  1 1 0

name: Quality Vineyard
search: Quality Vineyard After several generations, farmers will under certain conditions start to grow vineyards of fine quality grapes, which will be dearly appreciated by the wealthy and nobles (often the same people). 62 ID_STRUC_REFINED_VINEYARD $ID_STRUC_REFINED_VINEYARD 2 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural   Wine      Food_Output Income_Output  1 0 0  Vineyard

name: County Market
bonus_summary: Food 3, Money 2 | from: Livestock, Fruits, Legumes, Fishes
search:
County Market This market still specializes in agricultural products, but it has gained in size and now merchants will come to some distance to buy products in large quantities. Some men from surrounding counties will even be drawn by the activity and can be recruited by the local lord in the militia.

EVENT: Although of great value in the high season, the market will be closed in winter. 63 ID_STRUC_COUNTY_MARKET $ID_STRUC_COUNTY_MARKET 2 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural  Grain Preserved Food  Livestock Fruits Legumes Fishes Food_Output Manpower_Output Income_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 12 Trade Range Bonus: 1 Farmer's Market

name: County Market (Off-season)
search: County Market (Off-season) This is off-season for the market and there is not much activity currently. Come back next year! 64 ID_STRUC_CLOSED_COUNTY_MARKET $ID_STRUC_CLOSED_COUNTY_MARKET 2 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural           1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 4 Farmer's Market

name: Fish Farming
search: Fish Farming Freshwater fishing is a good source of Food but seldom produces enough to feed a large population. Using ponds as a fish farm on the other hand was a clever practice first developed by monks of the Cistercian order. 65 ID_STRUC_FISH_FARMING $ID_STRUC_FISH_FARMING 2 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural   Fishes      Food_Output  1 1 0 Transverse output: 4 money from Smokehouse

name: Farm Workhorses
search:
Farm Workhorses With the widespread development of craftsmen specialized in the production of harnesses and horseshoes, the usage of light horses to plough and more generally work muddy lands not fit for the work of bullocks became the norm.
This building increases by one the number of buildings slots in the region. 66 ID_STRUC_FARM_WORKHORSES $ID_STRUC_FARM_WORKHORSES 2 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural  Tools   Horses    Food_Output Bonus_Infra 1 1 0

Replaces: Fruit Trees Orchard
Transverse output: 6 money from Brewery
search: Cider Farm Cider is a fruity alcohol commonly used by the lower classes. Cider Farm does not produce more fruits than the Fruit Orchard, but provides a generous income in coins should a Brewery be installed nearby. This improved building prevents the appearance of the Large Fruit Orchard though. 67 ID_STRUC_CIDER_FARM $ID_STRUC_CIDER_FARM 2 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural   Fruits      Food_Output Income_Output  1 1 0 Transverse output: 6 money from Brewery Fruit Trees Orchard

search: Large Fruits Orchard The Large Fruit Orchard is a vast exploitation producing large amounts of fruits. name: Exotic Fruits Orchard
search:
Exotic Fruits Orchard Exotic fruits (including all citrus trees producing oranges and lemons) net a good income and are always in demand, but they have the common trait of requiring a hot climate. The debilitating heat will take a toll on the local population unless proper clothes can be provided to the workers.

EVENT: Although only used as a bonus trade good, exotic fruits are in high demand in many societies. Their inherent health benefits can provide a nationwide health bonus, based on the total number of exotic fruits the gatherers collect (1% chance per fruit). 69 ID_STRUC_EXOTIC_FRUITS_ORCHARD $ID_STRUC_EXOTIC_FRUITS_ORCHARD 2 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural   Exotic Fruits  Cloth    Food_Output Manpower_Output Income_Output Bonus_Manpower Bonus_Steward 1 1 0

name: Root Cellar
bonus_summary: Food 3, Stewardship 1 | from: Legumes, Nuts & Seeds, Pottery
search: Root Cellar An underground cellar used for long-term conservation of any food, as the temperature is kept rather low and stable. It gives a bonus against siege and can never be destroyed in case of raids, but requires Preserved Food. 70 ID_STRUC_ROOT_CELLAR $ID_STRUC_ROOT_CELLAR 2 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural  Preserved Food   Legumes Nuts & Seeds Pottery  Food_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Foodkept Bonus: 12% Communal Larder

name: Improved Crop Rotation
search: Improved Crop Rotation A replacement of the Fallow Field system, using the plough as a means to make the earth breathe better and seeding the resting field with coverage plants. Instead of keeping fields fallow half the time, they could be used every two years every three. The Improved Crop Rotation system has a base 1/15 chance per turn to be removed and immediately produce 150 food (EVENT), although the presence of Farm fields will slightly improve the odds (making the system more efficient). This represents the extra productivity achieved by the local farmlands from this practice. 71 ID_STRUC_IMPROVED_CROP_ROTATION $ID_STRUC_IMPROVED_CROP_ROTATION 2 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural         Food_Output Infra_Output  1 1 0 Food Bonus: 10%

name: Wood Cutting
search: Wood Cutting A very basic activity done by a few villagers, Wood Cutting is of great importance. The Wood trade good produced will give a decent production bonus to several buildings, like the Wheelmaker and it is also used by Harbors for ship making, and for mining operations. It can be upgraded to the Logging Camp, still a Tier I Building, which will produce more, if needed. 73 ID_STRUC_WOOD_CUTTING $ID_STRUC_WOOD_CUTTING 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Wood      Infra_Output  1 1 1

name: Stone Quarry
search:
Stone Quarry The Stone Quarry can produce large amounts of Stone that can be refined by the Stone Mason into building materials. It is the exclusive alternative to the Clay Pit, which produces less Stone but gives a specific bonus to the Brickwork and its upgrade.

Stone also finds a use in castle construction and improvements, even if the first levels of fortifications are usually made of Wood. EVENT: Could lead to the discovery of a Marble Vein. 74 ID_STRUC_STONE_QUARRY $ID_STRUC_STONE_QUARRY 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Stone      Infra_Output  1 1 1

name: Clay Pit
search:
Clay Pit The clay extracted from this site won't match the Stone output from a Stone Quarry, but it still has its uses and can be made into bricks. This building will thus grant a significant bonus to the Brickworks structure and is the exclusive alternative to a Stone Quarry (only available in rough terrain though).

The  wattle-and-daub construction material evolved in the 13th century to using cob, made from clay. This was a very durable and fireproof material that could last centuries. 75 ID_STRUC_CLAY_PIT $ID_STRUC_CLAY_PIT 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Stone      Infra_Output  1 1 1

name: Tar Pit
search:
Tar Pit Tar will only be found in sufficient quantity in swamp regions and if you have tools available. Advanced Shipyards will grant an Equipment bonus if they have access to Tar, and it can also be used by some craftsmen to render their work watertight.

This substance is used in ship making to coat hulls and prevent their fouling by small animals and algae. Tar is usually not transported any distance. 76 ID_STRUC_TAR_PIT $ID_STRUC_TAR_PIT 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Tar  Tools    Infra_Output Bonus_Infra 1 1 1

name: Bog Iron Deposit
search: Bog Iron Deposit Sometimes called peasant iron, this low-quality iron can be extracted from swamps and bogs. This is the only possible way to get Iron with Tier I buildings. In addition, it does produce a trickle of usable metal by itself, but is mostly interesting for the Iron trade good. 77 ID_STRUC_BOG_IRON_DEPOSIT $ID_STRUC_BOG_IRON_DEPOSIT 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Iron Ore      Infra_Output Metal_Output  1 1 1 Health Penalty: 4%

modifiers_note_html: Transverse output: 4 infra from Clay Pit
search: Brickworks The Brickworks will produce the Tiles & Brick trade good, which is used both for the Builder Hall (Tier II Infrastructure providing a discount on structures cost) and the Freemen Cottages (a very good Tier I Stewardship building). 78 ID_STRUC_BRICKWORKS $ID_STRUC_BRICKWORKS 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Tiles & Brick      Infra_Output  1 1 0 Transverse output: 4 infra from Clay Pit

search: Charcoal Burner Charcoal will help produce more and better metal in smelters and early furnaces. search:
Mining Exploitation The Mining Exploitation is a prerequisite for all mines (except the Bog Iron Deposit extracted in swamps). A large amount of Wood will also be necessary to consolidate the shafts of the mining site.

name: Copper Mine
search: Copper Mine Copper is of widespread use in medieval time, as it is used to make Brass, an alloy both for common implements and for work of arts. search: Zinc Mine Zinc is a metal mixed with Copper to make Brass, a commonly used alloy. It also finds some utility in medicine and alchemy. name: Tin Mine
search: Tin Mine Tin has been mined since ancient times for the production of bronze. It was also used as an alloy in making pewter, a flexible metal used extensively in the Middle Ages for the manufacture of plates, cups, bowls and utensils. Tin plating was a process of coating metal with tin to prevent rust, which was sometimes used to coat medieval construction materials such as nails and iron sheets. 85 ID_STRUC_TIN_MINE $ID_STRUC_TIN_MINE 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure Tin Vein  Tin      Infra_Output Metal_Output  1 1 1 Health Penalty: 4%

name: Crude Smelter
search: Crude Smelter A very basic iron smelter, using techniques developed in ancient times. The iron produced will often have too much carbon, leading to the so-called 'Pig Iron' of poor quality. At this point, depending on how much time the smiths could spare, it was refined further or left as is, as producing steel with such rough material was long and laborious. 86 ID_STRUC_CRUDE_SMELTER $ID_STRUC_CRUDE_SMELTER 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Iron Ore   Coal    Infra_Output Metal_Output Income_Output Bonus_Infra Bonus_Metal 1 1 1 Health Penalty: 4%

name: Village Roads
search:
Village Roads Even a network of modest roads, barely better than tracks, will help the circulation of goods and people in the region. Military units will also benefit from the increased mobility provided.
This infrastructure can also be created by a few units with engineering capabilities (Baggage Train notably). 87 ID_STRUC_TRACK $ID_STRUC_TRACK 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure         Infra_Output Income_Output  1 1 1

search: Domain Expansion I This is the first land expansion, ordered by the local lord. name: Well

name: Bloomery
search:
Bloomery A Bloomery (named after the chunk of iron it produces) is an upgrade of the Crude Smelter. It is used to melt iron at a low temperature to make wrought iron (bendable). Adding Coal (charcoal preferably) will produce some steel of average quality. Until the 13th century, it was the most advanced building in the Christian world for producing refined metal..

Muslim nations will instead upgrade to the Early Furnace, superior to the Bloomery, thanks to their knowledge of wootz steel manufacturing process. 97 ID_STRUC_BLOOMERY $ID_STRUC_BLOOMERY 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Iron Ore   Coal    Metal_Output Income_Output Bonus_Infra Bonus_Metal Bonus_Steward 1 0 0 Health Penalty: 4% Crude Smelter

name: Brass and Pewter Smelter
bonus_summary: Infra 2, Money 5, Piety 2 | from: Tin, Lead
search:
Brass and Pewter Smelter Brass was commonly used in the Middle Ages to make all sorts of implements for daily life, but also by the clergy to embellish religious structures. Brass is necessary for the Cauldronmaker, a Tier I Infrastructure building, and is used as a bonus by several other buildings.

It's an alloy made of copper and either zinc (common) or lead (rarer and more prestigious), although other metals can be used, even arsenic or aluminium.

Brass is not bronze, another copper alloy using Tin, which was much more common in ancient times. In Medieval times Tin is used to make pewter, an alloy used for decorative purposes. 98 ID_STRUC_BRASS_SMELTER $ID_STRUC_BRASS_SMELTER 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Copper Brass  Tin Lead    Bonus_Infra Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety 1 1 0 Health Penalty: 4%

Few men will want to work there, if they can find another job. 99 ID_STRUC_LOGGING_CAMP $ID_STRUC_LOGGING_CAMP 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Wood      Infra_Output Manpower_Output  1 1 0 Health Penalty: 5% Wood Cutting

name: Chalk Quarry

search: Stone Mason Having a Stone Mason in the community is a big advantage for the construction of solid, multi-story buildings including the construction of castles and fortifications. name: Early Furnace
search:
Early Furnace The Early Furnace is an upgrade of the Crude Smelter which is used to melt iron at a low temperature, making wrought iron (bendable). Adding Coal (charcoal preferably) will produce some steel of average quality. Can be upgraded further to the Tier II Furnace.

This is a superior version of the Bloomery, only available to Muslims nations, the result of contact with India and its excellent wootz steel manufacturing process. 107 ID_STRUC_MUS_EARLY_FURNACE $ID_STRUC_MUS_EARLY_FURNACE 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Iron Ore   Coal    Metal_Output Income_Output Bonus_Infra Bonus_Metal Bonus_Steward 0 1 0 Health Penalty: 4%

name: Marble Vein
search:
Marble Vein Event Building.

Sometime while digging for stones, the local workers will find the existence of a deep Marble Vein. Marble can be readily sold or be used by the Stone Cutter, a Tier 2 Infrastructure building, to embellish a lot of structures. 108 ID_STRUC_EVT_MARBLE_VEIN $ID_STRUC_EVT_MARBLE_VEIN 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Marble Vein  Tools    Infra_Output Income_Output Bonus_Income 0 0 0

name: Slave Workforce
search:
Slave Workforce This is a work camp for slaves, where they will toil the land and transport goods. Up to three Slave Workforces can be present in a given region, and they appear spontaneously if the conditions are met (a battle must have happened and religions must differ, although no Christian will capture slaves). EVENT: After a while, the camp will be removed as the slaves die from exhaustion or simply grow old.

Slavery subsided in medieval times compared to the Ancient era, in part because peasants were a cheap workforce, often bound to the land they were born. Christianity also forbade slavery of fellow Christians and the Quran also disallowed it against other Muslims.
When another religion is involved though, it was permitted although you would not often see it in practice in mainland Europe.
Norse populations, until Christianization, did capture people which were named thralls, and were slaves in all but name. Many monks and educated clergymen found themselves in Scandinavia because of Vikings raids, and it is said that this influenced the speed of spread of Christianity in the region. 109 ID_STRUC_EVT_SLAVE_WORKFORCE $ID_STRUC_EVT_SLAVE_WORKFORCE 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure     Tools    Food_Output Infra_Output Income_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Infra 0 0 0 Loyalty Penalty: 10 Revolt Increase: 35%

name: Salthouse
search: Salthouse The Salthouse will make use of Salt to preserve food, thus providing a hefty Health bonus in the region. Preserved Food can then be used either by the military or in some circumstances by the nobility. 110 ID_STRUC_SALTHOUSE $ID_STRUC_SALTHOUSE 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health  Salt Preserved Food  Livestock Fishes   Food_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Income 1 1 1 Health Bonus: 8%

name: Partial Swamp Draining
search: Partial Swamp Draining Very costly for a low-tier building, it can only be initiated in swamps and marshes. The region will have more land to be exploited afterward. EVENT: Can lead to the discovery of a Brine Well. 111 ID_STRUC_SWAMP_DRAINING1 $ID_STRUC_SWAMP_DRAINING1 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health           1 1 1

name: Leper House
search:
Leper House The Leper House gathered together, willingly or not, poor people with all sorts of skin conditions (not only leprosy), and can help in containing epidemics
It can be upgraded to a Leprosarium, but only if there is an Hostel of God or a Physician House is in the region. 112 ID_STRUC_LEPER_HOUSE $ID_STRUC_LEPER_HOUSE 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health  Drugs & Herbs   Cloth    Food_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 0 0 Health Bonus: 4% Loyalty Bonus: 4 Flavor: 0

name: Apothecary
search:
Apothecary The Apothecary has a more formal approach to disease treatment than healers and Wise Women, and is thus welcomed in any village or town. He sells medicinal plants, ointments and salves for good profit.
To store and reference properly all his products, will require an ample supply of Ceramics, produced locally (or in a region adjacent to his).

The apothecary can be directly upgraded to the Physician House, and does not require being a Medical Practitioner at any time, which is probably a good thing for the populace. 113 ID_STRUC_APOTHECARY $ID_STRUC_APOTHECARY 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health  Drugs & Herbs   Ceramics    Income_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Health Bonus: 8%

name: Barber
search: Barber Another profession that might seem to be of low importance, the Barber often doubled as a rudimentary surgeon, especially when an army is nearby! He will do what he can to fix the minor wounds (using his knives and razors!) of the soldiers, but also of the poorer peasants in the area unable to see anyone else. There is a modest (30%) chance that a unit recovering its effectiveness will also get a missing hit back, provided it is in supply (this chance is 100% with the Tier II Hospitals). 114 ID_STRUC_BARBER $ID_STRUC_BARBER 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health     Tools    Income_Output Bonus_Manpower 1 1 0 Health Bonus: 3% Hitsrecovery: 3

name: Water carrier
search:
Water carrier Water Carriers play a vital role in a tribal or nomadic society, as they are the ones who know where the springs and wells are. They will help the villagers get the water needed for drinking, cooking and washing.
EVENT:This building can help in containing epidemics. 115 ID_STRUC_TN_WATERCARRIER $ID_STRUC_TN_WATERCARRIER 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health     Tools     Bonus_Manpower 0 0 1 Health Bonus: 4%

name: Open Canalization
search: Open Canalization The complex sewer systems of the much-admired Romans is now long gone and people are reverting to the bad practices of tossing garbage (and worse) everywhere. An open canalization system is simply gutters in the street where the population can put their wastes, with the occasional flow of water to clean it from time to time. This is still a significant improvement compared to nothing, but rivers around might become polluted (EVENT). 116 ID_STRUC_OPENCANALIZATION $ID_STRUC_OPENCANALIZATION 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health     Lead     Bonus_Infra 1 1 1 Health Bonus: 9%

name: Hammam
modifiers_note_html:
Health Bonus: 9%
Transverse output: 5 money from Soap Workshop
search: Hammam Hamman, or public baths, are derived from the Roman and Byzantine thermes. In the Muslim world, they are strongly tied to the required cleanliness before the prayer. Nonetheless, they are very common in all Muslim cultures and are a definitive help when it comes to countering diseases. 117 ID_STRUC_MUS_HAMMAM $ID_STRUC_MUS_HAMMAM 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health         Income_Output  0 1 0 Health Bonus: 9% Transverse output: 5 money from Mus Soap Workshop

name: Modest Bimaristan
search:
Modest Bimaristan A modest Bimaristan is the equivalent of a Leper House for Muslim nations. Poor people with all sorts of skin conditions (not only leprosy) will be gathered there more humanely than their Christian counterparts, and can help to contain epidemics.
Zakat Collectors will collect money for it. 118 ID_STRUC_MUS_MODEST_BIMARISTAN $ID_STRUC_MUS_MODEST_BIMARISTAN 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health  Drugs & Herbs   Cloth    Food_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 0 1 0 Health Bonus: 6% Loyalty Bonus: 6 Flavor: 0

name: Clear Water
search:
Clear Water Event building.

An abundance of springs provide clear water in the region, which will provide a productivity bonus to the Watermill. 119 ID_STRUC_EVT_CLEARWATER $ID_STRUC_EVT_CLEARWATER 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health         Food_Output  0 0 0 Health Bonus: 5%

name: Rats
search:
Rats Event building.

Rats tend to appear where there is a lot of Food while Health is bad or the population count is high. Use a Rat Catcher to get rid of them (and prevent their return, most of the time). Having one hundred rats is an issue, having ten thousand is a big problem, so don't be surprised to see more and more of the varmints!

Rats are known to lead to the appearance of epidemics or even worse, like a plague (EVENT). 120 ID_STRUC_EVT_RATS $ID_STRUC_EVT_RATS 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health     Grain    Food_Output Bonus_Food 0 0 0 Health Penalty: 6% Charhealth Penalty: 3%

name: Master Herbalist
search:
Master Herbalist Rarely will a person acquire such a vast knowledge of plants and their benefits, and thus become a master of their art, the equal of Galen perhaps, who knew hundreds of medicinal plants. As with the more common Herbalist, they will allow many people to stay healthy in the region.

Weigh carefully the choice of not to building this structure, as this is a rare proposal. 121 ID_STRUC_MASTER_HERBALIST $ID_STRUC_MASTER_HERBALIST 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health   Drugs & Herbs      Manpower_Output  1 1 0 Health Bonus: 8% Herbalist

name: Master Rat Catcher
search:
Master Rat Catcher The Rat Catcher in this region is so good that one might even say he is a Master Rat Catcher! With his horde of cats and -- oddly -- a minuscule flute, he will get rid of any rats in the region in no time. Who knows, he might even travel a bit further to remove the varmints of a neighboring region (EVENT)...

Weigh carefully the choice of not building this structure, as this is a rare proposal. 122 ID_STRUC_MASTER_RAT_CATCHER $ID_STRUC_MASTER_RAT_CATCHER 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health           1 1 0  Rat Catcher

name: Builder Hall
search: Builder Hall A central place for craftsmen to discuss and plan the new major buildings of the region, in a collegial manner. It will prevent the appearance of the more solitary-minded stone cutters, experts of their craft. As an urban building, it often had an elaborate facade, made of tiles and bricks, which will place a demand on these Trade Goods. 124 ID_STRUC_BUILDER_HALL $ID_STRUC_BUILDER_HALL 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Tiles & Brick       Infra_Output  1 1 0 Jobprod1 Bonus: 2 Freemengrow Bonus: 15% Stone Mason

name: Stone Cutter
search:
Stone Cutter Stone Cutters are instrumental in the building of resilient structures, with interlocked stones that won't require any mortar. This structure prevents the construction of the Builder Hall, which is more about mutualizing resources and spreading knowledge to as many craftsmen as possible.
As the Stone Mason, this artisan will also use and demand a lot of stones. Should you prefer to make use of Tile & Bricks, the Builder Hall is a better option. 125 ID_STRUC_STONECUTTER $ID_STRUC_STONECUTTER 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Stone   Marble    Infra_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Structure Costreduction: 15% Freemengrow Penalty: 8% Stone Mason

name: Warehouses
search: Warehouses The Warehouse is a nice complement to the Builder Hall, as resources can be accumulated there before a major project. A part of it is also commonly used to store the local militia weapons. 126 ID_STRUC_WAREHOUSE $ID_STRUC_WAREHOUSE 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure         MBPStockpile Infra_Output  1 1 0 Infra Bonus: 12%

name: Iron Mine
search: Iron Mine Iron was of greatest importance in the Medieval Ages. It is converted into steel in furnaces and then processed by smiths into hundreds of different objects, tools, armor, and weapons. He who has no access to Iron is condemned to be the vassal of a nation having some! 129 ID_STRUC_IRON_MINE $ID_STRUC_IRON_MINE 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure Iron Vein  Iron Ore      Infra_Output Metal_Output  1 1 0 Health Penalty: 4%

modifiers_note_html: Transverse output: 7 infra from Water Mill
search: Sawmill The Sawmill will allow workers to exploit forests for their Wood, in a quasi-industrial manner. It benefits greatly from a Water Mill (late tier II building) which will double its infrastructure output. A Sawmill can't be built in Arid terrain, as there won't be enough wood for it. 130 ID_STRUC_SAWMILL $ID_STRUC_SAWMILL 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Wood      Infra_Output  1 1 0 Transverse output: 7 infra from Water Mill

name: Marble Quarry
search: Marble Quarry Marble is a noble material used since antiquity for architecture and sculpture. It will be used to decorate prestigious buildings and monuments like manors, royal courts, cathedrals and mosques. 131 ID_STRUC_MARBLE_QUARRY $ID_STRUC_MARBLE_QUARRY 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure Marble Vein  Marble      Infra_Output Income_Output  1 1 0

name: Tar Pit
search: Tar Pit Tar is an important material in shipbuilding to prevent fouling, the corrosion of the hull by saline water, and small sea animals. On land, builders use it for mostly the same reason, insulating structures from the weather. Providing workers with Leather outfits will go a great way in reducing the mortality rate of their job. 132 ID_STRUC_TAR_KILN $ID_STRUC_TAR_KILN 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Wood Tar  Leather    Infra_Output Manpower_Output Bonus_Manpower 1 1 0

name: Lime Kiln
search: Lime Kiln This kiln uses vast quantities of limestone and Coal (as an energy source) to make lime, a highly prized cement for construction. Lime kilns workers are often sick from the toxic fumes and complaints from the neighborhood are common, they are thus regularly pushed to the outskirts of most cities. Kiln workers toil in difficult work and providing them with Leather outfits will go a great way in reducing the mortality rate of their job. 133 ID_STRUC_LIME_KILN $ID_STRUC_LIME_KILN 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Coal   Leather    Infra_Output Manpower_Output Bonus_Manpower 1 1 0 Infra Bonus: 10% Health Penalty: 4% Loyalty Penalty: 6

Trade Acumen Bonus: 1
search: Barrels Workshop If the amphora was the staple transportation container of antiquity, the barrel is the equivalent in the medieval ages. Made of sturdy wood, barrels will allow peasants to store and transport all of their output to markets and fairs, where the goods will be further redistributed by merchants across the country and even farther. 134 ID_STRUC_BARRELS_WORKSHOP $ID_STRUC_BARRELS_WORKSHOP 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Wood   Tar    Infra_Output Income_Output Bonus_Infra 1 1 0 Transverse output: Gain 1.5 food for any building under Agriculture category. Limit 12. Trade Acumen Bonus: 1

name: Rope Maker
search: Rope Maker Rope makers will use vast quantities of hemp to make ropes (for craftsmen on land) and cordages (for ship rigging). Sailmakers will greatly benefit from the presence of a competent rope maker. 135 ID_STRUC_ROPE_MAKER $ID_STRUC_ROPE_MAKER 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Hemp       MBP_Output Infra_Output Income_Output  1 1 0 Recruit Mbp Reduction: 10% (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP)

name: Glass Blower
search: Glass Blower Glassware was already known by the Romans, but techniques have much improved since ancient times, as was the making of window panes. Transparent glass windows were rare and mostly used in prestigious buildings, with stained glass often seen in Christian cathedrals. 136 ID_STRUC_GLASSBLOWER $ID_STRUC_GLASSBLOWER 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Glassware      Infra_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output  1 1 0

name: Wheelbarrow Maker
search: Wheelbarrow Maker Wheelbarrows appeared around the XIIth century in Europe and quickly spread in many countries. The peasantry and workers could at last move easily a lot of resources with a cheap implement that could be produced by the local smiths or carpenter. 137 ID_STRUC_WHEELBARROW_MAKER $ID_STRUC_WHEELBARROW_MAKER 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure         Infra_Output  1 1 0 Food Bonus: 5%

name: Rustic Treadwheel Crane
search: Rustic Treadwheel Crane Cranes fell in disuse after the fall of the western roman empire, and only re-appeared in significant numbers around the XIIIth century, in no small part as a necessity for the construction of monumental structures such as cathedrals and mosques. The Byzantines kept using them,  derived from Roman engineering expertise, for their own monuments though. 138 ID_STRUC_RUSTICTREADWHEELCRANE $ID_STRUC_RUSTICTREADWHEELCRANE 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure           1 1 0 Infra Bonus: 18%

search: Master Toolsmith This Master Toolsmith, accompanied by many aides, will provide the community with a large number of quality Tools and implements. search:
Domain Expansion III This is the third land expansion, ordered by the local lord. With arduous work, the peasants and hired freemen can make the land more habitable, thus giving some extra room for the growing population to expand.
The expansion following this one can only be started if you have enough population and Stewardship in the region. 141 ID_STRUC_DOMAIN_EXPANSION3 $ID_STRUC_DOMAIN_EXPANSION3 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure     Tools    Steward_Output Bonus_Infra Bonus_Income 1 1 0  Domain Expansion II

name: Medical Practitioner
bonus_summary: Money 3 | from: Drugs & Herbs, Tar, Salt
modifiers_note_html:
Health Bonus: 4%
Transverse output: 5 money from Leech Gatherer
search:
Medical Practitioner The Medical Practitioner will heal people by purging the body of foul things, through bleeding, cupping and the profuse use of leeches. Most of the time, it would not harm the patient too much. This building provides Health, but can be removed spontaneously, representing some locals being furious against the medieval doctor (EVENT).

Practicing medicine in medieval ages meant you mastered the concepts of humors, yellow bile, phlegm, black bile, and blood, as described by Hippocrates. In theory as until the late XIIth century, the concept itself of a medical license did not even surface. 142 ID_STRUC_MEDICAL_PRACTITIONER $ID_STRUC_MEDICAL_PRACTITIONER 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health     Drugs & Herbs Tar Salt   Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Health Bonus: 4% Transverse output: 5 money from Leech Gatherer

name: Restored Aqueduct
search:
Restored Aqueduct The local aqueduct, built by the wise and powerful Romans can be restored, if we put a lot of effort into it. This is a rare opportunity, and despite the costs, what it will bring to the city won't be matched by our current water system. Only regions with significant Stewardship will have the resolve to engage in such monumental restoration task.

Aqueducts are very much associated with Roman antiquity, justly so. And yet several aqueducts were known to work up to the XIIth century, some being even restored and maintained, like in the Byzantium empire or in Valencia (Spain). Later in the period, the Ottomans resumed this kind of monumental structure, for their own cities. 143 ID_STRUC_RESTORED_AQUEDUCT $ID_STRUC_RESTORED_AQUEDUCT 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health  Stone         1 1 0 Health Bonus: 15% Loyalty Bonus: 10

name: Wishing Well
search:
Wishing Well This rare upgrade of a Well doesn't deliver much more water and will prevent the upgrade to a Deep Well.

EVENT: The building once in a while (when the commoners throw a piece or two) will generate 'luck', an unfathomable resource that will be used automatically to avoid character's fate events, death in battles and assassination attempts.

Weight carefully the choice of not building this structure, as this is a rare proposal. 144 ID_STRUC_WISHING_WELL $ID_STRUC_WISHING_WELL 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health         Income_Output  1 1 0 Newborn Bonus: 5% Well

name: Drainage System
search:
Drainage System This is an improvement of the Open Canalization system, but not by a high margin, except for the city itself. Depending on the wealth of the city, the wastewater system will be underground, like the various 'cloaca' running under Rome, or will be partially in the open air. Still, they are built to be of sufficient diameter to tackle everything thrown into it (including an occasional corpse). The waste will then be discharged further down a river or better into the sea.

EVENT: There is a very low chance that an adjacent Fish resource is removed from the resulting pollution.

Lead coating parts of the tunnels will make it more robust and in need of less maintenance. 145 ID_STRUC_DRAINAGE_SYSTEM $ID_STRUC_DRAINAGE_SYSTEM 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health     Lead     Bonus_Infra 1 1 0 Health Bonus: 13%

name: Dung Cart
search: Dung Cart As their name implies, the ubiquitous Dung Carts (or dung boats) were a means to remove the waste produced by the citizen of a city. It goes without saying that the workers were not highly regarded by their fellows, even if paradoxically they were providing them with a greatly appreciated service. Some of their workers would sometimes improve their income by selling their load to farmers, as fertilizer, although not all authorities accepted this, as it was reckoned to be a factor in spreading diseases. 146 ID_STRUC_DUNG_CART $ID_STRUC_DUNG_CART 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health         Manpower_Output Income_Output  1 1 0 Health Bonus: 5%

modifiers_note_html:
Transverse output: 6 money from Partial Swamp Draining
Health Bonus: 2%
search:
Leech Gatherer Leeches were very often used by the medieval doctor, to purge the body of its foul humors. Gathering leeches was thus a lucrative job, which required not much expertise, a good technique was wadding through still water and having the little slugs attach to your legs. Ceramic pots will be very useful to keep them alive longer.

The presence of a partially drained swamp will be ideal for the activity. Salt will be useful to detach the leeches faster, thus increasing productivity. 147 ID_STRUC_LEECH_GATHERER $ID_STRUC_LEECH_GATHERER 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health  Ceramics   Salt    Income_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Transverse output: 6 money from Swamp Draining1 Health Bonus: 2%

Red Smith Red Smiths or coppersmiths work copper into precision tools, medical instruments, and merchant scales. As a result their efforts are appreciated by many other professions.

name: Master Brazier
search:
Master Brazier Master Braziers specialize in the construction of sophisticated and ornate heating apparatus for the mansions of the wealthier nobles. Mastering both the work of different metals and how hot air flows through conduits, their work is often considered an art by itself.

Weigh carefully the choice of not building this structure, as this is a rare proposal. A caveat though: This master will make use of Brass, contrary to the more common Red smith who uses Copper... 152 ID_STRUC_MASTER_BRAZIER $ID_STRUC_MASTER_BRAZIER 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Brass   Copper    Income_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Commerce Bonus: 10% Red Smith

name: Forest Glassworks
search: Forest Glassworks Workshops producing Glass started to develop nearby or even within Germany's expansive forests, as a practical means to get the large amount of Wood needed for the heating of silica (sand mostly) into glass and glassware. This production practice, known as waldglas, spread over time to many parts of Europe. 153 ID_STRUC_FOREST_GLASSWORKS $ID_STRUC_FOREST_GLASSWORKS 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Wood Glassware      Infra_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output  1 1 0

name: Forest Assarting

name: Silver Mine
search: Silver Mine This Silver Mine will make the owner rich, provided he can find workers for the extraction and purification of the precious metal. name: Mentoring Hall
modifiers_note_html:
Replaces: Builder Hall
Structure Costreduction: 15%
Jobprod1 Bonus: 2
Freemengrow Bonus: 15%
search:
Mentoring Hall As cities grow, craftsmen tend to congregate into communities of experts where the most informed will mentor their assistants into journeymen. Over time these societies will become corporations with significant powers, able to contest the arbitrary decisions of the nobility. They will then further evolve into the powerful guilds of the late medieval period.
EVENT: Occasionally, craftsmen will play a key role in the construction of a magnificent, prestigious building. 157 ID_STRUC_MENTORING_HALL $ID_STRUC_MENTORING_HALL 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Tiles & Brick   Tiles & Brick Marble   Infra_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Structure Costreduction: 15% Jobprod1 Bonus: 2 Freemengrow Bonus: 15% Builder Hall

name: Water Mill
search: Water Mill Installed along rivers, Water Mills will make use of the formidable power of a water flow to grind Grains, cut Wood, or keep dry mine shafts. Engineering techniques developed over time and many centuries, from the simple Noria to complex paddle wheels made of wood and steel. 158 ID_STRUC_WATER_MILL $ID_STRUC_WATER_MILL 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure         Food_Output Infra_Output Income_Output Steward_Output  1 1 0 Flavor: 0 Rustic Water Mill

name: Cobblestone road paver
search: Cobblestone road paver The road network in medieval times was often tracks and dirt roads, thus limiting how easy people (civilians and soldiers) could travel. With enough effort and the decision of a powerful noble or merchant corporation, some roads made of stones were made, provided you can muster enough workers for the immense work required. 159 ID_STRUC_COBBLESTONE_PAVER $ID_STRUC_COBBLESTONE_PAVER 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Stone       Infra_Output  1 1 0 Trade Acumen Bonus: 2 Infra Bonus: 10%

name: Physician House
bonus_summary: Money 5 | from: Drugs & Herbs, Tar, Salt
search:
Physician House This physician is more versed than a practitioner in medical science. He might have attended a medical school and probably can read Latin and Greek, thus allowing him to read many treatises. If a Muslim, they might have been translated into Arabic. His skills partly come from previous curing attempts on the less fortunate, whose fate was seen to be of no importance. With enough experience, he can even be employed by kings.

Dissection was looked down upon but not completely forbidden by the Church, but considered foul by Islam. Still, the presence of a Cemetery in the same region will be largely profitable to his skill (EVENT).

The Physician can make a lucrative job of his knowledge should Herbs, Salt or Naphtha be available.
An Hostel of God or a Physician House is required to build a Leprosarium. 160 ID_STRUC_PHYSICIAN_HOUSE $ID_STRUC_PHYSICIAN_HOUSE 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health     Drugs & Herbs Tar Salt   Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Health Bonus: 10% Charhealth Bonus: 6% Loyalty Bonus: 5 Apothecary Medical Practitioner

modifiers_note_html:
Health Bonus: 8%
Transverse output: 6 infra from Well
Noblegrow Bonus: 20%
search: Water Channel This is a sophisticated method of bringing water from a source into some parts of the city (either for the nobility or for some craftsmen, so it rarely benefits more than a small fraction of the overall population). Close to a running water system, it benefits from the presence of a Well or a Water Mill (or the successors to these buildings). 161 ID_STRUC_WATER_CHANNEL $ID_STRUC_WATER_CHANNEL 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health         Steward_Output  1 1 0 Health Bonus: 8% Transverse output: 6 infra from Well Noblegrow Bonus: 20%

name: Cesspool
modifiers_note_html:
Health Bonus: 7%
Transverse output: 6 money from Dung Cart
search:
Cesspool Cesspools were often communal and were not necessarily connected to any drainage system. They could be filled by dedicated workers using 'Dung Carts' or by individuals belonging to the same neighborhood bringing their own waste.
Comparatively, Cesspools are not very costly for the benefits provided. 162 ID_STRUC_CESSPOOL $ID_STRUC_CESSPOOL 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health           1 1 0 Health Bonus: 7% Transverse output: 6 money from Dung Cart

name: Hostel of God
search:
Hostel of God Sitting in between the religious and secular worlds, the Hostel of God is the hospital's ancestor. Most of the staff are made of religious personnel and mainly women, and as such only regions with significant Piety will host one. This is a huge structure that can handle a large number of patients, and is fit to combat efficiently widespread disease: it is unique in providing a removal chance every turn of a disease event afflicting the region.

Frankincense, a fragrant plant of religious symbolism, will also be quite useful to cover the foul smells of certain diseases.
An Hostel of God or a Physician House is required to build a Leprosarium. 163 ID_STRUC_HOSTEL_OF_GOD $ID_STRUC_HOSTEL_OF_GOD 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health  Glassware   Frankincense    Manpower_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 0 0 Health Bonus: 14% Piety Bonus: 10% Hitsrecovery: 10 Flavor: 0

name: Leprosarium
search:
Leprosarium Leprosarium, leper colonies or leper hospitals all deal with people afflicted by leprosy, a bacterial infection. Contrary to a widespread belief, lepers were not rejected and then confined systematically like plague victims were, as people of the time already knew that leprosy was not very contagious. It was more a matter of practicality, by grouping together people with the same needs. Structures under religious authorities could sometimes ask the leper to make some wows, as a way of cleaning both the soul and body.
A Physician House or Hostel of God is required to build a Leprosarium. 164 ID_STRUC_LEPROSARIUM $ID_STRUC_LEPROSARIUM 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health  Cotton   Frankincense Spices Sulfur  Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 0 0 Health Bonus: 7% Flavor: 0

name: Muslim Hospital
search:
Muslim Hospital Contrary to its Christian counterpart (Hostel of God), this hospital is a direct upgrade of the Modest Bimaristan, and remains effective at fighting off many epidemics and plagues (It lowers their appearance chances and then actively removes them).

Muslim hospitals, sometimes named Bimaristans, appeared as early as the VIIIth century. Drawing initially from the knowledge of the ancient Greeks and in contact with the Byzantines, they soon excelled in treating their patients. The moral obligation to care for any sick person, according to Islam, led to the creation of dozens of hospitals, through donations and charities and mostly with secular (non-religious) staff, this is the reason why this building is twice as cheap as a Hostel of God. 165 ID_STRUC_MUS_EARLY_HOSPITAL $ID_STRUC_MUS_EARLY_HOSPITAL 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health  Cotton   Drugs & Herbs    Manpower_Output Bonus_Manpower Bonus_Piety 0 1 0 Health Bonus: 16% Loyalty Bonus: 7 Hitsrecovery: 10 Flavor: 0

name: Deep Well
search: Deep Well A Deep Well never run dry, as it taps into an underground aquifer. This is a great addition to any region, as the water is very pure and will be a large plus in case of siege. 166 ID_STRUC_DEEP_WELL $ID_STRUC_DEEP_WELL 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health           1 1 0 Health Bonus: 5% Extra Supply: 10 Well

name: Embankment Dam
search:
Embankment Dam Gaining land from the sea or marshy regions is (perhaps surprisingly given the complexity of the task) something that has been performed since antiquity. This structure can only be built on the coast, but will free up 2 slots for more buildings. It requires significant maintenance though and will be made of earth walls reinforced with sturdy wood and metal.

If it ever happens it is destroyed because of a hostile raid, one population will be immediately lost from the flood. 167 ID_STRUC_EMBANKMENT_DAM $ID_STRUC_EMBANKMENT_DAM 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health  Wood   Iron Ore    Metal_Output Bonus_Infra 1 1 0

name: Local Market
bonus_summary: Money 3 | from: Wine, Wool, Preserved Food, Flax
search: Local Market A staple of a good economy, the Local Market will need an ample supply of Pottery, used by the lower classes in their daily life (and by many Muslims, who were forbidden to use metal plates). It can generate a steady income if more wares are present. Its evolution, the Provincial Market, focuses more on manufactured products, so an upgrade here might not be beneficial if you don't have many workshops in the region or around it. 174 ID_STRUC_LOCAL_MARKET $ID_STRUC_LOCAL_MARKET 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Pottery   Wine Wool Preserved Food Flax Income_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 1

name: Potters Workshop
modifiers_note_html: Transverse output: 4 money from Clay Pit
search:
Potters Workshop Still very much used in the Middle Ages by the poor in their daily life and as universal containers, Pottery is a necessary trade good for the Local Market and will provide bonus income for several buildings.

In the Middle East, Spain and North Africa, pottery was developed into a fine art, and was highly prized. Byzantine potters, poised between the Christian world of Europe and the Islamic world, were able to see and copy styles and techniques from both traditions. 177 ID_STRUC_POTTERS_WORKSHOP $ID_STRUC_POTTERS_WORKSHOP 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce   Pottery      Income_Output  1 1 1 Transverse output: 4 money from Clay Pit

search: Butchery Livestock can be turned into consumable meat through the Butchery, which will lead to a fairly decent income. Sometimes the butcher will prepare special meals for the noble, if Salt and Wine is available. With enough practice, he can even be promoted to be the personal cook of a lord. 182 ID_STRUC_BUTCHERY $ID_STRUC_BUTCHERY 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Livestock   Wine Salt   Food_Output Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Steward 1 1 1

name: Soap Workshop
search:
Soap Workshop This workshop will provide a gold bonus to the Hamman building and has a decent Money income on its own. Soap was often made with fat and ash. Although the presence of this building in a region won't translate in a direct Health bonus, it will be factored in reducing plague.

Soap usage declined in Europe with the fall of the Roman empire, and is one of the reason of the numerous plagues and illness that afflicted medieval Europe. It was a thriving industry in Al-Andalus and in some parts of the Muslim world though. 183 ID_STRUC_MUS_SOAP_WORKSHOP $ID_STRUC_MUS_SOAP_WORKSHOP 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce     Honey & Wax Spices   Income_Output Bonus_Income 0 1 0 Flavor: 0

Traders Borough The Traders Borough will unlock extra Commerce buildings in the region, which will provide a significant increase in Money income.

Once enough craft and commercial ventures are set up, traders will naturally congregate in a small community. Gambling Ring Gambling was a common leisure activity for all social classes in medieval society. Dice were often favored, but cards and chess might be preferred by the more educated classes like nobles or monks.

Maisir The activity of gambling in the Islamic faith is strongly forbidden, but a populace recently or partially converted will still indulge into this activity (including animal-baiting) but it will be reprimanded by the clergy.

name: Brine Well
search:
Brine Well Event Building.

Draining the swamps revealed a large concentration of brine in deep pockets of water. This will allow Salt production at regular intervals, with the collected brine stored in Pottery containers. 202 ID_STRUC_EVT_BRINE_WELL $ID_STRUC_EVT_BRINE_WELL 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Pottery Salt  Tools    Income_Output Bonus_Income 0 0 0

name: Itinerant Caravan
search: Itinerant Caravan The Itinerant Caravan will more often appear if there is at least one of his bonus goods available (EVENT), but can also be used to transport goods and materials between villages. Contrary to the Traveling Merchant, the Itinerant Caravan will never enter a foreign nation, unless it is tribal or nomadic. It also cannot create a Trade Route. 203 ID_STRUC_TN_ITINERANT_CARAVAN $ID_STRUC_TN_ITINERANT_CARAVAN 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Cloth   Pottery Horses Livestock  Infra_Output Income_Output Bonus_Income 0 0 1 Trade Acumen Bonus: 3

name: Pronoia
search:
Pronoia This piece of land is still owned by the Empire, but has been delegated to a noble (or war veteran). It can only be removed through the use of the regional decision 'Remove Civilized'. EVENT: Each turn there is a low chance that a single domain without any Pronoia gets one (up to 10 in your realm).

Pronoia are non-transferable rights to receive income (often from a plot of land) given by the Byzantine Emperor to a noble or war veteran, as a reward or payment. Initially only a means to enrich the beneficiary, it evolved into something closer to the feudal system of Western Europe, as the people living within the Pronoia (the paroikoi) could be levied into a 'Pronoiar'. There was no formal obligation to assist the Emperor, except the looming possibility of having your Pronoia confiscated if he was defeated! 204 ID_STRUC_BYZ_PRONOIA $ID_STRUC_BYZ_PRONOIA 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce         Manpower_Output Income_Output  0 0 0 Adminburden Bonus: 5% Safemanpower Penalty: -25 Lobbyhidden: 0 Loyalty Bonus: 10

name: Venetian Commercial Enclave
search:
Venetian Commercial Enclave Fondachi (singular Fondacho) are merchant outposts established in foreign lands, this one belongs to Venice.

They procure amenities for the citizens of the maritime republic in charge of the enclave, and possess a large number of warehouses. Although they compete fiercely with local merchants, they can end up opening new commercial opportunities for the clever ruler (EVENT).

This structure can't be disbanded by the region owner, as strictly speaking, it does not belong to him. 210 ID_STRUC_IMR_ENCLAVE_VEN $ID_STRUC_IMR_ENCLAVE_VEN 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce         Income_Output Steward_Output  0 0 0 Gs Commerce Bonus: 3% Lobbyhidden: 0

name: Usurer

modifiers_note_html:
Replaces: Money Lender
Loyalty Penalty: 6
Simple Taxation Bonus: 20%
search:
Usurer The Usurer is a rare evolution of the Money Lender that can happen in very wealthy region when Piety is low. He makes a lot of money with his dubious lending practices that will result in many people being strangled by debts. This will greatly displease the clergy, but the truth is that the vast amount of riches he accumulates has been known to help a lord or even a king fund his kingdom or a crusade.
This building is not provided to nations of Muslim faith, as outright usury was deemed to be sinful. 219 ID_STRUC_EVT_USURER $ID_STRUC_EVT_USURER 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce     Silver Gold Precious Stones Luxury Income_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 0 0 0 Loyalty Penalty: 6 Simple Taxation Bonus: 20% Money Lender

name: Anchorage
search: Anchorage An Anchorage will prevent the loss of effectiveness for troops when they land or embark, but won't reduce the boarding or disembarking cost. However, unlike more developed ports, they are unable to produce any ships. They provide a trickle of income making them a bit of help to local commerce. 220 ID_STRUC_ANCHORAGE $ID_STRUC_ANCHORAGE 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce         Income_Output  1 1 1 Fowlift Adjsea: 1

name: Small Harbor
search:
Small Harbor A Small Harbor will help troops board or disembark faster and without effectiveness loss. It can't produce warships though, you'll need the Tier II port facilities for that. Nonetheless, it provides some income and generates the Transport Ship goods which are used at the national level to determine the quality of available troop transportation assets.

In feudal times, many nations did not have a warfleet and relied on hiring ships from merchant republics or a few maritime nations (being allied with them might help provide you with this benefit). It took a dedicated effort from the ruler to build and maintain war galleys at all times. 221 ID_STRUC_SMALL_HARBOR $ID_STRUC_SMALL_HARBOR 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Wood Transport Ships  Tar Hemp Sails  Income_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 1 Commerce Bonus: 10% Trade Acumen Bonus: 2 Hidden: 1 Fowlift Adjsea: 1 Shipbuilding: 1 Anchorage

Sailmaker Canvas sail cloths are of the utmost importance for ships, even galleys. Sails produced in this workshop will be used in vast numbers by shipyards or their Maritime Republic equivalent, maritime arsenals. A Rope-Maker in close proximity will be a certain plus to the Sailmaker.

name: Spinning Mill - Cotton
search:
Spinning Mill - Cotton Several looms, vertical or horizontal are being serviced by women in this building. The most advanced ones might even have some pedals, a technical improvement from distant China. This workshop specializes in cotton clothing and canvas, both in large demand by villages and towns. Cotton progressively made its way to Europe but was produced initially only in Muslim nations.

Only one spinning mill among three possibilities can be present in a given region. 226 ID_STRUC_SPINNING_MILL_COTTON $ID_STRUC_SPINNING_MILL_COTTON 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Cotton Cloth  Dyes    Infra_Output Income_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Recruit Mbp Reduction: 10% (params: $FAMCAVALRY, $FAMHVYCAVALRY, $FAMINFANTRY, $FAMHVYINFANTRY)

The most advanced ones might even have some pedals, a technical improvement from distant China. This workshop specializes in wool clothing and canvas, both in large demand by villages and towns.

Only one spinning mill among three possibilities can be present in a given region. 227 ID_STRUC_SPINNING_MILL_WOOL $ID_STRUC_SPINNING_MILL_WOOL 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Wool Cloth  Dyes    Infra_Output Income_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Recruit Mbp Reduction: 10% (params: $FAMCAVALRY, $FAMHVYCAVALRY, $FAMINFANTRY, $FAMHVYINFANTRY)

Spinning Mill - Flax Several looms, vertical or horizontal are being serviced by women in this building. The most advanced ones might even have some pedals, a technical improvement from distant China. This workshop specializes in flax (leading to linen) clothing and canvas, both in large demand by villages and towns. Flax is a common plant in Europe, but is difficult to dye.

Only one spinning mill among three possibilities can be present in a given region. 228 ID_STRUC_SPINNING_MILL_FLAX $ID_STRUC_SPINNING_MILL_FLAX 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Flax Cloth  Cotton Wool   Infra_Output Income_Output Bonus_Infra 1 1 0 Recruit Mbp Reduction: 10% (params: $FAMCAVALRY, $FAMHVYCAVALRY, $FAMINFANTRY, $FAMHVYINFANTRY)

bonus_summary: Money 3, Piety 2, Stewardship 2 | from: Silk, Indigo, Cloth
search:
Prestige Clothier This craftsman will produce quality clothes, fit for the nobility.

Although medieval garments were often colored, it was mostly true for people with a certain level of income, as cotton was not available for the common people, and flax a common resource did not dye well. 229 ID_STRUC_PRESTIGE_CLOTHIER $ID_STRUC_PRESTIGE_CLOTHIER 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Dyes   Silk Indigo Cloth  Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 1 0

name: Master Clothier
bonus_summary: Money 8, Piety 5, Stewardship 5 | from: Silk, Indigo, Cloth
search:
Master Clothier The clothes don't make the man. Except if your clothes come from a Master Clothier, at the peak of his skills.

This workshop is an evolution of the Prestige Clothier and is a rare opportunity to pick as your next choice.

If both bonuses are collected by the Master Clothier (Indigo and Silk), then the building will most of the time generate a bonus of 5 Legacy Points per turn (given every 5 turns), although very rarely it will generate much more (EVENT). 230 ID_STRUC_MASTER_CLOTHIER $ID_STRUC_MASTER_CLOTHIER 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Dyes   Silk Indigo Cloth  Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 1 0  Prestige Clothier

name: Stained Glass Maker
search: Stained Glass Maker This glassmaker specializes in stained glass, used in many religious buildings. The making of such delicate glass is difficult and requires the local availability of raw resources, such as glassware. Lead, a very toxic metal, is also needed to stabilize the paintings, while the ready availability of pigments ensures that the work is of great quality. Glassware is very fragile and must be locally sourced, not imported. 231 ID_STRUC_STAINED_GLASS_MAKER $ID_STRUC_STAINED_GLASS_MAKER 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce Glassware Lead   Dyes    Infra_Output Income_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety 1 1 0 Piety Bonus: 15% Health Penalty: 4%

Trade Acumen Bonus: 6

search: Fair Fairs were the merchant hubs of the Medieval ages, although the comparison to a stock exchange would be appropriate too, as transactions were numerous and of importance, both in volume and worth. They could reach a very large size and be semi-permanent, becoming the 'place to be' for any serious merchant. 232 ID_STRUC_FAIR $ID_STRUC_FAIR 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Glassware   Wine Pottery Preserved Food Cloth Income_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Trade Acumen Bonus: 6 Commerce Bonus: 10% Local Market

name: Mordant Storage
bonus_summary: Infra 2, Money 2 | from: Glassware, Tiles & Brick, Lead
search:
Mordant Storage Tanners, dyers, parchmenters, and many other craftsmen have a use for mordant products for their work. Mordants are products used to stabilize or clean other materials (often hides from animals). They can be chemical in nature like lye or can come from plants, or even urine, which is still collected in the middle age as it was by the Roman.

Storage requires cheap and stable containers, like Pottery and this drudge work of collection and handling is often done by the poorest of the poorest. Still, the service they provide is invaluable! 233 ID_STRUC_MORDANT_STORAGE $ID_STRUC_MORDANT_STORAGE 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Pottery   Glassware Tiles & Brick Lead  Income_Output Bonus_Infra Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Struc Output Transverse: 12 (params: $TRANSVERSE_OUTPUT_POP1, $POP_JOB_INFRA, $POP_JOB_COMMERCE, 150, 150)

name: Ceramics Workshop
search:
Ceramics Workshop Ceramic pottery, an art perfected by the Chinese was also developed greatly by Byzantine and Muslim potters. At its base, it is often clay pottery which is then glazed to make it impermeable and then ready for painting.
Porcelain will use instead of clay another material named silicate.
Techniques for the making and painting of ceramic evolved exquisitely over the age, to the point that many pieces are works of art fit for a noble or a king! 234 ID_STRUC_CERAMICS_WORKSHOP $ID_STRUC_CERAMICS_WORKSHOP 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce   Ceramics  Dyes    Income_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 1 0

name: Perfumery
search: Perfumery The Muslim world used perfumes since the IXth century (thanks to one of the greatest savant of that era Al-Kindi), although the delimitation between essential oil, pharmaceutic, and 'true' perfume was at this time rather blurry, with products being used for several usages. 235 ID_STRUC_MUS_PERFUMERY $ID_STRUC_MUS_PERFUMERY 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce   Luxury  Spices Amber Fruits  Income_Output Bonus_Income 0 1 0 Health Bonus: 6%

name: Black Market

search: Black Market The Gambling Ring attracts all manner of shady individuals with dubious connections; and so on rare occasions this will create a Black Market in the city. The Black Market will sometimes procure rare merchandise for the wealthy, although one would be at loss to know beforehand exactly what they will find and when. Weigh carefully the choice of not building this structure, as this is a rare proposal. Most of the time, the effect of the Black Market will be beneficial, although this is unpredictable. 236 ID_STRUC_BLACK_MARKET $ID_STRUC_BLACK_MARKET 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce   Luxury  Gold Precious Stones   Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Attribute: 1 (params: $ATTR_BLACKMARKET) Safemoney Penalty: 500

name: Troubadours
bonus_summary: Money 4 | from: Luxury, Silver, Gold, Precious Stones
search:
Troubadours Troubadours are medieval performers with many skills. They can entertain their audience with fantastic stories, poems, songs, and even deliver satires. There is no strict equivalent to them in the Muslim world, but predating troubadours, a long-standing tradition of recounted odes, the qasida, was common in such nations.

EVENT: Troubadours are known to travel. One day they are there, the other they are gone! (They can rarely go into a foreign nation's territory, but never if it's a tier I Realm). 241 ID_STRUC_TROUBADOURS $ID_STRUC_TROUBADOURS 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce     Luxury Silver Gold Precious Stones Food_Output Infra_Output Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 12 Struc Output Transverse: 6 (params: $TRANSVERSE_OUTPUT_POP1, $POP_JOB_AGRI, $POP_JOB_COMMERCE, 100) Revolt Increase: 20% Acrobats

name: Caravansery
bonus_summary: Money 3 | from: Preserved Food, Spices, Livestock
search: Caravansery A large commercial relay in the Muslim world where merchants can rest their animals and themselves or store their merchandise temporarily. They are mostly built along major roads, and many harbored permanent guards and extra weapons, in case of bandits and marauders. You need at least Roads (and not Tracks) for this building. 242 ID_STRUC_MUS_CARAVANSERY $ID_STRUC_MUS_CARAVANSERY 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Luxury   Preserved Food Spices Livestock  MBPStockpile Income_Output Bonus_Income 0 1 0 Trade Range Bonus: 1 Trade Acumen Bonus: 4

name: Bank
bonus_summary: Money 4 | from: Gold, Precious Stones, Silver
search:
Bank Banking activities would range from Usurers (who were despised and promised Hell by the Church and were ranked at the same level as prostitutes), to the more accepted activity of Money Changer (if they abide by a certain morality in their conduct) to the prestigious merchant banker.
The latter dealt in part with international commerce and in part with loaning money to monarchs and other established power groups (including the Church).
Banks are quite important if you want to store your precious gold safely, as they allow you to increase the upper limit before which 25% of your Money is lost per turn. 244 ID_STRUC_BANK $ID_STRUC_BANK 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Silver   Gold Precious Stones Silver  Income_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Safemoney Bonus: 500 Commerce Bonus: 20%

name: Trade Road
modifiers_note_html:
Replaces: ROAD/HRE ROAD
Commerce Bonus: 12%
Trade Range Bonus: 1
Trade Acumen Bonus: 4
search:
Trade Road The Trade Road represents a portion of a Trade Route, which can possibly run for thousands of leagues, or be more humble and only serve two nearby cities. It can't be built and will only appear after some time if you have Itinerant Merchants or caravans (from a Caravansery) running in the vicinity.
It allows for the creation of Emporium and Exotic Merchants, both being able to provide rare trade goods never seen before in the region.

A Trade Road does not replace any kind of road and is not easing movement. It provides an extra free bonus to commerce. Should the region becomes pillaged, it is automatically destroyed though. 245 ID_STRUC_TRADE_ROAD $ID_STRUC_TRADE_ROAD 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce         Income_Output  1 1 0 Commerce Bonus: 12% Trade Range Bonus: 1 Trade Acumen Bonus: 4

name: Paved Road
modifiers_note_html:
Replaces: Regional Roads
Commerce Bonus: 16%
Trade Acumen Bonus: 4
search:
Paved Road The Paved Road is the ultimate land transportation means of the time, allowing merchants and soldiers to travel as fast as possible. It requires significant efforts, and in Medieval Times was not something often considered by monarchs and powerful nobles.
Immigration is made easier, and these foreigners can also be drafted indiscriminately. 246 ID_STRUC_PAVED_ROAD $ID_STRUC_PAVED_ROAD 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce         Manpower_Output Income_Output  1 1 0 Commerce Bonus: 16% Trade Acumen Bonus: 4 Regional Roads

name: Money Changer
search:
Money Changer In between the much-despised Usurer (or Money Lender) and the prestigious merchant banker, the Money Changer will flourish by converting the money of foreign merchants into the local currency.

With some 'arrangements', he could also provide a bonus (in effect, an interest rate) if you deposited some Money, or allow a trader to deposit his wealth in a place and still miraculously use it in another place! In doing these activities, he exposed himself to the wrath of the religious authorities though, always on the lookout for people committing the sin of greed. 247 ID_STRUC_MONEY_CHANGER $ID_STRUC_MONEY_CHANGER 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce     Gold Silver   Income_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Deficitrecovery Bonus: 3%

Inn A rest for the weary, Inns also play an important role as a place where news can be exchanged and deals struck.
As alcohol was sometimes banned,  this building will be less common in Muslim nations. 248 ID_STRUC_INN $ID_STRUC_INN 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Wine   Livestock Preserved Food Fishes  Food_Output Income_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Income 1 0 0 Transverse output: 8 money from Brewery Trade Acumen Bonus: 2 Ale House

name: Merchant Confraternity
search: Merchant Confraternity With the increased demands on many goods and the means to carry them fast and at great distances through commercial fleets, a new social class appears progressively, wielding power through its monetary might. To further their goals, they soon congregate into confraternities, which are precursors to the merchant guilds of the XIVth century. 251 ID_STRUC_MERCHANT_CONFRATERNITY $ID_STRUC_MERCHANT_CONFRATERNITY 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce         Income_Output Steward_Output  1 1 0 Commerce Bonus: 20% Missing Tg Bonus: 15%

Hostelry Hostels will benefit greatly from a nearby Fair, as they provide the means for the merchants to stay and operate much more easily for weeks and weeks.
A good Hostelry is one well supplied by local goods. Some practiced some form of charity and would accommodate for negligible fees any passing pilgrims. 252 ID_STRUC_HOSTELRY $ID_STRUC_HOSTELRY 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce     Fruits Legumes Fishes Livestock Income_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Health Bonus: 5% Transverse output: 8 money from Fair

name: Trade Emporium
search:
Trade Emporium This Emporium is a large area of warehouses where a notable merchant stores his imports of trade goods. Many merchandises can be found there, but the place also lends itself to the exchange of information, news, and even plots. Both the local authorities and the clergy while not overtly hostile to this mercantile activity will try to limit its corrupting (or so they say) influence over the population.

Will allow the creation of a single 'Emporium Depot' which will generate missing Trade Goods.
Will diversify what is offered by a Black Market, if one is present. 254 ID_STRUC_EMPORIUM_MAIN $ID_STRUC_EMPORIUM_MAIN 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce     Transport Ships    Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Missing Tg Bonus: 20%

search: Shipyard Shipyards are specialized structures where a lot of ships can be produced in an efficient way. Transport ships, galleys, galleass, roundships and many more types will benefit from the facility provided here. If you consider serious sea operations or want to contest maritime supremacy with another nation, they are a must-have. 275 ID_STRUC_SHIPYARD $ID_STRUC_SHIPYARD 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Sails Transport Ships  Tar Hemp   MBPStockpile MBP_Output Bonus_MBP 1 1 0 Recruit Mbp Reduction: 20% (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP) Recruit Money Reduction: 15% (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP) Creation Xp Bonus: 10 (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP)

name: Grande Arsenale Marittimo
modifiers_note_html:
Replaces: Shipyard
Recruit Mbp Reduction: 30% (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP)
Recruit Money Reduction: 20% (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP)
Creation Xp Bonus: 20 (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP)
search:
Grande Arsenale Marittimo This Arsenal is exclusive to nations with a maritime tradition and will make producing warships even easier than with the more common shipyard. The quality of the ship will also be better, with more advanced techniques in hull making.

In the classical era, ships were built with a very sturdy hull, where planks were held together in a very strong fashion, thanks to tenons (pieces of wood) inserted into mortises (holes in the planks shaping the hull). In Medieval Times, the process of hull making was often done in a kind of reverse fashion, with much fewer tenons and mortises, while planks were put against an internal frame (the ship 'skeleton'). The construction required less expertise, was slightly more fragile, and asked for more caulking to protect the hull against fouling. 276 ID_STRUC_IMR_ARSENAL $ID_STRUC_IMR_ARSENAL 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Sails Transport Ships  Tar Hemp   MBPStockpile MBP_Output Bonus_MBP 0 0 0 Recruit Mbp Reduction: 30% (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP) Recruit Money Reduction: 20% (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP) Creation Xp Bonus: 20 (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP) Shipyard

modifiers_note_html:
Replaces: Shipyard
Recruit Mbp Reduction: 40% (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP)
Recruit Money Reduction: 25% (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP)
Creation Xp Bonus: 25 (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP)
search:
Arsenale di Venezia This arsenal will produce ships at a reduced cost and with an increase in their quality. It will also allow the production of larger ships, exclusive to Italian Maritime Republics, like the powerful galleass.

The largest quasi-industrial site in the world at its peak, the Venetian Arsenal could produce on average one ship per day, thanks to many rationalized processes close to a production chain, with the use of standardized equipment, and the control of all raw resources, including dedicated forests. 277 ID_STRUC_VEN_ARSENAL $ID_STRUC_VEN_ARSENAL 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Sails Transport Ships  Tar Hemp   MBPStockpile MBP_Output Bonus_MBP 0 0 0 Recruit Mbp Reduction: 40% (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP) Recruit Money Reduction: 25% (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP) Creation Xp Bonus: 25 (params: $FAMLTWARSHIP, $FAMWARSHIP, $FAMHVYWARSHIP) Shipyard

Medium Harbor The first Harbor available to nations without a maritime tradition that can produce warships, the Medium Harbor provides a decent income, extends local trading range and generates the Transport Ship goods.

This is a special Trade Good which will determine the quality and speed of your troop transports.
As with other harbors, it has a large Siege Resist value, making any siege lasts a very long time if not under blockade by enemy ships. 278 ID_STRUC_MEDIUM_HARBOR $ID_STRUC_MEDIUM_HARBOR 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Wood Transport Ships  Tar Hemp Sails  Income_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Commerce Bonus: 16% Trade Range Bonus: 1 Hidden: 1 Fowlift Adjsea: 1 Boarding Cost: 2 Shipbuilding: 3 Small Harbor Protected Harbor
