As with other harbors, it generates the Transport Ship goods.

This is a special Trade Good which will determine the quality and speed of your troop transports.
It also has a large Siege Resist value, making any siege lasts a very long time if not under blockade by enemy ships. 281 ID_STRUC_PROT_LARGE_HARBOR $ID_STRUC_PROT_LARGE_HARBOR 2 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Wood Transport Ships  Tar Hemp Sails  Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_MBP Bonus_Income 0 0 0 Commerce Bonus: 25% Trade Range Bonus: 3 Hidden: 1 Fowlift Adjsea: 1 Boarding Cost: 3 Shipbuilding: 4 Medium Harbor Medium Protected Harbor

name: Peasants Roundup
search:
Peasants Roundup Peasants will be promptly drafted in the region, despite their unwillingness to join the army. This enlistment is easy to setup and will provide a significant number of recruits.

EVENT: If you have none in your hand, this structure can rarely provide an extra Regional Decision 'Draft Levies,' able to furnish you with many peasants and marauders (depending on the population of the targeted region). 283 ID_STRUC_PEASANTS_ROUNDUP $ID_STRUC_PEASANTS_ROUNDUP 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Fishes Legumes   Manpower_Output Bonus_Manpower 1 1 1 Loyalty Penalty: 5

name: Levy Camp
search:
Levy Camp All Medium Infantry Levy units can be recruited in any region, but you'll need Levy Camps within your nation to remove the 'Hastily Levied' penalty they get. Up to three Levy Camps can be built in a single region, each providing a modest discount on the recruitment cost and a small amount of Equipment production.

A Levy Camp makes use of the Flax trade good, as light to medium armor such as gambesons and brigantines make use of it. 284 ID_STRUC_LEVY_CAMP $ID_STRUC_LEVY_CAMP 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Flax       MBP_Output Manpower_Output  1 1 1 Recruit Mpw Reduction: 15% (params: $FAMLTINFANTRY, $FAMSKIRMISHER, $FAMINFANTRY) Loyalty Penalty: 2 Attribute: 2 (params: $ATTR_MEDLEVY_IMP)

name: Local Watch
search:
Local Watch The Local Watch will patrol villages, and can fend off bandits and low intensity raids. It can be further upgraded to the town then the city watch.

It makes use of the Wool trade good, as light armors such as gambeson and brigantines make use of it. 285 ID_STRUC_LOCAL_WATCH $ID_STRUC_LOCAL_WATCH 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Wool       Manpower_Output  1 1 1 Loyalty Bonus: 5 Town Watch

name: Stable
search: Stable The Stable will remove the penalty Levy Cavalry gets when recruited and will provide an additional experience bonus to SAU Medium Cavalry. There is no obligation to set up military stables if you favor quantity over quality though, as all Levy units can be recruited without any specific buildings. 286 ID_STRUC_STABLE $ID_STRUC_STABLE 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Horses   Legumes Grain   MBP_Output Bonus_Infra 1 0 0 Attribute: 3 (params: $ATTR_MEDCAV_IMP)

name: Fine Horse Breeder
search:
Fine Horse Breeder This Horse Breeder building is a superior version of the more common Stable. It not only represents Arabian horse breeders (Bedouin tribes) but also any horse breeder with a long tradition of quality horses, such as found in nomadic nations.  It will remove the penalty Levy cavalry gets when recruited and provide an extra bonus to SAU Medium Cavalry.

It will produce a trickle of Legacy points each turn, and some Stewardship points if the horses can be decorated with beautiful garments.

Fast, smart, resistant, Arabian horses are the pride of the Bedouin tribes (Arabian Peninsula), to the point that their pedigree is duly recorded over centuries, and that their favorite animals are kept at night in their own tent, if need be. 287 ID_STRUC_SPE_FINE_HORSE_BREEDER $ID_STRUC_SPE_FINE_HORSE_BREEDER 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Horses   Dyes Cloth   MBP_Output Bonus_Steward 0 1 0 Attribute: 3 (params: $ATTR_MEDCAV_IMP)

name: Leather Workshop
search: Leather Workshop The Leather Workshop will produce quilted or padded armors with the outer layer made of thick Leather. It is an essential workshop for the equipment of soldiers. 288 ID_STRUC_LEATHER_WORKSHOP $ID_STRUC_LEATHER_WORKSHOP 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Leather   Wool Flax Cotton  MBP_Output Bonus_MBP 1 1 1

name: Minor Stockpile
search: Minor Stockpile A Minor Stockpile of provisions and equipments has been constituted in the region, to feed an army or in case of siege. It is created through requisition among the population, especially of Food. 289 ID_STRUC_MINOR_STOCKPILE $ID_STRUC_MINOR_STOCKPILE 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Preserved Food       MBPStockpile  1 1 0 Extra Supply: 10 Loyalty Penalty: 3

name: Spearmaker
search:
Spearmaker Spearmakers assemble and store a variety of light to medium weaponry. Spearmakers will efficiently (and specifically) improve your spearmen Levy units, converting their 'Raw' version to a more experimented one.
As with the other buildings that improve units, you don't need to actually station the unit in the region, being within your nation and in supply is sufficient. 290 ID_STRUC_SPEARMAKER $ID_STRUC_SPEARMAKER 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Wood   Weapons    MBPStockpile MBP_Output Bonus_MBP 1 1 1 Attribute: 2 (params: $ATTR_HEAVYLEVY_IMP)

name: Closed Settlement
search:
Closed Settlement This structure will provide a Fortification bonus to the region, and once enough Fortification points are accumulated, a new castle-like building will be available for construction.

This modest hamlet, made of only a few houses huddled together is easy prey for bandits and robber knights. It can also attract some migrants.

This is cheap option to begin the fortifying of a region, its drawback being there is a 10% chance bandits appear once completed (EVENT). 291 ID_STRUC_CLOSED_SETTLEMENT $ID_STRUC_CLOSED_SETTLEMENT 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Protected Settlement
search:
Protected Settlement This hamlet will let you jump start the Fortification of a region, as a few inhabitants have some experience in defensive works. It is still possible bandits can be attracted though (15% chance) (EVENT).

This is a somewhat rare structure giving two Fortifications levels at once at the outset. 292 ID_STRUC_PROTECTED_SETTLEMENT $ID_STRUC_PROTECTED_SETTLEMENT 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Discreet Hamlet
search:
Discreet Hamlet The site of this hamlet has been choosen with care and it should not attract the unwanted attention of bandits.

This choice of Fortification will not attract any bandits. What's more, the land should provide enough Food for the inhabitants (EVENT: lump sum of Food provided). 293 ID_STRUC_DISCREET_HAMLET $ID_STRUC_DISCREET_HAMLET 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Rudimentary Earthworks
search:
Rudimentary Earthworks Around the settlement, the earth has been raised in a kind of crude breastwork, to funnel assaillants towards the two guarded entrances.

This Fortification choice will give a bonus in Infrastructure production, as the inhabitants have now some experience in levelling terrain. 294 ID_STRUC_RUDIMENTARY_EARTHWORK $ID_STRUC_RUDIMENTARY_EARTHWORK 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Infra Bonus: 15%

name: Trench and stakes
search:
Trench and stakes This minor locality is now protected in some places by a shallow trench with outward stakes. It is better than nothing to slow down raiders, or so the local lord said.(EVENT) He will use the opportunity of this task to enlist peasants into his militia (+1 Peasant unit).

This is a cheap choice to continue Fortifying the region. 295 ID_STRUC_TRENCH_STAKES $ID_STRUC_TRENCH_STAKES 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Timber Keep
search:
Timber Keep The local petty lord of the region decided a Timber Keep would be a fitting place for him and his handful of men. It certainly add some defensive value to the settlement.

This is a costly option compared to other choices, but it will raise the Fortification level by two and provide a Stewardship bonus (EVENT). 296 ID_STRUC_TIMBER_KEEP $ID_STRUC_TIMBER_KEEP 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Timber Wall
search:
Timber Wall A wall, even if only made of timber, would be a good choice to protect the settlement. It will not endure a siege, but would help fend off bandits or raiders.

This Fortification choice will give a Loyalty bonus to the region, as the inhabitants feel more protected than ever. 297 ID_STRUC_TIMBER_WALL $ID_STRUC_TIMBER_WALL 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 8

name: Fortified Manor
search:
Fortified Manor The Fortification level in the region is high enough to consider building a Fortified Manor, a first-level permanent defensive structure. It will allow the defenders to stay behind protective walls and any attacker will need either to assault or besiege.

The local lord is asking the populace to strongly fortify his manor into a defensive position. He has promised that many people will be able to retreat within its walls in case of an attack.

This permanent defensive building will provide a one-time Stewardship bonus to the region (EVENT). It allows the recruitment of Medium Infantry Standing Army Units, if you have any. 298 ID_STRUC_FORTIFIED_MANOR $ID_STRUC_FORTIFIED_MANOR 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 0 0

name: Fortified Village
search:
Fortified Village The population is taking things in hand by considerably Fortifying the village with a palisade enclosing it entirely.

The added security will help the peasants focus on their daily work, granting a bonus in Food productivity. It allows the recruitment of Medium Infantry Standing Army Units, if you have any. 299 ID_STRUC_FORTIFIED_VILLAGE $ID_STRUC_FORTIFIED_VILLAGE 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Fishes Legumes   Steward_Output Bonus_Manpower 1 1 0 Food Bonus: 15%

name: Garrisoned Village
search:
Garrisoned Village This village has grown enough that many men are always ready to take arms as warriors in a matter of minutes. The weak palisade won't deter much the enemy though.
This structure appears spontaneously once the required population is reached and will be replaced by the stronger Palisaded Village once the region reaches a population of 8. It allows the recruitment of Medium Infantry Standing Army Units, if you have any. 300 ID_STRUC_TN_GARRISONED_VILLAGE $ID_STRUC_TN_GARRISONED_VILLAGE 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         MBPStockpile MBP_Output  0 0 1

name: Qasaba
search:
Qasaba The Fortification level in the region is high enough to consider building a Qasaba, a first level permanent defensive structure. It will allow the defenders to stay behind protective walls and any attacker will need either to assault or besiege.

A Qasaba is usually a single stone or rock tower, either as part of a tower house or a tower isolated on a hilltop or commanding a field.

This defensive building will provide a one-time Stewardship bonus to the region (EVENT). It allows the recruitment of Medium Infantry Standing Army Units, if you have any. 301 ID_STRUC_MUS_QASABA $ID_STRUC_MUS_QASABA 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           0 1 0

name: Baronial Vassal
search:
Baronial Vassal Nominally, this small holding is under the control of the regional owner. Practically, a landed noble (Baron or equivalent) is ruling some part of it. He will provide to his liege some Manpower when needed and will defend fiercely his own little domain, but a large chunk of the taxes are kept for his own expenses. It's not all bad though, as his firm hand is of certain help in keeping the populace under control, in an age where authority was often decentralized.

It's possible to give a minor noble a barony through the regional decision 'Grant Land' and revoke it through the decision 'Remove Civilized'.
It allows the recruitment of Medium Infantry Standing Army Units, if you have any.
If the National Authority is very strong, this Baronial holding can either be disbanded automatically or provide an Absorb Vassal decision, should you have none (EVENT). 302 ID_STRUC_SPE_BARONIAL_VASSAL $ID_STRUC_SPE_BARONIAL_VASSAL 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         Food_Output Manpower_Output Income_Output  0 0 0 Standing Army Flat Bonus: 2 Lobbyhidden: 0 Loyalty Bonus: 10

name: Minor Fiefdom
search:
Minor Fiefdom Nominally, this small holding is under the control of the regional owner. Practically, a landed noble or a minor warlord is ruling some part of it. He will defend fiercely his own little domain and a large chunk of the regional taxes are kept for his own expenses. It's not all bad though, as his firm hand is of certain help in keeping the populace under control, in an age where authority was often decentralized.

It's possible to remove him from power by stationing an army or playing the regional decision 'Remove Civilized'. An extra fiefdom can be added to your realm with the decision 'Grant Land'.
It allows the recruitment of Medium Infantry Standing Army Units, if you have any.
If the National Authority is very strong, this fiefdom can either be disbanded automatically or provide an Absorb Vassal decision, should you have none (EVENT). 303 ID_STRUC_SPE_MINOR_FIEFDOM $ID_STRUC_SPE_MINOR_FIEFDOM 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         Food_Output Manpower_Output Income_Output  0 0 0 Standing Army Flat Bonus: 2 Lobbyhidden: 0 Loyalty Bonus: 10

name: Barracks
search: Barracks Barracks will allow Levy Heavy Infantry to be properly armed and provided with at least a rudimentary drilling. A nation without any barrack will indeed have mostly raw levies in its ranks. 304 ID_STRUC_BARRACKS $ID_STRUC_BARRACKS 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Weapons Armors   MBPStockpile Bonus_MBP 1 1 0 Creation Xp Bonus: 10 (params: $FAMHVYINFANTRY) Attribute: 6 (params: $ATTR_ANYINF_LEVY_IMP)

name: Tournament Ground
search:
Tournament Ground A place where the nobles will meet and clash in a mock fashion. The structure provides a decent amount of experience to several mounted units categories but does not unlock heavy cavalries; you'll need a Rudimentary Fort (or equivalent), another Tier I Military defensive structure.

The Tournament Ground is an early arena of jousting where knights, on horse or on foot (it was called a melee in this case) practiced against each other in two teams. While in theory a mock battle, injuries and bruises were numerous, along with the occasional death or severe trauma. 305 ID_STRUC_TOURNAMENT_GROUND $ID_STRUC_TOURNAMENT_GROUND 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Weapons Armors   MBPStockpile MBP_Output Steward_Output Bonus_MBP Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Creation Xp Bonus: 10 (params: $FAMCAVALRY, $FAMHVYCAVALRY)

name: Bowyer
search:
Bowyer The Bowyer will produce and store equipment for your army. He will also enable the recruitment of Archers and Bowmen, if they are part of your army roster. During peacetime, he can sell some of his work to hunters.
Finally, he can remove the 'Hastily Levied' penalty most ranged levies get upon being recruited, either in the region or within your nation (with reduced efficiency). 306 ID_STRUC_BOWYER $ID_STRUC_BOWYER 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         MBPStockpile MBP_Output Income_Output  1 1 1 Attribute: 3 (params: $ATTR_ARCHER_IMP)

name: Fletcher
search: Fletcher The Fletcher is a nice complement to the Bowyer, as he will provide quality material for your archers and javelinmen. During peacetime, he can sell some of his work to hunters. 307 ID_STRUC_FLETCHER $ID_STRUC_FLETCHER 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         MBPStockpile MBP_Output Income_Output  1 1 1 Creation Xplevel Bonus: 1 (params: $FAMARCHER, $FAMSKIRMISHER)

name: Levy Weaponsmith
search: Levy Weaponsmith The Levy Weaponsmith specializes in the modification of agricultural or craftsmen tools into weapons. Scythes, hammers, picks and many other implements of the daily life can be converted into remarkably efficient lethal weapons. This is made at the expense of civilian usage though. 308 ID_STRUC_LEVY_WEAPONSMITH $ID_STRUC_LEVY_WEAPONSMITH 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Tools    MBP_Output Infra_Output Bonus_MBP 1 1 1 Recruit Mbp Reduction: 35% (params: $FAMLTINFANTRY, $FAMSKIRMISHER, $FAMINFANTRY) Recruit Money Reduction: 25% (params: $FAMLTINFANTRY, $FAMSKIRMISHER, $FAMINFANTRY)

name: Warrior Assembly
search: Warrior Assembly The population of the region is made of hunters and men not softened by the life in the great cities of Germany, Italy or Al-Andalous. The local chiefs can summon them rapidly, and then will come in drove, ready to take arms, to fend off invaders or mount raids against their neighbors. 309 ID_STRUC_TN_WARRRIOR_ASSEMBLY $ID_STRUC_TN_WARRRIOR_ASSEMBLY 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Flax   Weapons    MBP_Output Manpower_Output Bonus_MBP 0 0 1 Creation Xp Penalty: 10

name: Blacksmith
search: Blacksmith With the Blacksmith, a substantial production of war equipment is at last achieved, which is a necessity if one wants to equip knights or armored footmen with quality weapons and armor. 310 ID_STRUC_BLACKSMITH $ID_STRUC_BLACKSMITH 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Iron Ore       MBPStockpile MBP_Output  1 1 0

name: Master Blacksmith
search:
Master Blacksmith This blacksmith masters his craft with such expertise that he is renowned in the whole region. Weigh carefully the choice of not building this structure, as this is a rare proposal.

The Master Blacksmith is also capable of improving any Standing Army Unit, whatever its category. 311 ID_STRUC_MASTER_BLACKSMITH $ID_STRUC_MASTER_BLACKSMITH 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Iron Ore Weapons      MBPStockpile MBP_Output Metal_Output Steward_Output  1 1 0 Mbp Bonus: 25% Attribute: 6 (params: $ATTR_EVERYTHING_IMP)

name: Reinforced Palisade
search:
Reinforced Palisade A reinforced palisade would be a sound addition to the fortified building of this region. With what is already in place, it should not require that much work to finish.

This Fortification option is cheap to complete but provides no additional bonus beyond a Fortification level. 312 ID_STRUC_REINFORCED_PALISADE $ID_STRUC_REINFORCED_PALISADE 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Refuge
search:
Refuge Some people in the village have advocated building a Refuge. This is a tunnel leading to a small underground cave where food and water is stored in case of siege.

This Fortification option will give a temporary bonus in Free Supply (will help feed people and military units, even during a siege). 313 ID_STRUC_REFUGE $ID_STRUC_REFUGE 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Extra Supply: 12

name: Trou de loup
search:
Trou de loup A Trou de Loup is a conical pit about 2 meters deep and 1.2 to 2 meters wide at the top. At the bottom of the pit, a sharpened wooden stake would be hammered in. That's what the local lord decided to make the peasants plant around the village, to secure it.

EVENT: This Fortification option has a 50% chance to provide one extra 'Remove Hostiles' Regional Decision, useful to get rid of bandits or marauders. 314 ID_STRUC_TROU_DE_LOUP $ID_STRUC_TROU_DE_LOUP 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Abattis
search:
Abattis Trees are felled and laid in row, to present an obstacle difficult to cross by any attacker.

This is an inexpensive option that does not give an extra bonus besides a single point in Fortifying the region. 315 ID_STRUC_ABATTIS $ID_STRUC_ABATTIS 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Sunken Opening
search:
Sunken Opening A Sunken Opening is being built as the only entrance to the village. It requires significant effort but the tunnel thus formed is very defensible by the inhabitants.

This Fortification option gives two points at once. 316 ID_STRUC_SUNKEN_OPENING $ID_STRUC_SUNKEN_OPENING 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Rudimentary Guardhouse
search:
Rudimentary Guardhouse Things are getting organized in the village and the local lord decides it is time for a modest permanent militia. They will be located in a guard house nearby the entry gate.

This Fortification option will provide one extra garrison to the Stronghold Site. 317 ID_STRUC_RUDIMENTARY_GUARDHOUSE $ID_STRUC_RUDIMENTARY_GUARDHOUSE 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Reinforced Wooden Gate
search:
Reinforced Wooden Gate The village peasants are reinforcing the gate with extra sturdy timbers interlaced with iron bars. The weakest point in the defense is now the strongest!
(EVENT)The local lord will use the opportunity of this task to enlist peasants into his militia (+2 Peasant units). 318 ID_STRUC_REINFORCED_WOODEN_GATE $ID_STRUC_REINFORCED_WOODEN_GATE 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Watch Tower
search:
Watch Tower A Watch Tower is being built, allowing easy surveillance of the countryside. In case of attack, it can be used to rain arrows down onto the enemy.

This Fortification option, in addition to providing two points at once, will provide a permanent bonus point in Siege Resist to the Stronghold Site. 319 ID_STRUC_WATCH_TOWER $ID_STRUC_WATCH_TOWER 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Extra Watch Tower
search:
Extra Watch Tower The local lord believes that an Extra Watch Tower would be a great addition to the defenses of the village, and he has promised to station within it a few of his men.

This Fortification option will provide one extra garrison to the Stronghold Site. 320 ID_STRUC_EXTRA_WATCH_TOWER $ID_STRUC_EXTRA_WATCH_TOWER 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Thicker Timber Wall
search:
Thicker Timber Wall A palisade is good, but a thick palisade would be better. The wall surrounding the village will be doubled in thickness by adding thick logs to it.

This Fortification option provides two points at once. 321 ID_STRUC_THICKER_TIMBER_WALL $ID_STRUC_THICKER_TIMBER_WALL 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Solid Foundation
search:
Solid Foundation Before building up a fort or small castle, the local lord wants a solid foundation to be made. This way, the future structure will be able to handle sieges much better.

This Fortification structure will provide a permanent bonus points in Siege Resist to the Stronghold Site. 322 ID_STRUC_SOLID_FOUNDATION $ID_STRUC_SOLID_FOUNDATION 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Rudimentary Fort
search:
Rudimentary Fort The Fortification level in the region is high enough to consider building a Rudimentary Fort, a second level permanent defensive structure. It will allow the defenders to stay behind protective walls and any attacker will need either to assault or besiege.

A Rudimentary Fort, made of logs, will be erected within the village wall. The lord is confident he will be able to defend the location with ease, while staying safe himself.

This defensive building is not the best to resist a siege, but the work required to complete it is rather modest. It also sports two extra garrisons compared to the Ringwork Castle. 323 ID_STRUC_RUDIMENTARY_FORT $ID_STRUC_RUDIMENTARY_FORT 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Flavor: 0

name: Palisaded Village
search:
Palisaded Village This village is almost as big as a small town in Medieval Europe. A great number of men are always ready to take arms as warriors in a matter of minutes. The weak palisade won't deter much the enemy though.
This structure appears spontaneously once the required population is reached. 324 ID_STRUC_TN_PALISADED_VILLAGE $ID_STRUC_TN_PALISADED_VILLAGE 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         MBPStockpile MBP_Output Steward_Output  0 0 1  Garrisoned Village

name: Rustic Ringwork Castle
search:
Rustic Ringwork Castle The local lord thinks nothing less than a wall of stone can stop determined assailants. Having one in a roughly circular shape within the village will be a great asset to defend it. A Timber Keep will reinforce it, in what could be viewed as a rustic castle of sort.

This defensive building provides a decent defensive bonus for its level, and will boost the loyalty of the local governor (Peer of the Realm) if there is one (EVENT). In addition, it has enough room to store significant Food reserves in case of siege. 325 ID_STRUC_RINGWORK_CASTLE $ID_STRUC_RINGWORK_CASTLE 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Extra Supply: 15 Flavor: 0

name: Rustic Hill Fort
search:
Rustic Hill Fort A hill located near the village (some call it a motte) will have a fort built on it, giving the location a very good defensive strength. Within the walls of the fort, extra militiamen can sortie against the attackers, should they assault the village instead.

This defensive building provides the best defensive bonus for its level. It cannot be built everywhere, though. 326 ID_STRUC_HILLFORT $ID_STRUC_HILLFORT 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Flavor: 0

name: Defensive Burgh
search:
Defensive Burgh A defensive burgh is a small fortified village from where the Anglo-Saxon soldiery can patrol the land.

It makes use of the Wool trade good, as light armors such as gambeson and brigantines make use of it. 327 ID_STRUC_ASX_DEFENSIVE_BURGH $ID_STRUC_ASX_DEFENSIVE_BURGH 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Wool       MBPStockpile MBP_Output Manpower_Output Steward_Output  0 0 0

name: Detinet
search:
Detinet This structure will appear spontaneously if the region is adjacent to a hostile non-Slav region. It can be manually upgraded to a Kremlin, a more powerful and versatile structure. Should you not do that once the population reaches 15, the Detinet will be removed for good.

A detinet is the fortified part of a Rus' inner city. It was a fairly common layout around the Xth century, as the vast tracks of lands that made Rus' were far from being safe, with principalities having only a firm hold on the most populated areas, with loose control on thousands of square kilometers around these urban centers. Pagan natives were still numerous, often hostiles and nearby principalities were not always friendly either. 328 ID_STRUC_RUS_DETINET $ID_STRUC_RUS_DETINET 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Wood         0 0 0 Loyalty Bonus: 5

name: Stronghold Site
search: Stronghold Site This special structure represents the location of the regional stronghold, be it a modest rustic fort or a mightly concentric castle. It is given for free once you start building a permanent Fortification and will support the various bonus provided by temporary defense buildings. 329 ID_STRUC_STRONGHOLD_SITE $ID_STRUC_STRONGHOLD_SITE 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           0 0 0

name: Bailey
search:
Bailey This structure will provide a Fortification bonus to the region, and once enough Fortification points are accumulated, a new castle-like building will be available for construction.

In its most basic definition, a Bailey is an inhabited enclosure protected by a wall of sort (it can be as simple as a palisade), adjacent to a fortified tower hosting the noble and his family. Originally appearing in Europe, the same concept more or less applied in some parts of the Muslim world (notably Persia) with the Kuhandiz, a combination of fortified and inhabited enclosure adjacent to a fortress.
This structure is a staple of fortified development and provides a solid bonus plus 2 Fortifications points. 331 ID_STRUC_BAILEY $ID_STRUC_BAILEY 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 8 Food Bonus: 12%

name: Cleared surrounding
search:
Cleared surrounding This structure will provide a Fortification bonus to the region, and once enough Fortification points are accumulated, a new castle-like building will be available for construction.

All trees have been felled around the castle and most obstacles displaced. The glacis thus created will prevent the attackers from approaching with impunity.
Rough terrains won't be appropriate for this work but where it can be done you'll get a bonus to siege resistance. 332 ID_STRUC_CLEARED_SURROUNDING $ID_STRUC_CLEARED_SURROUNDING 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Bastle Houses
search:
Bastle Houses This structure will provide a Fortification bonus to the region, and once enough Fortification points are accumulated, a new castle-like building will be available for construction.

These are reinforced farms around the castle, improving the protection of wealthier farmers.
Although fortifying farms have certain merits, making people more confident in their lord, it requires vast efforts in term of construction. 333 ID_STRUC_BASTLE_HOUSES $ID_STRUC_BASTLE_HOUSES 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 12 Infra Penalty: 15%

name: Bivallated Palisades
search:
Bivallated Palisades This structure will provide a Fortification bonus to the region, and once enough Fortification points are accumulated, a new castle-like building will be available for construction.

Two concentric palisades are erected around the keep, usually the interior one is taller than the exterior, so that archers can fire outside the initial perimeter.
EVENT: This structure will provide a significant and permanent Siege Resist bonus. 334 ID_STRUC_BIVALLATED_PALISADES $ID_STRUC_BIVALLATED_PALISADES 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Arrow Slits
search:
Arrow Slits This structure will provide a Fortification bonus to the region, and once enough Fortification points are accumulated, a new castle-like building will be available for construction.

Arrow slits allow archers to fire with almost impunity. Sometimes they were round and were called oillets (like an eye).
This structure will immediately raise an Archer unit if one is available to be recruited, if not Javelineers or Slingers will be drafted. 335 ID_STRUC_ARROW_SLITS $ID_STRUC_ARROW_SLITS 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Skins and Hides
search: Skins and Hides The most exposed parts of the keep and the defensive towers are covered with animals' skins or hides, soaked in water. This provides decent fire protection in case of attack (Siege Resist will significantly improve). 336 ID_STRUC_SKINS_AND_HIDES $ID_STRUC_SKINS_AND_HIDES 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Boulder Reserve
search: Boulder Reserve Disposition has been taken to gather a large reserve of boulders of different sizes, ready to be thrown from the wall against the enemy. 337 ID_STRUC_BOULDER_RESERVE $ID_STRUC_BOULDER_RESERVE 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Castle Lip
search:
Castle Lip The stone keep or primitive castle has the lower part of its wall inclined so that anything falling from the battlement (boulders pushed by defenders e.g.) would bounce further against the assailants, thus inflicting extra casualties to the enemy.
The Fortification level will only raise by a single point and no further bonus will be granted, but this option is rather cheap. 338 ID_STRUC_CASTLE_LIP $ID_STRUC_CASTLE_LIP 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Bailey with Watch towers
search:
Bailey with Watch towers The bailey, which is usually made of a single palisade has received extra defenses with the addition of a few watchtowers.
This will translate to more militiamen ready to defend the fortified site. 339 ID_STRUC_BAILEY_WATCHTOWERS $ID_STRUC_BAILEY_WATCHTOWERS 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Mantlet Wall
search: Mantlet Wall This is a low wall encircling the keep, protecting the base of the tower. There is no space left between the mantlet (or apron) and the keep itself, and it can replace entirely the motte on some Norman castles. It makes the keep much sturdier and more stable, allowing thicker walls. This is a rather costly option, but it bears some prestige for the local lord (while adding to the defensiveness of the site). 340 ID_STRUC_MANTLET_WALL $ID_STRUC_MANTLET_WALL 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Keep with basement
search: Keep with basement The keep is complemented by a large basement where vast stockpiles are gathered. It will help both the daily routine of maintaining soldiers gear and in case of siege, thanks to the extensive reserves. 341 ID_STRUC_KEEP_WITH_BASEMENT $ID_STRUC_KEEP_WITH_BASEMENT 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Unit Upkeep Bonus: 15% Extra Supply: 8

name: Large Motte
search:
Large Motte This artificial motte is very big and can thus support a larger-than-usual keep and protective wall. This is a costly and uncommon option that will ask many peasants to work as diggers and masons for a long time. The expertise is not lost, but the crops will suffer.
This option will instantly removes 100 accumulated Food, while adding a temporary modifier. 342 ID_STRUC_LARGE_MOTTE $ID_STRUC_LARGE_MOTTE 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Food Penalty: 10% Infra Bonus: 15%

name: Town Watch
search: Town Watch The Town Watch is an oddity among the military buildings, as it mostly benefits the citizens of the city it is in! The utility of the watch in case of serious fighting is indeed quite dubious, although it can be used in an auxiliary role. It can be further upgraded into the City Watch, a tier II stewardship building. 343 ID_STRUC_TOWN_WATCH $ID_STRUC_TOWN_WATCH 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Wool   Weapons    Manpower_Output Bonus_Manpower 1 1 0 Revolt Decrease: 15% Loyalty Bonus: 8 Local Watch

name: Armor Smith
search:
Armor Smith Armor is a catch-all term for a very large range of body protection. Light armor, such as leather cuirasses and gambesons can be made fairly easily, but it requires a lot of expertise, time, and resource to make high-quality armor as worn by knights and heavy lancers.
Such armors, contrary to popular belief, were not that heavy and had often to be tailored to the man they were destined to.
Depending on the quality, a piece of armor could take from 10 to 30 days, with a full set requiring several months (Prestige armor like the ones done for a King were playing in another court altogether, with a fabrication time of thousands hours). 344 ID_STRUC_ARMORSMITH $ID_STRUC_ARMORSMITH 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Iron Ore Armors  Tools    MBPStockpile MBP_Output Bonus_MBP 1 1 0

name: Weapons Workshop
search: Weapons Workshop Moving past the local Blacksmith, talented but unable to mass produce quality weapons to equip thousands of heavy infantry soldiers, Weapons Workshops group many craftsmen, from apprentices to very skilled workers, in the same infrastructure. Heralding of a time when the most successful and lucrative crafts will form guilds, this workshop is a necessity for a large standing army of heavy soldiery. 345 ID_STRUC_WEAPONS_WORKSHOP $ID_STRUC_WEAPONS_WORKSHOP 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Iron Ore Weapons  Tools    MBPStockpile MBP_Output Bonus_MBP 1 1 0

name: Coastal Watchtower
search:
Coastal Watchtower The Coastal Watchtower is one of the few buildings (in addition to harbors) that will uncover Fog of War in any adjacent sea region. Without this, enemy ships will pass unnoticed nearby your coasts ... And who knows if they won't land a party for a surprise raid?

Having an ample access to Stone and Wood will allow sturdier towers, which will comfort the local authority. 346 ID_STRUC_COASTAL_WATCHTOWER $ID_STRUC_COASTAL_WATCHTOWER 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Wood Stone   Steward_Output Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Fowlift Adjsea: 1

name: Wallburg
search: Wallburg A wallburg is a walled village as encountered in many nations of central Europe. A possible declination is the Gorod or Grad, in Slavonic countries. It features a massive wall often earth-filled with material taken from the exterior ditch and compacted then constrained by stone or timbers. This is a very resistant construction given its thickness, although the wall formed is seldomly very high. Within it live the population and the local noble. 347 ID_STRUC_WALLBURG $ID_STRUC_WALLBURG 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         Manpower_Output  0 0 0 Food Bonus: 5% Infra Bonus: 5% Stewardship Bonus: 5% Flavor: 0

name: Grad
search: Grad The Grad is an early Medieval fortified town in Slavonic countries (Gorod is the specific Russian designation, but it fills the same function). It features a massive wall often earth-filled with material taken from the exterior ditch and compacted then constrained by stone or timbers. This is a very resistant construction given its thickness, although the wall formed is seldomly very high. Within it live the population and the local noble. 348 ID_STRUC_GRAD $ID_STRUC_GRAD 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         Manpower_Output  0 0 0 Food Bonus: 5% Infra Bonus: 5% Stewardship Bonus: 5% Flavor: 0

name: Trelleborg
search: Trelleborg This is a rather uncommon Norse ring fortress. It takes the shape of a round fort and can be established at a crossroad to guard it. In this case, it will have several entrances, with the road passing in the middle. In other cases, it can serve as a boot camp and it is not uncommon to have it as the gathering point for hundreds of warriors before a raid or expedition. 349 ID_STRUC_TRELLEBORG $ID_STRUC_TRELLEBORG 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         MBPStockpile MBP_Output Manpower_Output Steward_Output  0 0 0 Stewardship Bonus: 10% Commerce Bonus: 8% Flavor: 0

name: Shell Keep
search: Shell Keep The keep is fully enclosed by a sturdy stone wall, with buildings set against it. There might be no additional tower and the design is still somehow simple, much more in any case than the later concentric castle design. 350 ID_STRUC_SHELL_KEEP $ID_STRUC_SHELL_KEEP 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         MBPStockpile MBP_Output Steward_Output  1 1 0 Stewardship Bonus: 15% Flavor: 0

name: Motte-and-Bailey
search: Motte-and-Bailey Illustrative of the fortification system in Western Europe in the Xth to XIIth centuries, the Motte-and-Bailey compound appeared in France and Normandy but then spread into England and the Holy Roman Empire. The villagers had some security provided by the bailey palisade while the lord enjoyed a strong protection thanks to a keep enclosed by at least a palisade, but often a wall, sitting on a small hill. With the need for more significant defenses, it evolved into stone castles, which abandoned all forms of permanent defenses for the population. 351 ID_STRUC_MOTTEBAILEY $ID_STRUC_MOTTEBAILEY 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         MBPStockpile MBP_Output Steward_Output  1 0 0 Stewardship Bonus: 15% Flavor: 0

name: Qal'at
search: Qal'at The Qalat is a small Fortress or fortified place in the Muslim world. It could function in the same way as a motte-and-bailey system, with a possible village built up against one of the walls. 352 ID_STRUC_QALAT $ID_STRUC_QALAT 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         MBPStockpile MBP_Output Steward_Output  0 1 0 Stewardship Bonus: 15% Flavor: 0

name: Filled Wall (Earth)
search: Filled Wall (Earth) By mustering the local peasants, even if crops could suffer from this, the local lord can have a sturdy wall erected around his stronghold. Filled with a vast quantity of dirt excavated by the serfs, it will be a formidable obstacle to any attacker. 353 ID_STRUC_FILLED_WALL_EARTH $ID_STRUC_FILLED_WALL_EARTH 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Food Penalty: 10% Infra Bonus: 15%

name: Ditch
search: Ditch A deep ditch surrounds entirely the stronghold making climbing or breaching the defensive wall harder. This option gives a significant Fortification bonus and some Siege Resist. 354 ID_STRUC_DITCH $ID_STRUC_DITCH 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Partitioned Bailey
search:
Partitioned Bailey Appreciated by the large local population, the lord decided that the bailey where the villagers live will be further partitioned into several neighborhoods with weak palisades, thus providing extra defenses. This vast bailey can only be built in regions with at least 10 populations.

The bonus provided will be twice as long as the usual. 355 ID_STRUC_PARTITIONED_BAILEY $ID_STRUC_PARTITIONED_BAILEY 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Food Bonus: 15% Loyalty Bonus: 8

name: Heated Sand
search:
Heated Sand Sand is commonly available in many regions and when heated and poured against the assailants will inflict major damage (oil was actually very rarely used!). Heating, transporting, and throwing sand or boulders was often a job that could be done by women if all the men were already defending the fortification.
EVENT: As long as the long-duration modifier 'Heated Sand' exists in the region, any besieger will suffer 10 damages every turn. 356 ID_STRUC_HEATED_SAND $ID_STRUC_HEATED_SAND 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Flavor: 0

name: Thick Ramparts
search:
Thick Ramparts This defensive wall or rampart is thick enough that it can host barracks for the castle defenders.

A bonus experience will be provided to Skirmishers and Medium Infantry recruited in this region (the regional modifier will last 15 turns). 357 ID_STRUC_THICK_RAMPARTS $ID_STRUC_THICK_RAMPARTS 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Creation Xp Bonus: 10 (params: $FAMLTINFANTRY, $FAMSKIRMISHER, $FAMINFANTRY)

name: Large Keep
search: Large Keep The keep of this stronghold is particularly massive, an oddity unheard of dozens of miles around. This certainly gives the local lord a kind of prestige or authority among his peers (provides a lump sum of Stewardship points plus one extra National Authority point). 358 ID_STRUC_LARGE_KEEP $ID_STRUC_LARGE_KEEP 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Hoarding
search: Hoarding This is an extra protected balcony, a  timber gallery built on top of a wall and projecting forward from the parapet, to give the defenders extra room and a better field of fire (extra Siege Resist). 359 ID_STRUC_HOARDING $ID_STRUC_HOARDING 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Tower House
search: Tower House In the same vein as the Bastle Houses or fortified farms, the Tower House is another means to strengthen a region's defense using buildings external to the main stronghold. Mostly developed in Italy and named Casatorre this system can deploy extra soldiers in these forward fortifications (One extra garrison added permanently to the Stronghold site). 360 ID_STRUC_TOWER_HOUSE $ID_STRUC_TOWER_HOUSE 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Flooded Moat
search: Flooded Moat A moat is a ditch filled with water. This provides a very good defense, as it means a lot of trouble to the attackers, whatever their siege method. This Fortification option provides a fair amount of Fortification points in addition to extra Siege Resistance, but can't be deployed in Arid terrains. 361 ID_STRUC_FLOODED_MOAT $ID_STRUC_FLOODED_MOAT 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Crenellated walls
search: Crenellated walls The top of the defensive wall is an alternation of merlons (plain wall) and crenels (gaps). Soldiers can stand protected behind this parapet, and rain fire on the enemy with impunity (extra Siege Resist). 362 ID_STRUC_CRENELLATED_WALLS $ID_STRUC_CRENELLATED_WALLS 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: City Militia
search: City Militia The City Militia is formed by the citizens of a large town or city. This building is a cheap way of filling the ranks in light troops, should the region is attacked, but requires a fairly high population count. 363 ID_STRUC_CITY_MILITIA $ID_STRUC_CITY_MILITIA 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Leather   Weapons    MBPStockpile Manpower_Output Bonus_Manpower 1 1 0

name: Master Bowyer
search: Master Bowyer The Master Bowyer will produce superb yet powerful bows fit for mightly lords. The numerous journeymen and apprentices under his guidance will also create very good bows which can equip elite soldiers. 364 ID_STRUC_MASTER_BOWYER $ID_STRUC_MASTER_BOWYER 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Wood    MBPStockpile MBP_Output Income_Output Bonus_MBP 1 1 0 Flavor: 0 Creation Xp Bonus: 10 (params: $FAMARCHER, $FAMSKIRMISHER) Attribute: 3 (params: $ATTR_ARCHER_IMP) Bowyer

name: Stable Master
search: Stable Master Stable masters are a rarity, but are true 'master of horses', knowing the animals in all their subtle facets and behaviors. They help select the best breeds, and once done, will also teach to horsemen how to best care and train their steed. 365 ID_STRUC_STABLE_MASTER $ID_STRUC_STABLE_MASTER 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Grain Flax   MBP_Output Steward_Output Bonus_MBP Bonus_Infra 1 1 0 Flavor: 0 Creation Xp Bonus: 10 (params: $FAMLTCAVALRY, $FAMSKIRMISHERCAVALRY, $FAMCAVALRY, $FAMHVYCAVALRY) Attribute: 6 (params: $ATTR_ANYCAV_IMP) Stable

name: Siege Workshop
search:
Siege Workshop Having a siege fabrication workshop on site is of great help to a stronghold, as it allows the maintenance of expert engineers of a large group of Siege Engines (it will provide extra Siege Engines in case of siege).
The workshop uses a great deal of Wood, but you'll also need either Tar or Stone as a bonus for the structure to be buildable. 366 ID_STRUC_SIEGE_WORKSHOP $ID_STRUC_SIEGE_WORKSHOP 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Wood   Naphta Stone   MBPStockpile MBP_Output Bonus_MBP 1 1 0

name: Army Camp
search: Army Camp EVENT: This army camp will speed up unit recovery, with a faster replenishment rate (+1 Effectiveness per turn, but 5 XP lost). 367 ID_STRUC_ARMY_CAMP $ID_STRUC_ARMY_CAMP 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         MBP_Output  1 1 0 Unit Upkeep Penalty: 5%

name: Shell Keep with Stone Bailey
search:
Shell Keep with Stone Bailey The keep is fully enclosed by a sturdy stone wall, with buildings set against it. There might be no additional tower and the design is still somehow simple, much more in any case than the later Concentric Castle design. This one has also a stone bailey, or inhabited enclosure for the villagers, protected by a solid stone wall.

The successors to this building (e.g. Concentric Castle) need an availability of Stone 'on site' and they won't be imported from afar.

EVENT: When built, this castle will provide you (once) an extra Military-themed Regional Decision. 368 ID_STRUC_SHELLKEEP_STBAILEY $ID_STRUC_SHELLKEEP_STBAILEY 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Stone       MBPStockpile MBP_Output Income_Output Steward_Output  1 1 0 Leader Quality Bonus: 1 Lobbyhidden: 0 Flavor: 0 Attribute: 3 (params: $ATTR_ANYINF_SAU_IMP)

name: Stone Castle
search:
Stone Castle One of the epitomes of the Medieval Ages, the Stone Castle is a massive endeavor whose construction requires ample Manpower, for a long time. A high stone wall defines the outer shape, with fortified towers at regular intervals. In its center, a stone keep is the last refuge of the nobility and what remains of the garrison but will see action from the start, as it is higher than the outer wall. This is not yet a Concentric Castle, with 2 concentric walls, but is already a formidable obstacle to overcome, either by assault or sieging.
Even if the style or name might differ, Stone Castles are ubiquitous in the medieval world, from Ireland to Persia.

The successors to this building (e.g. Concentric Castle) need an availability of Stone 'on site' and they won't be imported from afar.

EVENT: When built, this castle will provide you (once) an extra Regional Decision 'Absorb Vassal'. 369 ID_STRUC_STONE_CASTLE $ID_STRUC_STONE_CASTLE 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Stone       MBPStockpile MBP_Output Income_Output Steward_Output  1 1 0 Leader Quality Bonus: 1 Lobbyhidden: 0 Flavor: 0 Attribute: 3 (params: $ATTR_ANYINF_SAU_IMP)

name: Box Machicolation
search:
Box Machicolation Box Machicolation, also called brattice is a drop-box, an opening in the upper part of the wall where defenders can drop projectiles or boiling liquids against the attackers. It is made of wood or stone.
Given the importance of the Fortification, an extra detachment of Archers will be assigned to the edifice (free extra unit). 370 ID_STRUC_BOX_MACHICOLATION $ID_STRUC_BOX_MACHICOLATION 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Draw Bridge
search: Draw Bridge A Draw Bridge can be raised over a ditch or moat, to render an assault harder. The secondary function is that it will funnel all traffic through its passage, allowing easy taxation of the goods entering the city. 371 ID_STRUC_DRAW_BRIDGE $ID_STRUC_DRAW_BRIDGE 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Commerce Bonus: 8%

name: Portcullis
search: Portcullis A Portcullis is a massive metal gate that can be lowered rapidly. It requires the expertise of a Blacksmith and the assurance of a good supply of quality Metal to be manufactured (Will deduct 25 Money upon construction, but the establishment of a new Blacksmith will provide 10 Metals). 372 ID_STRUC_PORTCULLIS $ID_STRUC_PORTCULLIS 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Zwinger
search:
Zwinger This is a killing ground between two walls: The walls are sufficiently close to one another and at such angles that it is easy for the defender to rain projectiles against a struggling attacker.
EVENT: As long as the long-duration (30 turns) modifier 'Zwinger' exists in the region, any besieger will suffer 15 damages every turn. 373 ID_STRUC_ZWINGER $ID_STRUC_ZWINGER 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Flavor: 0

name: Machicolated Corner Tower
search: Machicolated Corner Tower The stone keep in the center of the castle is large enough to support extra corner towers by itself, which are themselves machicolated: they have openings where the defender can drop boulders or hot liquid against the assailants. This requires a large construction effort though, but lends prestige to the local lord. 374 ID_STRUC_MACHICOLATED_CT $ID_STRUC_MACHICOLATED_CT 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Improved Merlon
search:
Improved Merlon The merlons (the solid part between two gaps, the crenellations) of the castle's main wall are quite elaborate in shape. In Italy, they could have a swallowtail shape or be in a V-shaped notch so that a standing man can fire while still being protected. They were rounded in Africa and Islamic countries (and thus more decorative).
Given the importance of the fortification, an extra detachment of Light Infantry will be assigned to the edifice (free extra unit).
Merlons will greatly enhance the defensive capability of the structure (3 Points of Fortification and a significant Siege Resist bonus). 375 ID_STRUC_IMPROVED_MERLON $ID_STRUC_IMPROVED_MERLON 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Gatehouse/Guardhouse
search:
Gatehouse/Guardhouse The gate that harbors the portcullis is quite massive and a building by itself. Aptly named the Gatehouse or Guardhouse, it can be used as barracks for the soldiery.
The guards posted there will greatly help the local watch in fighting off highwaymen, marauders, or thieves (EVENT: One extra Decision 'Remove Hostiles' provided). 376 ID_STRUC_GATEHOUSE_GUARDHOUSE $ID_STRUC_GATEHOUSE_GUARDHOUSE 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Postern
search:
Postern This is a secondary and very discreet small door at the bottom of a wall. It allowed besieged to come and go unspotted, for a sortie or foraging for example. This could also be considered a liability, if the attackers spotted it, although it was always very well hidden (-1 to Siege Resist).
The modifier provided will have double the usual duration (30 turns). 377 ID_STRUC_POSTERN $ID_STRUC_POSTERN 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Extra Supply: 10

name: Shield Wall
search:
Shield Wall This specific fortification requires Hill terrain. This is an absolutely massive high wall protecting the only avenue of attack of a hill castle, like the shield of a soldier. It is rock solid but features little if any sophistication. The castle itself is thus very asymmetric.

A shield wall will add no less than 7 Siege Resist to the stronghold of the region. 378 ID_STRUC_SHIELD_WALL $ID_STRUC_SHIELD_WALL 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Chemin-de-ronde
search:
Chemin-de-ronde Sometimes called Allure or Walkway, a Chemin-de-Ronde is a protected passage near the top of a wall, where soldiers can stand and patrol. This is a very common feature of many castles, but it might be improved with stairs, recesses, and bartizans.

The regional stronghold will permanently receive an extra garrison and an increased anti-raiding capability. 379 ID_STRUC_CHEMIN_DE_RONDE $ID_STRUC_CHEMIN_DE_RONDE 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Inner wall
search:
Inner wall This Inner Wall is higher than the outer wall and can be manned with good use as soon as an assault begins on the exterior wall. Once it becomes equipped with its own set of towers, the whole stronghold could be called a Concentric Castle.
This structure will give a major Fortification bonus and the extra Siege Resist provided to the regional Stronghold is also quite significant. 380 ID_STRUC_INNER_WALL $ID_STRUC_INNER_WALL 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Cistern
search:
Cistern A Cistern will be a great asset in case of siege, should the castle well (if there is even one!) is too modest to sustain the garrison for a long time.
The modifier provided will have double the usual duration (30 turns). 381 ID_STRUC_CISTERN $ID_STRUC_CISTERN 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Extra Supply: 12 Health Bonus: 2%

name: Fighting Platform
search:
Fighting Platform A Fighting Platform is a flat area at the top of a keep or tower. It can be used to add a siege engine, but operating one (or several) for an extended period of time requires some logistics, and thus foreplanning.
You'll get a permanent extra Heavy Artillery from this structure. 382 ID_STRUC_FIGHTING_PLATFORM $ID_STRUC_FIGHTING_PLATFORM 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Barbican
search:
Barbican A Barbican is a fortified gateway or forward tower before the main entrance. Some can be quite large and sophisticated (and thus quite costly!), like a forward mini-castle.
Depending on the effort put into it, and how many (not very lucky) men garrisoning it, it can add a mild to very large resilience power to the main Stronghold (2 extra garrisons, Large Fortification and Siege Resist bonus). 383 ID_STRUC_BARBICAN $ID_STRUC_BARBICAN 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Training Ground
search: Training Ground The Training Ground will drill soldiers and horsemen alike. Moving in formation, understanding the signals and a lot of basic training with your weapon are necessary steps to strengthen both resolve and efficiency, when facing the enemy on the battlefield. 384 ID_STRUC_TRAINING_GROUND $ID_STRUC_TRAINING_GROUND 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Weapons       MBPStockpile  1 1 0 Creation Xp Bonus: 10 (params: $FAMINFANTRY, $FAMHVYINFANTRY) Attribute: 3 (params: $ATTR_EVERYTHING_IMP)

name: Jousting Field
search: Jousting Field A Jousting Field will see the 'fine fleur' of the chivalry compete against one another. It will help settle quarrels between nobles, entertain the king and last but not least, the necessity to be a good jouster will impose on the nobility the need of a constant training in the art of fighting. 385 ID_STRUC_JOUSTING_FIELD $ID_STRUC_JOUSTING_FIELD 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Weapons Armors   MBPStockpile MBP_Output Steward_Output Bonus_MBP 1 1 0 Standing Army Flat Bonus: 1 Flavor: 0 Attribute: 6 (params: $ATTR_HVYCAV_SAU_IMP) Tournament Ground

name: Supply Camp
search: Supply Camp In Medieval times, Supply Camps (or depots) were a rarity. Most armies dragged behind them a logistic train made of slow, lumbering wagons. Some nomads did without it through more frugality asked to their warriors and more looting of the countryside. A king planning in advance the stockpiling of Food and spare Equipment had to face numerous passive resistances like misappropriation at all levels of the supply chain or the simple fact the logistic system simply was not fit for the job. 386 ID_STRUC_SUPPLY_CAMP $ID_STRUC_SUPPLY_CAMP 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Preserved Food       Food_Output  1 1 0 Unit Upkeep Bonus: 10%

name: Concentric Castle
modifiers_note_html:
Replaces: Shell Keep with Stone Bailey, Stone Castle, BASTIONED_CASTLE
Leader Quality Bonus: 1
War Weariness Bonus: 2%
Lobbyhidden: 0
Flavor: 0
Attribute: 6 (params: $ATTR_ANY_SAU_IMP)
search:
Concentric Castle A Concentric Castle is the high point of medieval castle design. Its main feature is to have two walls, the inner one being higher than the outer one, effectively creating a 'castle in a castle'. These defenses were often so formidable that no assault was even attempted, and the garrison resistance to a long siege was the prime factor (if not the only one!) to consider. Their main drawback was their cost, for example Edward I 'longshanks' spent the equivalent of one year of the national budget for the construction of Caernarfon.

Concentric castles need an availability of Stone 'on site' and they won't be imported from afar.

EVENT: In addition to its other bonuses, the castle will grant you an extra Regional Decision, providing you with an extra <color>character once and a lump sum of 150 Legacy Points. 387 ID_STRUC_CONCENTRIC_CASTLE $ID_STRUC_CONCENTRIC_CASTLE 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military Stone Armors       MBPStockpile MBP_Output Income_Output Steward_Output  1 1 0 Leader Quality Bonus: 1 War Weariness Bonus: 2% Lobbyhidden: 0 Flavor: 0 Attribute: 6 (params: $ATTR_ANY_SAU_IMP) Shell Keep with Stone Bailey Stone Castle

name: Large Concentric Castle
modifiers_note_html:

Leader Quality Bonus: 1
War Weariness Bonus: 3%
Lobbyhidden: 0

Attribute: 6 (params: $ATTR_ANY_SAU_IMP)
search:
Large Concentric Castle This is an even larger than normal Concentric Castle, a good example would be the 'Krak des Chevaliers' in Syria. It possesses at least two walls, the inner one being higher than the outer one, effectively creating a 'castle in a castle'. Some fortresses, like the Krak would even have some parts protected by a third wall and several keeps. An inner glacis would also be made in the inner structure so that defenders could go from one side to the other with ease.

Concentric Castles need an availability of Stone 'on site' and they won't be imported from afar.

EVENT: In addition to its other bonuses, the castle will grant you an extra Regional Decision, providing you with an extra character once and a lump sum of 250 Legacy Points. 388 ID_STRUC_LARGE_CONCENTRIC_CASTLE $ID_STRUC_LARGE_CONCENTRIC_CASTLE 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military Stone Armors       MBPStockpile MBP_Output Income_Output Steward_Output  1 1 0 Leader Quality Bonus: 1 War Weariness Bonus: 3% Lobbyhidden: 0 Flavor: 0 Attribute: 6 (params: $ATTR_ANY_SAU_IMP) Shell Keep with Stone Bailey Stone Castle

name: Bulwark Tower
search:
Bulwark Tower A precursor to late-era Roundel (or Artillery) Tower, The Bulwark Tower is a heavily modified castle tower which is supporting a large fighting platform, a place where several siege engines are set, ready to rain onto the opponent all sorts of projectiles.
It requires significant effort to build and can only be supported by Concentric Castles or later structures.
What's more the many siege engines servants will be an extra supply burden on the fortress, which will reduce its ability to resist a prolonged siege. 389 ID_STRUC_BULWARK_TOWER $ID_STRUC_BULWARK_TOWER 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Naphta         1 1 0

name: Minor Garrison
search:
Minor Garrison This represents a medium-sized defensive force of up to one thousand men, garrisoning either a fortress or an important holding. They won't engage in offensive operations but are able to fend off intrusions if the invader is not too numerous.

It will prevent adding a Major Garrison, which is stronger but twice as costly. Last but not least, garrisons require constant upkeep in Equipment and Money to keep the soldiers properly equipped and motivated. 390 ID_STRUC_MINORGARRISON $ID_STRUC_MINORGARRISON 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Weapons Armors   MBPStockpile MBP_Output Income_Output Bonus_MBP Bonus_Steward 1 1 0

name: Major Garrison
search:
Major Garrison This represents a significant defensive force of up to several thousands men, garrisoning either a fortress or an important holding. They won't engage into offensive operations, but are able to fend off any enemy invading the region, barring perhaps a large army.

It will prevent adding a Minor Garrison, which is weaker but much cheaper. Last but not least, garrisons require constant upkeep in Equipment and Money to keep the soldiers properly equipped and motivated. 391 ID_STRUC_MAJORGARRISON $ID_STRUC_MAJORGARRISON 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Weapons Armors   MBPStockpile MBP_Output Income_Output Bonus_MBP Bonus_Steward 1 1 0

name: Kremlin
bonus_summary: Food 3, Infra 3 | from: Stone, Preserved Food
search: Kremlin A Kremlin is a stronger version of an early Detinet. Made of stone, the walls are supporting many towers hosting a significant garrison. Detinets and Kremlins provide many advantages to Rus', one being that it is very easy to get a walled structure in one go, contrary to the incremental construction of a Western European Concentric Castle. 392 ID_STRUC_RUS_KREMLIN $ID_STRUC_RUS_KREMLIN 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Wood   Stone Preserved Food   MBP_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Infra 0 0 0 Loyalty Bonus: 8 Flavor: 0 Detinet

name: Theodosian Walls
bonus_summary: Equipment 10, Manpower 5 | from: Weapons, Armors, Naphta
search: Theodosian Walls The Theodosian Walls, the pinnacle of Byzantine military engineering, stand as formidable multi-layered barriers, enduring through time and withstanding numerous sieges. Stretching across the western approach to Constantinople for over 6.5 km, and fortified with 96 imposing towers, it is an intricate defense made up of three distinct walls: outer, middle, and inner. The outer wall, though the shortest, initiates the defense; the middle wall, taller and thicker, escalates the fortification; and the inner wall, the most formidable, reaches an impressive thickness of up to 5 meters. Atop the inner wall lies a wide terrace, designed for the movement and operation of siege engines. Together, these layered defenses rendered Constantinople unconquerable for centuries, a testament to their extraordinary strength and innovative design. 393 ID_STRUC_BYZ_THEODOSIAN_WALLS $ID_STRUC_BYZ_THEODOSIAN_WALLS 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Stone   Weapons Armors Naphta  Income_Output Bonus_MBP Bonus_Manpower 0 0 0 Attribute: 6 (params: $ATTR_ANY_SAU_IMP)

name: Salpeter Works
search:
Salpeter Works A facility devoted to the extraction and purification of natural nitrates from earth, waste, or decomposed matter. Its workers supply the essential ingredient that makes the new black powder possible.
With the Dovecote building, it is one of the few structures that is a prerequisite for the Gunpowder Workshop, a pivotal building for gunpowder units.
Although precious for war, its nitrate-rich residues also nourish the fields. 394 ID_STRUC_SALPETER_WORKS $ID_STRUC_SALPETER_WORKS 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0 Food Bonus: 10%

name: Gunpowder Workshop
search:
Gunpowder Workshop A semi-artisanal workshop where saltpeter, charcoal (required in the region only), and sulfur are carefully milled and mixed into usable powder. Production is slow and hazardous, still relying on manual grinding and instinct rather than precision.

Gunpowder units recruited in a region without such a workshop will not be properly drilled and will therefore suffer a penalty. 395 ID_STRUC_GUNPOWDER_WORKSHOP $ID_STRUC_GUNPOWDER_WORKSHOP 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military Coal Sulfur       MBPStockpile MBP_Output  1 1 0 Attribute: 5 (params: $ATTR_ANY_GUNPOWDER_IMP)

name: Artillery Foundry
bonus_summary: Equipment 10, Money -4 | from: Iron Ore, Wood
search:
Artillery Foundry This building is a strict requirement for the fabrication of bombards. In addition, if such a unit is recruited in a region without a Gunpowder Workshop, it will suffer a penalty.

This is a specialized industrial yard where bronze and iron are cast into standardized bombards and cannon pieces for field and fortress use. Skilled founders and moulders work under royal or civic contract to meet the growing demand for gunpowder artillery. Historical examples would be the royal foundries of Liège (Belgium) and Milan (Italy). 396 ID_STRUC_ARTILLERY_FOUNDRY $ID_STRUC_ARTILLERY_FOUNDRY 3 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Iron Ore Wood   MBPStockpile Bonus_MBP Bonus_Income 1 1 0

name: Royal Harness Workshop
search:
Royal Harness Workshop A specialized armory dedicated to crafting and maintaining harnesses, barding, and tack for heavy cavalry. The precision of such workshops ensured the endurance of both men and beasts. Historical examples include the royal stables of Blois (France) and Westminster (England).

EVENT: Non-levy Cavalry units recruited in the region will immediately receive the 'Improved Equipment' perk upon creation. 397 ID_STRUC_ROYAL_HARNESS_WORKSHOP $ID_STRUC_ROYAL_HARNESS_WORKSHOP 3 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military         MBPStockpile MBP_Output  1 1 0 Attribute: 6 (params: $ATTR_HVYCAV_SAU_IMP)

name: Permanent Company Quarters
search: Permanent Company Quarters Purpose-built housing for men-at-arms and sergeants, marking the rise of standing, salaried troops. These quarters offered stability and readiness between campaigns. An historical example would be the compagnies d'ordonnance of Charles VII in France. 398 ID_STRUC_PERMANENT_QUARTERS $ID_STRUC_PERMANENT_QUARTERS 3 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Weapons Armors   MBPStockpile Bonus_MBP 1 1 0 Unit Upkeep Bonus: 35% Maxmanpower Bonus: 4%

name: Scribe
search:
Scribe With a literacy rate of around 5% in Medieval Europe (mostly clergy and civil servants), compared to about 20% for the Roman Empire, this local Scribe has no trouble finding work to do, from minor request letters to formal legal documents. He is not affiliated with the Church in any way, which is always an issue as knowledge is power.

The Scribe produces less Legacy than a Monastery or Savant Monk, but does not require Parchment. 403 ID_STRUC_SCRIBE $ID_STRUC_SCRIBE 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Parchment & Vellum    Income_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Income 1 0 0

name: Elder
search:
Elder In societies where experience is not written in books but transmitted orally, Elders play a big role in the memory of the knowledge of the tribe. This will ensure that the young chiefs and warriors can avoid repeating the same mistakes as their forebear.

EVENT: The Elder will very infrequently generate 'luck', an unfathomable resource that will be used automatically to avoid characters fate events, death in battles and assassination attempts. 404 ID_STRUC_TN_ELDER $ID_STRUC_TN_ELDER 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Livestock    Steward_Output Bonus_Piety 0 0 1 Agetoken Bonus: 1% Lobbyhidden: 0

name: Professional Mourner
search:
Professional Mourner In regions where Muslim influence is still new or partial, the oriental custom is often to bring mourners external to the family of the deceased. Islam discourage excessive grieving and wailing, but some wealthy families will only adjust to the new faith progressively. This may cause the old ways to linger longuer, but will help keep the population satisfied.

A rather strong Tier I Piety building, but will be removed automatically when the population reaches 12. 411 ID_STRUC_MUS_PROFESSIONAL_MOURNER $ID_STRUC_MUS_PROFESSIONAL_MOURNER 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Drugs & Herbs    Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 0 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 8 Convert Penalty: 5%

search:
Initiation Ritual The initiation ritual is a very ubiquitous one, whatever the society encountered. It will cause many young men (and sometimes women, depending on the society) to become adults with full rights... and duties.

name: Burial Ritual
search:
Burial Ritual Burial rituals vary wildly between societies, but they all fill the same role: to cope with the inescapability and permanence of death. Hindus practice cremation, while Mongols perform the Sky Burial, which involves letting the body be devoured by birds. Odder, at least for monotheistic religions, are the practices of a few tribes of endocannibalism (eating the dead) or dancing with the remains every few years.

Once the preparation is done, the ritual will begin and will provide its Piety-generation effect for a few turns (EVENT: 20% chance per turn to be removed; once it happens, 3-6 Legacy Points will be granted). 417 ID_STRUC_TN_BURIAL_RITUAL $ID_STRUC_TN_BURIAL_RITUAL 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Drugs & Herbs       Piety_Output  0 0 1

name: Astrologer House
search:
Astrologer House An astrologer has set up in the region, studying the mysteries of the celestial sphere pierced here and there, allowing the light of heaven to become the stars we see from Earth, the center of the universe. As long as he aligns not only the planets but his views with the ones of the clergy, they will let him do his esoteric work.

search:
Astronomer Study This modest astronomer is a pioneer, and might have doubts about the current Ptolemaic and Aristotelian view of the Earth at the center of the universe. He is not very vocal and certainly not well known, so is mostly left alone by Church officials. Perhaps a minor noble might become his patron, if his work is of high enough quality.

He makes use of Copper in order to craft his scientific instruments. 423 ID_STRUC_ASTRONOMER_STUDY $ID_STRUC_ASTRONOMER_STUDY 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Copper   Brass Tools   Piety_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 0 0
