search:
Small Mosque This building is a pivotal one, as it will unlock the second part of Tier I Piety buildings.

The Mosque is the place of worship for Muslim populations and is the successor building to the Musalla. Its role is very important for any Muslim community, as this is where prayers are done and sermons listened to. 425 ID_STRUC_SMALL_MOSQUE $ID_STRUC_SMALL_MOSQUE 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Stone Tiles & Brick Silver  Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 0 1 0 Transverse output: Gain 1.5 piety for any building under Piety category. Limit 18. Convert Bonus: 10%

name: Minor Temple
bonus_summary: Piety 3 | from: Stone, Tiles & Brick, Silver
search:
Minor Temple A more permanent structure for the worship of many religions or cults (Christian and Muslim have their own buildings). It will provide spirituality and a place to meditate for the population.

This building unlock the second part of Tier I Piety buildings. 426 ID_STRUC_SPE_MINOR_TEMPLE $ID_STRUC_SPE_MINOR_TEMPLE 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Brass   Stone Tiles & Brick Silver  Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 0 0 0 Loyalty Bonus: 9 Convert Bonus: 7%

name: Pagan Minor Temple
bonus_summary: Piety 3, Stewardship 3 | from: Livestock, Wild Beasts
search:
Pagan Minor Temple A more permanent structure for the worship of one of the Pagan faith, such as Animism, Tengrism or the Old Norse religion. Pagan religious buildings often provide direct comfort to the locals, but are not very good at re-converting people who decide to follow one of the monotheistic religions of the Book.

This building unlock the second part of Tier I Piety buildings. 427 ID_STRUC_MINOR_PAGAN_TEMPLE $ID_STRUC_MINOR_PAGAN_TEMPLE 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Drugs & Herbs   Livestock Wild Beasts   Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 0 0 1 Loyalty Bonus: 6 Revolt Decrease: 14%

name: Sacred Altar
bonus_summary: Piety 3, Stewardship 3 | from: Livestock, Wild Beasts
search:
Sacred Altar Even more important than a worshipping spot is a consacred altar to the primodial gods of a nomadic or tribal nation. It is a great stabilizing factor in a tribal society, providing piety and stewardship to the local clergy and nobility.

This building unlock the second part of Tier I Piety buildings. 428 ID_STRUC_TN_SACRED_ALTAR $ID_STRUC_TN_SACRED_ALTAR 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Drugs & Herbs   Livestock Wild Beasts   Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 0 0 1 Piety Bonus: 10% Revolt Decrease: 10% Stewardship Bonus: 10%

name: Priest Parsonage
search: Priest Parsonage The Church priest is important and influential enough to have a parsonage built for him, even if some of the tithes will have to be diverted to fund it. 429 ID_STRUC_PRIEST_PARSONAGE $ID_STRUC_PRIEST_PARSONAGE 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Frankincense   Religious Craftsmanship    Income_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 0 0

name: Lord Chapel
bonus_summary: Piety 1, Stewardship 2 | from: Silver, Precious Stones
search: Lord Chapel At the juncture between the two influential worlds of the Middle Ages, this building will benefit both. It is often attached to the Lord's Manor, so is not using up a lot of real estate. 430 ID_STRUC_LORD_CHAPEL $ID_STRUC_LORD_CHAPEL 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Silver Precious Stones   Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 0 0

name: Monastery
bonus_summary: Food 2, Money 5 | from: Wine, Honey & Wax, Drugs & Herbs
search: Monastery A Monastery gathers behind its walls a community of monks or nuns, religious people aspiring to worship their god without too much interference from the material world (Monastery comes from Monos, 'alone' in Greek). They were productive people though, writing or copying religious books, and/or providing the region with quality foods and wines. 431 ID_STRUC_MONASTERY $ID_STRUC_MONASTERY 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Parchment & Vellum Religious Craftsmanship  Wine Honey & Wax Drugs & Herbs  Piety_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Income 1 0 0

name: Savant Monk
search: Savant Monk From time to time, a monk or nun will distinguish themselves. This enlightened person will go beyond the usual studies of religious texts and will produce works that will advance science. 432 ID_STRUC_SAVANT_MONK $ID_STRUC_SAVANT_MONK 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Parchment & Vellum       Piety_Output  1 0 0

name: Cloister
search: Cloister The Cloister is harboring a community under the vow of silence. They will not speak so as to be closer to God. This building requires a Monastery, and is perturbed by the presence of too many commercial activities, hampering the quietness the monks and nuns seek. 433 ID_STRUC_CLOISTER $ID_STRUC_CLOISTER 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Religious Craftsmanship    Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 0 0 Struc Output Transverse: 12 (params: $TRANSVERSE_OUTPUT_STC1_PENALTY, $POP_JOB_COMMERCE, $POP_JOB_PIETY, 100, 100)

name: Tithe Barn
bonus_summary: Food 2, Money 1, Piety -2 | from: Livestock, Legumes, Flax
search: Tithe Barn This was a type of barn used for storing rents and tithes. Farmers were required to give one-tenth of their produce to the established church, which often lead to discontentment and grumbling. 434 ID_STRUC_TITHE_BARN $ID_STRUC_TITHE_BARN 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Grain   Livestock Legumes Flax  Food_Output Income_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety 1 0 0 Loyalty Penalty: 4

name: Sufi House
search: Sufi House A single reclusive Sufi lives here and through meditation strives to reach a perfection in worship. At times these esoterics, almost mystical methods are disapproved by the Muslim clergy. In any case, his behavior will certainly have a lasting impact on how people consider their faith. The drawback of this building is that it must be upgraded to a Sufi Brotherhood before reaching a population or 12, or it will disappear, as the Sufi will move to a quieter place. 435 ID_STRUC_MUS_SUFI_HOUSE $ID_STRUC_MUS_SUFI_HOUSE 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Livestock Legumes   Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 0 1 0 Legacy Bonus: 1

name: Muslim Scholar
search: Muslim Scholar This is the equivalent to the Savant Monk of Christian nations, with the notable difference the scholar can easily becomes a Savant, representing here the more open approach to sciences most Muslim nations had during this times, contrary to Christian Europe. 436 ID_STRUC_MUS_SCHOLAR $ID_STRUC_MUS_SCHOLAR 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Parchment & Vellum       Piety_Output  0 1 0

name: Muslim Savant Study
search: Muslim Savant Study An improvement over the Muslim Scholar building, the Muslim Savant Study will provide more than one Legacy per turn to the owning nation, placing the Savant Study in a rare position among all tiers I Piety structures. What's more, the presence of a Calligrapher Artisan will certainly help him create beautiful books. 437 ID_STRUC_MUS_SAVANT_STUDY $ID_STRUC_MUS_SAVANT_STUDY 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Parchment & Vellum       Piety_Output  0 1 0

name: Imam House
search:
Imam House An Iman is a honorable member of a community, appointed with a majority of votes as the person in charge of convening the faithful to the prayers. He is a great addition to a Mosque, but requires it. His spiritual guidance is considered Religious Works.

A small fee will be necessary to organize properly the Muslim community around the new Mosque and its Imam. 438 ID_STRUC_MUS_IMAM_HOUSE $ID_STRUC_MUS_IMAM_HOUSE 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious   Religious Craftsmanship  Parchment & Vellum    Income_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 0 1 0

name: Calligrapher Artisan
search:
Calligrapher Artisan This artisan combines art and writing in a single approach. The design and execution of lettering with a broad tip instrument or brush in one stroke is used to decorate monuments, mosque or books.

EVENT: The artisan can rarely helps a Muslim Savant (if there is one in the region) produces a superb book akin to a work of art. 439 ID_STRUC_MUS_CALLIGRAPHER_ARTISAN $ID_STRUC_MUS_CALLIGRAPHER_ARTISAN 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Parchment & Vellum       Piety_Output Steward_Output  0 1 0 Flavor: 0 Loyalty Bonus: 4

name: Gravedigger
search: Gravedigger Every village needed a Gravedigger, given the low life expectancies of those disease-ridden times. He also provided a modest comfort to the family of the deceased, if no priest was available. 440 ID_STRUC_GRAVEDIGGER $ID_STRUC_GRAVEDIGGER 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious           1 1 0 Health Bonus: 2% Loyalty Bonus: 4

name: Walled Cemetery
bonus_summary: Piety 3, Stewardship 2 | from: Marble, Ceramics, Religious Craftsmanship
search: Walled Cemetery The Walled Cemetery is a much better maintained cemetery, where even petty nobles can be buried. Marble graves are occasionally present, as are mausoleums. 441 ID_STRUC_WALLED_CEMETERY $ID_STRUC_WALLED_CEMETERY 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Marble Ceramics Religious Craftsmanship  Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Health Bonus: 3% Loyalty Bonus: 3 Commoner Cemetery

name: Almshouse
search: Almshouse In the Christian world, Almshouses were established in the 10th century in Britain, to provide a place of residence for poor, old and distressed people, but they existed in all societies.  Almshouses were originally formed as extensions of the Church system and were later adapted by local officials and authorities. 442 ID_STRUC_ALMSHOUSE $ID_STRUC_ALMSHOUSE 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Legumes Cloth   Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 0 0 Revolt Decrease: 5%

name: Religious Celebration
search:
Religious Celebration One of the chief preoccupations of the time was to celebrate holy figures, saints and prophet(s). The most important of these holidays set aside all work, which was much appreciated by the toiling classes. EVENT: Once this building is done, it will be removed and a number of Piety points equal to the current Loyalty will be credited to the region. If Loyalty was 75 or more, 5 Legacy points will also be added.

This celebration will perform better if you have something to show to the crowd, as such Religious Works might be a necessity. 443 ID_STRUC_RELIGIOUS_CELEBRATION $ID_STRUC_RELIGIOUS_CELEBRATION 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Religious Craftsmanship         1 1 0

name: Religious Preceptor
search: Religious Preceptor Often from the clergy, preceptors were a form of private education for the scions of the nobles, where they would learn to read and write. They read religious texts most often, and if lucky (or from a great noble family) additional texts from the philosophers and statesmen of ancient civilizations (Greeks, Romans, Persians, Indians and many others). 444 ID_STRUC_RELIGIOUS_PRECEPTOR $ID_STRUC_RELIGIOUS_PRECEPTOR 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Gold Parchment & Vellum   Piety_Output Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Stewardship Bonus: 5%

name: Mask/Headdress Maker
bonus_summary: Money 2, Piety 2 | from: Luxury, Precious Stones
search: Mask/Headdress Maker Masks and headdresses are in great demand in many tribal or nomadic societies. Either as a mean to show one's importance, or as to designate a special function (priest, warchief, etc.). A special case, discreet in use but rather important nonetheless is the usage of masks in several African tribes and a few nomadic ones, as a mean to enter a Secret Society... 445 ID_STRUC_TN_MASKHEAD_MAKER $ID_STRUC_TN_MASKHEAD_MAKER 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Wood   Luxury Precious Stones   Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety 0 0 1

name: Scarification Ritual (Preparing)
search:
Scarification Ritual (Preparing) A scarification ritual is another way to show your dedication to your belief, be it religious or warlike. It will give fervor to warriors while reinforcing the control of local shamans over the population.

Once the preparation is done, the ritual will begin and provide its military-related effects for a few turns (EVENT: 20% chance per turn to be removed; once it happens, 3-6 Legacy Points will be granted). 446 ID_STRUC_TN_SCARIFICATION_RITUAL $ID_STRUC_TN_SCARIFICATION_RITUAL 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Drugs & Herbs       MBP_Output Piety_Output  0 0 1 Creation Xp Bonus: 10 Recruit Mpw Reduction: 10%

name: Scarification Ritual (Performing)
search:
Scarification Ritual (Performing) A scarification ritual is another way to show your dedication to your belief, be it religious or warlike. It will give fervor to warriors while reinforcing the control of local shamans over the population.

EVENT: This is the active version of the ritual; it has a 20% chance per turn to stop; once it happens, 3-6 Legacy Points will be granted. 447 ID_STRUC_TN_SCARIFICATION_RITUAL_ACTIVE $ID_STRUC_TN_SCARIFICATION_RITUAL_ACTIVE 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Drugs & Herbs       MBP_Output Piety_Output  0 0 1 Creation Xp Bonus: 10 Recruit Mpw Reduction: 10%

name: Trance Ritual (Preparing)
search:
Trance Ritual (Preparing) While in trance, the people undergoing the ritual will often experience visions of great deeds, although very disturbing experiences are not unheard of.

Once the preparation is done, the ritual will begin and provide its piety-related effect for a few turns (EVENT: 20% chance per turn to be removed; once it happens, 4-8 Legacy Points will be given, unless it goes badly, in which case you'll lose 1-4).

The chances of the ritual going badly are lowered with the presence of an Elder and/or a Bones Diviner, and this effect is cumulative. 448 ID_STRUC_TN_TRANCE_RITUAL $ID_STRUC_TN_TRANCE_RITUAL 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Drugs & Herbs       Piety_Output  0 0 1 Piety Bonus: 20%

name: Trance Ritual (Performing)
search:
Trance Ritual (Performing) People seem elated by what they experience.
While in trance, the people undergoing the ritual will often experience visions of great deeds, although very disturbing experiences are not unheard of.

EVENT: This is the active version of the ritual; it has a 20% chance per turn to stop; once it happens, 4-8 Legacy Points will be granted. 449 ID_STRUC_TN_TRANCE_RITUAL_ACTIVEG $ID_STRUC_TN_TRANCE_RITUAL_ACTIVEG 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Drugs & Herbs       Piety_Output  0 0 1 Piety Bonus: 20%

name: Trance Ritual (Performing)
search:
Trance Ritual (Performing) The ritual is going badly, and under trance, the subjects are all crying and howling! Expect no Legacy reward from it when it ends; on the contrary.

EVENT: This is the active version of the ritual; it has a 20% chance per turn to stop; once it happens, 1-4 Legacy Points will be lost. 450 ID_STRUC_TN_TRANCE_RITUAL_ACTIVEB $ID_STRUC_TN_TRANCE_RITUAL_ACTIVEB 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Drugs & Herbs       Piety_Output  0 0 1 Health Penalty: 10% Agetoken Penalty: 5%

name: Secret Society
search: Secret Society Not much is known on the extent of Secret Societies in tribal and nomadic societies. They are important in what they achieve but almost invisible to the commoner and utterly forbidden to the foreigner, even merchants commercing with the tribes for years. Their common role is probably to bond people of the same social strata together in the same, often mystic, activity, although it certainly facilitate the making of a coup to replace the local chief (EVENT). 451 ID_STRUC_TN_SECRET_SOCIETY $ID_STRUC_TN_SECRET_SOCIETY 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Furs & Pelts   Drugs & Herbs    Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Piety 0 0 1 Stewardship Bonus: 20% Revolt Increase: 25%

name: Iberian Orders - Commandery
base_output_summary: Food 10, Money -10, Equipment 5, Stewardship -6, Piety 8, Legacy 40
bonus_summary: Equipment 2, Manpower 1, Piety 2 | from: Parchment & Vellum, Weapons, Armors
search:
Iberian Orders - Commandery This represents a modest Commandery owned by a Christian Order of Knights. Not quite a castle, but still walled and somehow fortified, it houses a score or two of knights. These formidable opponents or allies are supported by men at arms and aspiring squires.

Despite being registered as belonging to the region owner, the Commandery can't be removed willingly and will tax some of the income of the region for the Knightly order and not for the region owner. EVENT: It can spontaneously raises its own troops or evolve to a larger structure. With times and the approval of the Holy See, a complete web of commanderies, priories and bailiwicks can emerge, a state within the state that can contest and challenge the decisions of the local king. 452 ID_STRUC_IOS_COMMANDERY $ID_STRUC_IOS_COMMANDERY 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious   Religious Craftsmanship  Parchment & Vellum Weapons Armors  MBP_Output Food_Output Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_MBP Bonus_Manpower Bonus_Piety 0 0 0 Convert Bonus: 15% Loyalty Bonus: 6

name: Occultist
search:
Occultist Event Building.

People in this region are not very faithful, and thus prone to turn themselves toward beliefs that are condemned by the clergy. This structure might appear in regions with low Loyalty, sapping Piety and favoring the creation of heresy. It never appears if there is an ongoing heresy though.

EVENT: If successful enough, an Occultist will sell trinkets supposedly embued with esoteric power to the gullible. He will also attract a few henchmen as followers. 457 ID_STRUC_EVT_OCCULTIST $ID_STRUC_EVT_OCCULTIST 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Sulfur   Brass    Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Manpower Bonus_Piety 1 1 1

name: Cult Site
search:
Cult Site This polytheist cult is rather secretive, although not completely closed to the outside world. The belief of the faithful here might be pagan, or a derivation of Hinduism or Buddhism.

EVENT: In any case, this is what the local population desires and it will be defended fiercely: If the region falls under the control of a monotheistic religion, it will be removed while triggering a revolt. 458 ID_STRUC_EVT_CULT_SITE $ID_STRUC_EVT_CULT_SITE 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Drugs & Herbs   Livestock    Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 1 1

name: Secret Cult
bonus_summary: Piety -6 | from: Precious Stones, Wild Beasts
search: Secret Cult This is a rare variation of the Cult Site building. The followers of this pagan faith are now hiding from the outside world, while growing their influence in the region. This is basically a stronger version of the Cult Site, with a more potent revolt if it is removed. 459 ID_STRUC_EVT_SECRET_CULT $ID_STRUC_EVT_SECRET_CULT 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Frankincense   Precious Stones Wild Beasts   Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 1 1 War Weariness Penalty: 10% Lobbyhidden: 0 Cult Site

name: Gypsy
bonus_summary: Manpower -1, Piety -4, Stewardship 5 | from: Precious Stones
search:
Gypsy Event Building.

A Gypsy has settled in the region! This intriguingly exotic woman will foretell your future with her tarots cards, and it is said that nobles, under disguise, come to visit her. The clergy pretends they might succumb to the Devil in doing so. The truth is probably somewhere in between.

The Gypsy will generate rarely 'luck', an unfathomable resource that will be used automatically to avoid characters fate events, death in battles and assassination attempts. 460 ID_STRUC_EVT_GYPSY $ID_STRUC_EVT_GYPSY 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Precious Stones    Piety_Output Bonus_Manpower Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Flavor: 0

name: Shrine
bonus_summary: Piety 8 | from: Horses, Furs & Pelts, Weapons
search:
Shrine A blessed place for a pagan religion. A hierophant or shaman is always worshiping here, and the faithful believe a god of their pantheon can manifest their pleasure via a sign, if they bring enough offerings.

This building has a low chance to trigger a beneficial effect. 461 ID_STRUC_TN_SHRINE $ID_STRUC_TN_SHRINE 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Drugs & Herbs   Horses Furs & Pelts Weapons  Food_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 0 0 1 Flavor: 0

name: Mass Graves
search:
Mass Graves Event Building.

After a great battle, there is a chance the local population is requisitioned to bury the dead in large pits, lest disease and plague spread.

This building will disapear by itself after some time.||NOTE: Modifier giving -10% health. Some chance to appear after big battles. 10% chance to be removed per turn, no other mean. 462 ID_STRUC_EVT_MASS_GRAVES $ID_STRUC_EVT_MASS_GRAVES 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious           1 1 1 Health Penalty: 10%

name: Grave Robbers
bonus_summary: Equipment 2, Money 2, Metal 1, Stewardship -3 | from: Weapons, Armors
search:
Grave Robbers Event Building.

Sometime with mass graves come Grave Robbers. These unsavory individuals will do anything to get the gold and the weapons of the deceased, to the dismay of the population and the clergy.

They will go away when the mass graves disapear, or if the army hunts them.||NOTE: 25% chance to appear per turn if there is a mass grave and less than 50 Cbt power. Can be removed as an impediment with an army. 463 ID_STRUC_EVT_GRAVEROBBERS $ID_STRUC_EVT_GRAVEROBBERS 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Weapons Armors   MBP_Output Metal_Output Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_MBP Bonus_Income Bonus_Metal Bonus_Steward 1 1 1 Flavor: 0

name: Abbey
bonus_summary: Food 2, Money 4, Stewardship -2 | from: Wine, Honey & Wax, Drugs & Herbs, Livestock
search:
Abbey An evolution of a Monastery, the Abbey (from abba, Aramean word for 'father') often houses a large community of monks, with a no-so-small part of their activities dedicated to agricultural and industrious activities. Their dedication to these secular tasks made them very efficient, which translated into rich and powerful monastic orders throughout the Christian world.

As the economic weight of Abbeys developed, they favored the Church over the local lord in the mind of many people.

Contrary to the Monastery, an Abbey will generate Religious Works without consuming Parchments, it can be the desired outcome, or a problem for the region.
EVENT: The Abbey has a low chance to create a Priory in the region, for free, this chance is much increased with a pious ruler. 465 ID_STRUC_ABBEY $ID_STRUC_ABBEY 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious   Religious Craftsmanship  Wine Honey & Wax Drugs & Herbs Livestock Piety_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Income Bonus_Steward 1 0 0  Monastery

name: Priory
bonus_summary: Food 2, Money 2, Stewardship -1 | from: Wine, Honey & Wax, Drugs & Herbs, Livestock
search:
Priory A Priory is a kind of annex from an Abbey, to further improve how the land is administered by it.
You can either wait for the Abbey to build one (freely with a low chance per turn) or go ahead and order it by investing Infrastructure points.

A small delegation of monks, with a Prior at its head, will work with local resources, making the Abbey and thus the whole order even richer. 466 ID_STRUC_PRIORY $ID_STRUC_PRIORY 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Wine Honey & Wax Drugs & Herbs Livestock Piety_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Income Bonus_Steward 1 0 0

name: Cistercian Monastery
bonus_summary: Food 3, Money 6, Stewardship -2 | from: Wine, Fruits, Drugs & Herbs, Livestock
search:
Cistercian Monastery This is an uncommon upgrade of the 'common' Monastery with a high Piety requirement, and although costly to set up, it is well worth the investment in the long run (it is thus exclusive to the Abbey). It's probably not a good idea to invest in a Cistercian Monastery in unstable regions, as its loss would be a severe blow.

As the economic weight of the Cistercian order increased, it favored the Church over the many feudal petty lords in the mind of many people.

Contrary to the Monastery, a Cistercian Monastery will generate Religious Works without consuming Parchments, it can be the desired outcome, or a problem for the region.

A reformed order branch of the Benedictine Order (which at its peaks was made of nearly one hundred thousand monasteries!), the Cistercian Order was putting more emphasis on asceticism and manual labor. It also had an important role in the Christianisation of some parts of Europe. 467 ID_STRUC_CISTERCIAN_MONASTERY $ID_STRUC_CISTERCIAN_MONASTERY 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious   Religious Craftsmanship  Wine Fruits Drugs & Herbs Livestock Piety_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Income Bonus_Steward 1 0 0  Monastery

name: Cistercian Grange
bonus_summary: Food 3, Money 3, Stewardship -1 | from: Wine, Fruits, Drugs & Herbs, Livestock
search:
Cistercian Grange Akin to a Priory, a Cistercian Grange is a small community of monks exploiting a small parcel of land.
It's possible to have 2 Cistercian Granges per region. 468 ID_STRUC_CISTERCIAN_GRANGE $ID_STRUC_CISTERCIAN_GRANGE 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Grain   Wine Fruits Drugs & Herbs Livestock Piety_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Income Bonus_Steward 1 0 0 Food Bonus: 8%

name: Mausoleum
bonus_summary: Piety 3, Stewardship 3 | from: Brass, Stone, Religious Craftsmanship
search:
Mausoleum A Mausoleum is the finely crafted resting place of an important figure. Well-known mausoleum includes the ones built for Roman emperors Hadrian or Augustus, but potentially any noble could have one built, although doing so outside of a Walled Cemetery is not something the Church is fond about.

It is a rare building proposal, providing a significant Legacy income. 469 ID_STRUC_MAUSOLEUM $ID_STRUC_MAUSOLEUM 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Marble   Brass Stone Religious Craftsmanship  Piety_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 0 0

name: Library
modifiers_note_html:
Transverse output: 5 piety from Muslim Scholar
Transverse output: 5 piety from Muslim Savant Study
search:
Library This building is a more modest version of the Science Academy. Fairly common in many Muslim cities, it will help the savants and philosophers in their endeavors.

In the IXth century, the Caliph of Baghdad built a library to includes many manuscripts and books from around the world (from Greece to China). It became so large that it had to be expanded greatly by his son, who turned the initial building into an academy of wisdom and science, with many scholars greeted to study. Over the years, more libraries opened in major cities during the 'Islamic Golden Age'. It abruptly ended with the Mongol invasion and the sack of Baghdad (1258 CE). 470 ID_STRUC_MUS_LIBRARY $ID_STRUC_MUS_LIBRARY 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Parchment & Vellum   Religious Craftsmanship    Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 0 1 0 Transverse output: 5 piety from Mus Scholar Transverse output: 5 piety from Mus Savant Study

name: Mashad
bonus_summary: Piety 4 | from: Brass, Stone, Religious Craftsmanship
search:
Mashad The place of a Martyr in Islam, mausoleums (or Mashad) in the Muslim culture were geared more toward religious figures. This building will thus bolster more Piety than a Christian mausoleum, while providing slightly less Legacy.

The structure has a very low priority, after all martyrs are rare, but might be granted for free if a Muslim leader with a high Piety rating (4 or more) dies in the region, provided the people there are mostly Muslims (EVENT). 471 ID_STRUC_MUS_MAUSOLEUM $ID_STRUC_MUS_MAUSOLEUM 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Marble   Brass Stone Religious Craftsmanship  Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 0 1 0 Piety Bonus: 15%

name: Zawiya
search:
Zawiya This is an establishment for religious teaching dedicated to preparing men to become shaykhs (lately known as sheikhs), i.e. wise men. It subscribes to the principle of takiyya, or nondivulgation of knowledge to the non-initiate. Usually, it consists of a courtyard with a central pool and is surrounded by cells located behind galleries.
This is the closest to the equivalent of a Monastery in the Islam creed, as it is very much frowned upon to not live among the people, even for the Clergy (including taking spouses and having children).

It might turn away some men from secular matters and Clergy is exempted from being drafted. 472 ID_STRUC_MUS_SCHOOL $ID_STRUC_MUS_SCHOOL 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious   Religious Craftsmanship      Manpower_Output Piety_Output  0 1 0 Legedu Bonus: 2% Lobbyhidden: 0

name: Tariqa
search:
Tariqa This building represents a small community of Sufi, also called a Tariqa. Foreigners tend to see them as the equivalent of Christian monasteries, but the Sufi reject this appellation. They congregate around the tomb of a saintly figure, which is often placed at the center of the building. A mystic community, Sufis strive to provide the Baraka to their adepts, i.e. a blessing of sort.

EVENT: In practical terms, a Sufi brotherhood has some chance to provide 'luck', an unfathomable resource that will be used automatically to avoid characters' fate events, death in battles and assassination attempts.

As with the Sufi House, it depends on donation for the welfare of its devotees. 473 ID_STRUC_MUS_SUFI_BROTHERHOOD $ID_STRUC_MUS_SUFI_BROTHERHOOD 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Livestock Legumes   Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 0 1 0 Flavor: 0

name: Aromatic Herbs garden
search: Aromatic Herbs garden Often dependent on a religious or scientific building, here religious persons (monks in the Christian worlds), or secular herborists would cultivate on a large scale the many herbs necessary for medical and alchemical practices. 474 ID_STRUC_HERB_GARDEN $ID_STRUC_HERB_GARDEN 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious   Drugs & Herbs      Piety_Output  1 1 0

name: Scriptorium
search:
Scriptorium Also called copying chambers, the Scriptorium would see monks (in the Christian world) or scribes (elsewhere) create copies of important books, either in their original language or a translation of it (which led sometimes to magistral errors with rippling effects for centuries).

It was fairly common that some works would be sold to nobles or savants, for a good profit. 475 ID_STRUC_SCRIPTORIUM $ID_STRUC_SCRIPTORIUM 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Parchment & Vellum Religious Craftsmanship      Income_Output Piety_Output  1 1 0

name: Alchemist Laboratory
bonus_summary: Money 3, Stewardship 2 | from: Sulfur, Naphta, Tin
search:
Alchemist Laboratory This alchemist will provide several transformed materials to the authorities or wealthy merchants, far from the cliché of being only focussed on the fabled Philosophical Stone.

Alchemy dates back to antiquity but was particularly developed in the Byzantine empire. It also saw widespread development in some parts of the Arabic world. It often had as a goal the transmutation into precious metals of more common matter, but would also touch on other subjects, as alchemists had recipes for whitewash, soap, beer, Greek Fire (used by the Byzantine navy), and cinnabar pigment (used profusely by the Byzantine administration).
Until the XVIIth century the words alchemy and chemistry were perfectly interchangeable for scientists.

EVENT: This Alchemist Laboratory may, on extremely rare occasions, generate the Gold trade goods in the region, although hostile individuals will always accuse him of perpetrating a hoax! 476 ID_STRUC_ALCHEMIST_LABORATORY $ID_STRUC_ALCHEMIST_LABORATORY 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Lead   Sulfur Naphta Tin  Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Steward 1 1 0

name: Bishopric
bonus_summary: Piety 4, Stewardship -1 | from: Stone, Tiles & Brick, Silver
modifiers_note_html:
Replaces: Parish Church
Transverse output: Gain 2.0 piety for any building under Piety category. Limit 24.
Convert Bonus: 15%
search:
Bishopric A Bishopric is the place where a bishop, a magistratre of the Church (including the Orthodoxic one) administrates the multiple parishes under his control.
This building is pivotal to regional development, as it will open access to the second part of tier II Piety buildings.

Initially named by secular rulers and coming from the aristocracy, the Papacy promulgated several reforms to get this privilege back, with high intensity crisis triggered from this struggle (as in the Holy Roman Empire with the crisis investiture). The bishops function also include ordonning new priests and extra bishops, sanctify places and distribute the Holy Chrism (Saint Chrême), one of the 3 holy oil of the Christian liturgy.

Brass was often used in the making of the implements of the faith, when Silver and Gold was considered too costly. 477 ID_STRUC_BISHOPRIC $ID_STRUC_BISHOPRIC 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Brass   Stone Tiles & Brick Silver  Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 0 0 Transverse output: Gain 2.0 piety for any building under Piety category. Limit 24. Convert Bonus: 15% Parish Church

bonus_summary: Piety 4 | from: Stone, Tiles & Brick, Silver

Replaces: Parish Church

Convert Bonus: 15%
search: Fortified Church A fortified church has been deemed necessary in this region, as the area is far from being a safe place. The building is still primarily a church, but with some concern for defensiveness (a good example would be the Lisbon Cathedral, built during the Reconquista, which possesses crenellations and two flanking towers), as such, it will help maintain a firm authority in the region. 478 ID_STRUC_FORTIFIED_CHURCH $ID_STRUC_FORTIFIED_CHURCH 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Brass   Stone Tiles & Brick Silver  Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 0 0 Transverse output: Gain 2.0 piety for any building under Piety category. Limit 20. Convert Bonus: 15% Parish Church

name: Mosque
bonus_summary: Piety 4 | from: Stone, Tiles & Brick, Silver
modifiers_note_html:
Replaces: Small Mosque
Transverse output: Gain 2.0 piety for any building under Piety category. Limit 24.
Convert Bonus: 15%
search:
Mosque This building is a pivotal one, as it will unlock the second part of Tier II Piety building.
The Mosque is the place of worship for Muslim populations and is the successor building to the Small Mosque.

Its role is not limited to a religious one, as it often has adjoining buildings like a madrasa (library) or a university. It also routinely serves as a social gathering point for influential people. Mosques are very varied in their architecture and there is no strict rule on how many towers (minarets) or domes it should have. It all depends of the religious authorities and the funds they can bring for its construction (often from donations). 479 ID_STRUC_MUS_MOSQUE $ID_STRUC_MUS_MOSQUE 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Religious Craftsmanship   Stone Tiles & Brick Silver  Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 0 1 0 Transverse output: Gain 2.0 piety for any building under Piety category. Limit 24. Convert Bonus: 15% Small Mosque

bonus_summary: Piety 4 | from: Stone, Tiles & Brick, Silver

Loyalty Bonus: 15
Convert Bonus: 12%
search:
Temple This temple is a major gathering point for believers of different religions, including Jews, Hindus, and Buddhists. It has a pivotal role in the religious development of the region, as it gives access to the upper part of Tier II Piety buildings.

As with its Christian counterpart, the Bishopric, Brass metal was also used to make decorative objects, such as candlesticks and chalice. These objects were often highly ornate and were prized for their beauty and craftsmanship. 480 ID_STRUC_TEMPLE $ID_STRUC_TEMPLE 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Drugs & Herbs   Stone Tiles & Brick Silver  Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 0 0 0 Loyalty Bonus: 15 Convert Bonus: 12%

name: Astronomer Workshop
bonus_summary: Money 2, Piety -2, Stewardship 2 | from: Copper, Brass
search:
Astronomer Workshop This workshop houses an astronomer with some money to spare for scientific instruments (Such as an astrolabe or an armillary sphere) and treaties. Observation will be made with the naked eye though, as the first telescope, a mere spyglass will not appear before the XVIIth century.

This building is a relatively uncommon proposal.
EVENT: It will help remove (with a low chance per turn) the Occultist building in his region and all surrounding regions (a rarity), as demystifying the secrets of the world and universe is an efficient way to fight off irrational fears and beliefs. 481 ID_STRUC_ASTRONOMER_WORKSHOP $ID_STRUC_ASTRONOMER_WORKSHOP 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Copper   Copper Brass   Income_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 1 0

name: Song School
bonus_summary: Money -1, Piety 3, Stewardship 2 | from: Parchment & Vellum, Religious Craftsmanship
search:
Song School This building represents the various Song, Monastic, and Grammar schools of the Middle Ages. EVENT: When it is built there is a 35% chance that a Freemen will be converted to a Noble population.

The song school was a kind of elementary school where boys would learn to sing Latin hymns. The monastic school would provide a general culture, tinted with a religious approach in a systematic way (it was taught by monks). Grammar school would be reserved for the nobles and would teach you how to write and read Latin, with some notions of politics. 482 ID_STRUC_MEDIEVAL_SCHOOL $ID_STRUC_MEDIEVAL_SCHOOL 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Parchment & Vellum Religious Craftsmanship   Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 0 0 Noblegrow Bonus: 20% Noble Scmaxr Bonus: 20%

name: Mystery Plays
search: Mystery Plays Mystery or Miracle plays are an early form of public entertainment, with a strong religious theme. Much more rarely, a play done by craftsmen called a ministerium could be done. 483 ID_STRUC_MYSTERY_PLAYS $ID_STRUC_MYSTERY_PLAYS 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Tools    Piety_Output Bonus_Infra 1 0 0 Piety Bonus: 10% Revolt Decrease: 12% Loyalty Bonus: 6

name: Science Academy
bonus_summary: Money -2, Piety 6, Stewardship 6 | from: Marble, Brass
search:
Science Academy This large and costly building is the improved version of the more common Library. Only a few Muslim cities will have it and it will help greatly the savants and philosophers in their endeavor.
The Legacy it can generate is exceptionnal, but it is prone from being destroyed in case of a raid.

In the IXth century, the Caliph of Baghdad built a library to includes many manuscripts and books from around the world (from Greece to China). It became so large that it has to be expanded greatly by his son, who turned the initial building into an academy of wisdom and science, with many scholars greeted to study. Over the years, during the 'Islamic Golden Age', more libraries opened in major cities. It abruptly came to an end with the Mongol's invasion and the sack of Baghdad (1258 CE). 484 ID_STRUC_MUS_SCIENCE_ACADEMY $ID_STRUC_MUS_SCIENCE_ACADEMY 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Parchment & Vellum   Marble Brass   Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 0 1 0 Legedu Bonus: 7% Lobbyhidden: 0

name: Theological School
bonus_summary: Piety 5 | from: Stone, Tiles & Brick, Silver
search:
Theological School A place where theologists representing various currents of Islam debates and instruct the new generations. It was a time when the faith was still relatively young and the split between Sunni and Shia was less important. Other currents also subsisted.
EVENT: The building will rarely produces famous theological books, providing a large Legacy bonus, but only if the nation hosting the theological school is one of the top ten Piety producers. 485 ID_STRUC_MUS_THEOLOGICAL_SCHOOL $ID_STRUC_MUS_THEOLOGICAL_SCHOOL 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious   Religious Craftsmanship  Stone Tiles & Brick Silver  Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 0 1 0

name: Royal Khalwa
bonus_summary: Money -10, Piety 6, Stewardship 8 | from: Gold, Luxury, Precious Stones, Parchment & Vellum
search:
Royal Khalwa An exquisitely decorated funerary chamber, destined for a royal person. It must be part of a Necropolis, and will be decorated with faiences and a canopy of finely chiseled muqarnas (honeycomb-like embellishment in a stalactite manner). Tombs will be of marble.
It is not uncommon to be guarded by a small body of elite soldiers, if only to prevent dessecration by pillagers.
The Royal Tomb stands to gain immense value from the trade of precious goods, which not only enhances its majestic aura but also contributes to the renown of the nation in possession. However the associated expenses will be consequent. 486 ID_STRUC_MUS_ROYAL_TOMB $ID_STRUC_MUS_ROYAL_TOMB 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Gold   Gold Luxury Precious Stones Parchment & Vellum Piety_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 0 1 0 Legacy Bonus: 3

name: Reliquary
bonus_summary: Piety 6 | from: Brass, Marble, Precious Stones
search:
Reliquary This is the final resting place of a pious figure of the faith. This building can appear spontaneously in a region where a pious (Piety rating 4 or more) character dies (EVENT). There the population will build a beautiful vault where people can pray and perhaps even touch one of the remains of the soon-to-be-sanctified.

Although nothing is proven by the savants of the time, it is certain that often people who touch the remains will feel better and healthier. 487 ID_STRUC_RELIQUARY $ID_STRUC_RELIQUARY 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Gold   Brass Marble Precious Stones  Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 0 0 Gs Health Bonus: 1% Lobbyhidden: 0

name: Necropolis
search:
Necropolis Necropolises exist in most religions, as a way to protect the deceased graves and as a respectful (and convenient) way to give homage to the dead.

There's a slight chance to avoid a negative token event (i.e. losing a progress token or gaining a disorder token) if you have at least one Necropolis in your nation (EVENT).

A Necropolis is often a walled place and can be serviced by a small host of clergymen, themselves helped in their tasks by commoners. It is not rare that a Necropolis has also an adjoining 'funerary town', a small village that lives thanks to the services needed by the religious people in charge of the site. 488 ID_STRUC_NECROPOLIS $ID_STRUC_NECROPOLIS 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Ceramics   Glassware Pottery Silver  Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 1 0 Health Bonus: 5% Piety Bonus: 15%

name: Religious Festivities
bonus_summary: Piety 4, Stewardship 2 | from: Wine, Honey & Wax, Fishes
search:
Religious Festivities EVENT: When this structure completes, a large temporary increase in Loyalty and Piety generation will happen, as people will be happy with the ongoing event. Productivity will suffer though, mostly in Infrastructure, but also in Agricultural and Commerce.
The structure is destroyed once completed, as it represents an ongoing event.
You need the presence of local Religious Works in the region (only) for this structure to be allowed, even if none are used up. 489 ID_STRUC_RELIGIOUS_FESTIVITIES $ID_STRUC_RELIGIOUS_FESTIVITIES 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious Religious Craftsmanship    Wine Honey & Wax Fishes  Piety_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 1 0

name: Seer
bonus_summary: Money 3, Piety -2 | from: Honey & Wax, Drugs & Herbs
search:
Seer A Seer has some chance to provide 'luck', an unfathomable resource that will be used automatically to avoid characters fate events, death in battles and assassination attempts (EVENT).
It's a practice that all monotheistic religions discourage as being unfaithful though.

In many cultures throughout history, people have sought guidance from seers who claimed to have the ability to predict the future or to provide insight into hidden knowledge. 490 ID_STRUC_SEER $ID_STRUC_SEER 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious     Honey & Wax Drugs & Herbs   Piety_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety 0 0 0 Flavor: 0

name: Cathedral Foundation
search:
Cathedral Foundation The Cathedral is a massive structure, built in three distinct steps. The final one will be a building generating a lot of Piety, Legacy and even a faction-wide modifier with a religious theme.
You'll need a decent road network to continue working on this site.

The construction of a Cathedral in the Middle Ages was a complex and labor-intensive process that typically involved a large number of skilled workers, including stonemasons, carpenters, blacksmiths, and other craftsmen.

Once the design was complete, the construction process would begin by laying the foundation of the building.
This would typically involve digging a large hole and filling it with a mixture of sand, gravel, and other materials to create a sturdy base for the Cathedral. 491 ID_STRUC_CATHEDRAL1 $ID_STRUC_CATHEDRAL1 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Stone   Wood    Piety_Output Bonus_Infra Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 0 0 Infra Penalty: 10% Bishopric Fortified Church

name: Cathedral Construction Site
search:
Cathedral Construction Site This is the 2nd step of the construction of an imposing and monumental Cathedral.
The final step will provide several benefits to the owning nation.

In this step, the building of the walls is well underway, using blocks of stone that are carefully cut and shaped to fit together. As the Cathedral begins to take shape, the workers will add additional features, such as windows, doors, and arches, to give the building its distinctive appearance.

The roof of the Cathedral will typically be made of wood and will be supported by a network of beams and columns. 492 ID_STRUC_CATHEDRAL2 $ID_STRUC_CATHEDRAL2 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Religious Craftsmanship   Stone    Piety_Output Bonus_Infra Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 0 0 Infra Penalty: 10%

search:
Cathedral This is the final step in building a monumental Cathedral. A beacon of Christian faith, this building generates significant Piety and Legacy, and will provide a modifier that is contextual to the regional circumstances, if Christian only (EVENT). 

The interior of this Cathedral has been decorated with frescoes, stained glass windows, and other artwork .

The construction of a Cathedral can take many years to complete, and the process can often be disrupted by war, disease, or other events. 493 ID_STRUC_CATHEDRAL3 $ID_STRUC_CATHEDRAL3 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Religious Craftsmanship   Frankincense Marble Luxury Parchment & Vellum Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 0 0 Piety Bonus: 35%

search:
Grand Mosque Foundation The Large Mosque is a massive structure, built in three distinct steps. The final one will be a building generating a lot of Piety, Legacy and even a faction-wide modifier with a religious theme.
You'll need a decent road network to continue working on this site.

The construction of a Large Mosque was a complex and labor-intensive process that typically involved a large number of skilled workers, including stonemasons, carpenters, bricklayers, and other craftsmen.

Once the design was complete, the construction process would begin by laying the foundation of the building.
This would typically involve digging a large hole and filling it with a mixture of sand, gravel, and other materials to create a sturdy base for the mosque. 494 ID_STRUC_MUS_LARGE_MOSQUE1 $ID_STRUC_MUS_LARGE_MOSQUE1 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Stone   Wood    Piety_Output Bonus_Infra Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 0 1 0 Infra Penalty: 10% Mosque

name: Grand Mosque Construction Site
search:
Grand Mosque Construction Site This is the 2nd step of the construction of an imposing Large mosque.
The final step will provide several benefits to the owning nation.

In this step, the building of the walls is well underway, using bricks or blocks of stone that are carefully cut and shaped to fit together. As the mosque begins to take shape, the workers will add additional features, such as windows, doors, and arches, to give the building its distinctive appearance.

Often a dome will be constructed in lieu of the highest roof, as it symbolizes the vault of heaven, and its curved shape is thought to represent the unity and universality of Allah. 495 ID_STRUC_MUS_LARGE_MOSQUE2 $ID_STRUC_MUS_LARGE_MOSQUE2 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Religious Craftsmanship   Stone    Piety_Output Bonus_Infra Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 0 1 0 Infra Penalty: 10%

name: Grand Mosque
bonus_summary: Money -2, Piety 5, Stewardship 1 | from: Precious Stones, Marble, Luxury, Parchment & Vellum
search:
Grand Mosque This is the final step in building a very Large Mosque. A beacon of the Muslim faith, this building generates significant Piety and Legacy, and will provide a modifier that is contextual to the regional circumstances, if Muslim only (EVENT). 

The interior of the Large Mosque will be decorated with intricate patterns and designs and will include a prayer hall and a minbar (pulpit) from which the imam (religious leader) will deliver sermons.

The construction of a Large Mosque can take many years to complete, and the process can often be disrupted by war, disease, or other events. 496 ID_STRUC_MUS_LARGE_MOSQUE3 $ID_STRUC_MUS_LARGE_MOSQUE3 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Religious Craftsmanship   Precious Stones Marble Luxury Parchment & Vellum Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 0 1 0 Piety Bonus: 35%

name: Large Temple
bonus_summary: Money -2, Piety 5 | from: Drugs & Herbs, Precious Stones, Luxury, Parchment & Vellum
search:
Large Temple This Large Temple represents a major place of faith either for the Jewish community or for polytheist faiths like Hindus and Buddhists. It provides significant bonuses in terms of Piety and Legacy.

When it comes to the layout of a Jewish temple, or synagogue, it is typically characterized by a central sanctuary space as well as a bimah (raised platform) where the Torah is read.

Decoration will be sterner than one of a Buddhist temple which will have instead bright colors. Whereas a Buddhist temple is made of several smaller buildings (e.g. Pagodas), a Jewish temple will often have a single main building. 497 ID_STRUC_LARGE_TEMPLE $ID_STRUC_LARGE_TEMPLE 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Drugs & Herbs   Drugs & Herbs Precious Stones Luxury Parchment & Vellum Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety 0 0 0 Piety Bonus: 25% Temple

name: Hagia Sophia
bonus_summary: Money -5, Piety 16, Stewardship 4 | from: Frankincense, Marble, Luxury, Parchment & Vellum
search:
Hagia Sophia This World Wonder generates significant Piety and Legacy, and will provide a modifier that is contextual to the regional circumstances, if Christian only (EVENT).

The Hagia Sophia, over five centuries since its completion in 537 C.E, remains recognized as the world's most impressive cathedral in the XIth century. A testament to Byzantine architecture, its construction was a Herculean endeavor commissioned by Emperor Justinian. The empire's artistic ambition (and architectural superiority) is evident in its huge distinctive dome, ingeniously supported by a solid network of pendentives and semi-domes. Inside, the expansive nave is adorned with detailed mosaics and marble pillars, standing as a monument to Byzantine craftsmanship.
In term of physical footprint, only the Great Pyramid of Giza in Egypt rivaled the Hagia Sophia. 498 ID_STRUC_BYZ_HAGIA_SOPHIA $ID_STRUC_BYZ_HAGIA_SOPHIA 3 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Religious Craftsmanship   Frankincense Marble Luxury Parchment & Vellum Income_Output Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 0 0 Piety Bonus: 75%

Mendicant Friary Urban monastic house of a mendicant order focused on preaching, poverty, and service rather than landholding. Such friaries spread learning and charity in growing cities and strengthened Church authority among the urban population.
EVENT: This building has a low chance per turn to remove permanently 1 Unrest point from a peasant or freemen population if it has 4 or more Unrest (each population unrest is 1 penalty to regional loyalty). 
--
The Franciscan Friary of Assisi, founded around 1210, is a historical example of such a religious community. 499 ID_STRUC_MENDICANT_FRIARY $ID_STRUC_MENDICANT_FRIARY 3 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Legumes   Livestock Fishes   Food_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 18 Convert Bonus: 20%

The Sorbonne College in Paris, founded circa 1257, is a historical example of such an academic institution. 500 ID_STRUC_UNIVERSITY_COLLEGE $ID_STRUC_UNIVERSITY_COLLEGE 3 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Parchment & Vellum   Marble Brass   Piety_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Legedu Bonus: 7% Song School

The Toulouse Inquisition in Languedoc, instituted in 1233, is a historical example of such a tribunal. 501 ID_STRUC_INQUISITION_OFFICE $ID_STRUC_INQUISITION_OFFICE 2 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious  Tools   Wood Lead Coal  Metal_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 1 0 Flavor: 0

Freemen Cottages Some freemen have come to dwell in the region. They can work their land as they wish, provided they pay their taxes, are sufficiently pious, and recognize the local lord as their liege. They will support him if he is fair, and can help develop the wealth of the region if not impeded too much by the authorities.

This building will favor the appearance of the Freemen's social class (workers, craftsmen) while reducing the chance to get a Peasant, when you get a new population. 509 ID_STRUC_FREEMEN_COTTAGES $ID_STRUC_FREEMEN_COTTAGES 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Tiles & Brick   Horses Tools   Manpower_Output Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income 1 0 0 Health Bonus: 6% Trade Acumen Bonus: 1 Freemengrow Bonus: 25% Peasantgrow Penalty: 25%

Nobles Houses The wealthier or the nobility of the tribe live in these large houses, enjoying the amenities that is provided to them. In exchange, they usually lend support to the local chief or warlord.

This building will favor the appearance of the Freemen's social class (workers, craftsmen) while reducing the chance to get a Peasant, when you get a new population. 510 ID_STRUC_TN_NOBLE_HOUSES $ID_STRUC_TN_NOBLE_HOUSES 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Livestock   Horses Precious Stones   Steward_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Steward 0 0 1 Loyalty Bonus: 6 Revolt Decrease: 15% Freemengrow Bonus: 25% Peasantgrow Penalty: 25%

name: Muslim Tenants Houses
search:
Muslim Tenants Houses There was no serfdom in Muslim lands, except in a few backward regions. Many Muslims were considered freemen and would work the land of a landlord, retroceding a fraction of their harvest to him as retribution. The landlord in some cases did not even own in perpetuity the land. Indeed he could be under the Iqta' system, the equivalent of an apanage, but much more widespread and not reserved to the high-ranked nobles.

This building will favor the appearance of the Freemen's social class (workers, craftsmen) while reducing the chance to get a Peasant, when you get a new population. 511 ID_STRUC_MUS_TENANTS_HOUSES $ID_STRUC_MUS_TENANTS_HOUSES 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Tiles & Brick   Horses Tools   Manpower_Output Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income 0 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 6 Food Bonus: 8% Freemengrow Bonus: 25% Peasantgrow Penalty: 25%

modifiers_note_html: Transverse output: Gain 1.5 stewardship for any building under Stewardship category. Limit 18.
search:
Modest Muslim Landlord The feudal hierarchy did not exist per se in the Muslim world, although there were a large variety of situations. A common one was the existence of a minor noble with some lands, with tenants working it. They had much greater freedom in their work and how they led their life, compared to the serfs of feudal Europe those conditions confined to slavery.

This structure is a mandatory building to access the second part of Tier I Stewardship structures, which will provide more population management tools. 516 ID_STRUC_MUS_MODEST_LANDLORD $ID_STRUC_MUS_MODEST_LANDLORD 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Preserved Food   Tiles & Brick Horses Salt Silver Steward_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Income 0 1 0 Transverse output: Gain 1.5 stewardship for any building under Stewardship category. name: Lord's Tribunal
search:
Lord's Tribunal The Lord's Tribunal is the place (often in his own manor) where the local lord will give swift and sometimes unfair justice to the people of his domain. If you are a freeman or burgher, you might get the help of a scribe or the clergy to defend your case, otherwise, you better hope your lord is in good mood!

An increase in Revolt Risk is only relevant if the regional Loyalty is low, but it will magnify the revolt if it ever happens. 521 ID_STRUC_LORD_TRIBUNAL $ID_STRUC_LORD_TRIBUNAL 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship         Steward_Output  1 0 0 Loyalty Bonus: 8 Revolt Increase: 10%

name: Theme's Tribunal
search: Theme's Tribunal This is a Tribunal in a theme juridiction. Byzantine laws are applied here, convoluted yet sophisticated compared to Western Europe standards, this 'Corpus Juris Civilis' has been elaborated by emperor Justinian himself and will be adopted only around the XIIth century by western kingdoms. 522 ID_STRUC_BYZ_TRIBUNAL $ID_STRUC_BYZ_TRIBUNAL 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship         Steward_Output  0 0 0 Loyalty Bonus: 12 Revolt Decrease: 7%

name: Tribal Tribunal
search:
Tribal Tribunal Justice is often rendered by the local chief, sometimes by the elders. The common people are often treated with more fairness than in Feudal Europe, as the opinion of the community remains of utmost importance to the tribal ruler.

An increase in Revolt Risk is only relevant if the regional Loyalty is low, but it will magnify the revolt if it ever happens. 523 ID_STRUC_TN_TRIBAL_TRIBUNAL $ID_STRUC_TN_TRIBAL_TRIBUNAL 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship         Steward_Output  0 0 1 Loyalty Bonus: 4 Revolt Decrease: 10%

name: Qadi's Tribunal
search: Qadi's Tribunal The Qadi is the most common judge in medieval islam. With a deep knowledge of the all-encompassing Shariah, the religious law, he will rule over men over judicial affairs, but his power can extend to other topics, like mediation between merchants or nobles. 524 ID_STRUC_MUS_QADI_TRIBUNAL $ID_STRUC_MUS_QADI_TRIBUNAL 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship         Steward_Output  0 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 7

name: Gallows
search:
Gallows Hanging was a more or less common form of punishment for the seditious and unruly, depending on the temperament of the local lord. Justice was expedited and there was often no place for a middle ground. You were innocent or guilty, and if guilty, death was often around the corner. Given such methods, the population stayed mostly docile, until the lid broke and a widespread revolt occurred.

An increase in Revolt Risk is only relevant if the regional Loyalty is low, but it will magnify the revolt if it ever happens. 525 ID_STRUC_GALLOWS $ID_STRUC_GALLOWS 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship     Wood    Manpower_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Steward 1 0 0 Loyalty Bonus: 12 Revolt Increase: 20%

name: Diya Perceptor
search: Diya Perceptor The diya, or blood money is perceived by these men. This is a form of justice where the offended (or family of the deceased) gets money from the offender. That's a manner to settle an issue between 2 parties willing to conclude an issue without relying on bodily punishment, like flogging or stoning to death. It was a relatively common procedure when a noble or wealthy has been found guilty of an offense by a judge (qadi). 526 ID_STRUC_MUS_BLOOD_MONEY_PERCEPTOR $ID_STRUC_MUS_BLOOD_MONEY_PERCEPTOR 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship         Income_Output Steward_Output  0 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 10

name: Stocks
search:
Stocks Stocks were wooden frames with holes for the person's arms and sometimes legs. Being left in the stocks publicly for hours or days was both painful and humiliating. Although, all things considered, this was a favorable outcome compared to the Gallows…

An increase in Revolt Risk is only relevant if the regional Loyalty is low, but it will magnify the revolt if it ever happens. 527 ID_STRUC_STOCKS $ID_STRUC_STOCKS 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship     Wood    Manpower_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Steward 1 0 0 Loyalty Bonus: 9 Revolt Increase: 10%

name: Gald Executioner
search:
Gald Executioner A very common punishment in Medieval Islam was being submitted to gald or flogging, as it could be made more or less severe (with temporary or permanent injuries), depending on the gravity of the offense or crime. More drastic measures were the Ragm or Stoning to death. Thieves got a special treatment with Qat, the amputation of the hand.

An increase in Revolt Risk is only relevant if the regional Loyalty is low, but it will magnify the revolt if it ever happens. 528 ID_STRUC_MUS_GALD_EXECUTIONER $ID_STRUC_MUS_GALD_EXECUTIONER 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship     Wood    Manpower_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Steward 0 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 9 Revolt Increase: 10%

Loyalty Bonus: 10

search:
Night Watch Equipped with candles, lanterns, various cudgels, and short blades, the much-appreciated Night Watch guards against thieves and brigands. In an epoch where public lighting was nonexistent (except in some cities like Cordoba in al-Andalus), being outside at night could be a terrifying experience.
This Watch can deal with low-level threats but can't be upgraded, contrary to the line of Local, Town, then City watches.

The Candle Shop will provide an efficiency bonus to the watch, resulting in an increase in Stewardship in the region. 529 ID_STRUC_NIGHT_WATCH $ID_STRUC_NIGHT_WATCH 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship         Income_Output Steward_Output  1 1 1 Transverse output: 4 stewardship from Candle Shop Loyalty Bonus: 10 Revolt Decrease: 5%

search: Warchief's Guards These bodyguards often double as an elite retinue in case of war. search: Town Jail The Town Jail is managed by the town mayor and the local watch, and would almost be considered a humane treatment of the guilty (as an alternative to the Gallows) if the living conditions were not so frequently atrocious. name: Alcazar
bonus_summary: Infra -1, Money -2, Manpower -1, Stewardship 7 | from: Gold, Luxury, Precious Stones, Marble
search:
Alcazar This palace is both fortified and beautiful as it serves as one of the means to assert the legacy and supremacy of the Taifa it is dependent on. It will be considered a secondary National Capital for the nation, with all the benefits it entails.
The more luxurious resources are available, the better it is for the overall Stewardship of the ruler. name: Slum
search:
Slum Event Building.

 Slums can appear in regions with 9 or more population and a Health penalty. People will crowd into Shanties and Hovels as they have no other place to got. The only positive effects of Slums are that some of the poor can be enlisted in the army, and you'll get more Black Market opportunities. Beyond that, slums will favor the appearance of epidemics. 550 ID_STRUC_EVT_SLUM $ID_STRUC_EVT_SLUM 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship         Manpower_Output Steward_Output  1 1 0 Loyalty Penalty: 8

name: Lord's Kennel
search:
Lord's Kennel The dogs in this kennel are typically used for hunting and are bred and trained specifically for this purpose. In addition to helping in the hunt, they can track down bandits and thieves and even help the local militia in repelling low intensity raids. EVENT: Each Kennel has a low chance (each turn) to provide the Regional Decision 'Remove Hostiles,' if you have none available.

Quality dogs are highly valued in the Middle Age, as they play a key role in hunting and sporting activities. 552 ID_STRUC_LORD_KENNEL $ID_STRUC_LORD_KENNEL 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Livestock       Food_Output  1 1 0

name: Feudal Town
bonus_summary: Money 2, Manpower 1 | from: Livestock, Grain, Fishes, Flax
modifiers_note_html:
Replaces: Village
Loyalty Bonus: 16
Health Penalty: 5%
Trade Acumen Bonus: 2
Recruit Mpw Reduction: 15%
search:
Feudal Town This town is home to a few hundred inhabitants up to a thousand or two. In times of war, many men can be levied from there, willingly or not.
It has a small but not insignificant commercial activity when things are quieter.
There are two possible but exclusive Town structures, one using Cloth (Feudal Town), the other Tiles & Brick (Medieval Town). Choose wisely!

The layout of a medieval town was often irregular, with narrow, winding streets and tightly packed buildings. Most people would live in small, cramped houses made of wood or stone, with thatched roofs and dirt floors. These houses were often built close together, with little space between them, and they had few amenities or comforts. 553 ID_STRUC_FEUDAL_TOWN1 $ID_STRUC_FEUDAL_TOWN1 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Cloth   Livestock Grain Fishes Flax Manpower_Output Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Manpower 1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 16 Health Penalty: 5% Trade Acumen Bonus: 2 Recruit Mpw Reduction: 15% Village

name: Open Square
search:
Open Square This plaza of sorts is one of the few open spaces in a town. It is used for various purposes, including meeting neighbors, playing games, and holding fairs and festivals.
It requires the presence of a Feudal Town and allows the Town Council. 555 ID_STRUC_OPEN_SQUARE $ID_STRUC_OPEN_SQUARE 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship         Steward_Output  1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 10 Foodkept Bonus: 6% Village Green

name: Dungeon
search:
Dungeon Dungeons could be considered the ancestor of the modern-day penitentiary. EVENT: When built, it is assured to remove a Bandit or reduce by a single level of a Thief impediment in the region (even the powerful Underground Network, which would be reduced to a Thieves' Guild), then there will be a slim chance each turn that it further removes this kind of nuisances (half the success rate of a City watch).

They were often dark, cramped, and damp, and used to hold people considered enemies of the state, such as political prisoners or prisoners of war.
The conditions in Dungeons were often brutal, and prisoners were often subjected to torture or other forms of mistreatment.
Imprisonment as a sentence by itself, and not as a mean to keep people while they wait for their trial or sentence, was rare during antiquity but became widespread in the Middle Ages. 556 ID_STRUC_DUNGEON_PRISON $ID_STRUC_DUNGEON_PRISON 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship         Manpower_Output Steward_Output  1 1 0 Revolt Decrease: 20% Town Jail

name: Town Council
bonus_summary: Money 4, Stewardship 3 | from: Glassware, Dyes, Luxury
search:
Town Council Town Councils can be a mixed blessing for a monarch, as they give the habit to people to rule by themselves.

A Town Council is usually composed of wealthy merchants and landowners who are chosen by their fellow citizens to represent their interests and make decisions on their behalf.
Potentially, it has a wide range of functions: managing the town's finances,  organizing public works projects, and possibly some legislative function, depending on the town charter.

Town Councils make use of Parchments, representing the needs of a nascent bureaucracy.
Having an availability of trade goods typical of a wealthy merchant class will also render the council more influential. The Open Square is necessary to build a Town Council. 557 ID_STRUC_TOWN_COUNCIL $ID_STRUC_TOWN_COUNCIL 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Parchment & Vellum   Glassware Dyes Luxury  Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Authsucc Penalty: 5% Lobbyhidden: 0 Loyalty Bonus: 12 Stewardship Bonus: 12%

search: King's Envoy EVENT: This person has been sent by the King (or Warchief, Emir, etc.) to inform the local population of a new law or edict. Promulgating an edict will typically cost you less in National Authority (down to a minimum of 1). Dispatching such a person has a one-time cost of 1 National Authority point though. 558 ID_STRUC_KINGS_ENVOY $ID_STRUC_KINGS_ENVOY 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship         Steward_Output  1 1 0 Buildedict Bonus: 20%

search:
Tax Collector Tax Collectors were typically appointed by the government or the monarch, collecting taxes on agricultural produce, trade goods, or other forms of wealth.
This could involve going door-to-door to collect taxes, or setting up markets or other places where taxes could be paid.
Tax Collectors were often disliked by the general population because they were seen as a source of government oppression and were sometimes accused of corruption or other wrongdoing. They often had a token group of guards. 559 ID_STRUC_TAX_COLLECTOR $ID_STRUC_TAX_COLLECTOR 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship     Luxury Precious Stones Gold  Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Loyalty Penalty: 7 Simple Taxation Bonus: 45%

Many such persons will agree to be drafted for a military campaign, as they might earn some money and be properly fed.
The town might also be home to the poorer workers who are still nonetheless very valuable in term of workforce. 560 ID_STRUC_SHANTY_TOWN $ID_STRUC_SHANTY_TOWN 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Legumes   Livestock Fishes   Manpower_Output Bonus_Infra Bonus_Manpower 1 1 0 Health Penalty: 6% Infra Bonus: 18% Hovels Huddled Shanties

Medina A Medina is a rare, Muslim-restricted upgrade of the Medieval Town. It's an important place, both from a political, economic and cultural point of view. Life in a Medieval Medina is typically busy and vibrant, with a diverse population of people from different backgrounds and walks of life.

As the Medina replaces the regional town structure, the trade goods used will also change, which is something to factor in the economic development of the region. Worth also noting, it won't be upgradable to the Small City. 561 ID_STRUC_MUS_MEDINA $ID_STRUC_MUS_MEDINA 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Pottery   Salt Livestock Fishes  Food_Output Manpower_Output Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Manpower 1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 18 Commerce Bonus: 10%

Lord's Manor This structure is pivotal in developing Stewardship and National Authority in a region as it is mandatory to access the second part of Tier II Stewardship structures.

This Manor is a large estate owned by a nobleman. Not quite as big and impressive as a Paramount Lord Manor (from French Par à mont) it is big enough to host many rooms with dedicated purposes, such as a great hall, a chapel and a solar (private room of the lord). 562 ID_STRUC_LORD_MANOR $ID_STRUC_LORD_MANOR 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Preserved Food   Luxury    MBP_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Transverse output: Gain 2.0 stewardship for any building under Stewardship category. An Emir residence is equivalent to a Lord's Manor in other nations, with more emphasis on cultural projection and defensiveness, as Emirs would often rule from within a town or city as a minor political power. 563 ID_STRUC_MUS_EMIR_RESIDENCE $ID_STRUC_MUS_EMIR_RESIDENCE 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Preserved Food   Luxury    MBP_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Transverse output: Gain 2.0 stewardship for any building under Stewardship category. name: Royal Architect
search:
Royal Architect The Royal Architect will provide a cost reduction for any structure built, regardless of its category.
In case of sieges, they have a 15% chance per turn to repair a breach in the walls (EVENT).
Medieval architects were familiar with the basic principles of geometry, including concepts such as lines, angles, and geometric shapes. They also used geometric principles in the construction of buildings, such as using the golden ratio to determine the proportions of a structure or using geometric patterns in the design of decorative elements. Most honed their skills and techniques thanks to apprenticeship. 564 ID_STRUC_ROYAL_ARCHITECT $ID_STRUC_ROYAL_ARCHITECT 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Tools   Brass    Income_Output Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Structure Costreduction: 10%

Small cities are more geared toward regional trading than towns and more often than not depend on a network of villages to feed their population. They offer several amenities to the citizens, like one or several religious buildings, a central marketplace and the protection of a city watch. Although many houses are still made of wood, entire neighborhoods can be built out of stone, depending on the location and the wealth of the inhabitants. 565 ID_STRUC_SMALL_CITY1 $ID_STRUC_SMALL_CITY1 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Cloth   Livestock Grain Tiles & Brick Leather Food_Output Manpower_Output Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Manpower 1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 24 Health Penalty: 8% Trade Acumen Bonus: 4 Trade Range Bonus: 1 Recruit Mpw Reduction: 20%

name: Royal Executionner
search:
Royal Executionner The Royal Executioner, in addition to boosting the Loyalty of the region and lowering Revolt Risks, will double the chance to remove the 'Region in Unrest' modifier if present (EVENT).

In many cases, executioners were considered to be among the lowest members of society, and they were often ostracized and shunned by others. However, they were also necessary in a time when there were few effective forms of law enforcement, and they were often given the task of carrying out punishments for crimes such as theft, murder, and other offenses. Executioners were trained in the use of various methods of execution, such as beheading, hanging, and drowning, and they were responsible for carrying out these punishments in public, often in front of large crowds. 567 ID_STRUC_ROYAL_EXECUTIONNER $ID_STRUC_ROYAL_EXECUTIONNER 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship     Weapons Tools   Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 9 Revolt Decrease: 25%

name: Escape Tunnel
bonus_summary: Infra 1, Stewardship 1 | from: Tar, Stone, Wood
search:
Escape Tunnel These tunnels will serve as avenues for soldiers to receive supplies and food, and for the wounded to be evacuated from the ongoing siege. Overall, they offer hope to all defenders that, should things take a turn for the worse, there's a way to evade death. However, in reality, only lords and senior officers are likely to use them.

Escape Tunnels were not very common, as they were difficult and expensive to construct. To be of real practical use, they had to be dug deep underground and with a significant length, two parameters making the excavation a major task to accomplish. Another issue about them was how widespread the knowledge of their existence was, and the more important the task to dig a tunnel was, the harder it was to keep it secret enough that it would not be common knowledge. 568 ID_STRUC_ESCAPE_TUNNEL $ID_STRUC_ESCAPE_TUNNEL 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship     Tar Stone Wood   Bonus_Infra Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 8 Extra Supply: 8 Hitsrecovery: 3

name: City Watch
bonus_summary: Stewardship -3 | from: Gold, Luxury, Precious Stones
search:
City Watch Barring the sending of a rather large army, the City Watch is one of the few forces capable of handling major Thieves' Guilds (even the almighty Underground Network), albeit with a low chance of success, eliminating them entirely.

The City Watch was typically made up of volunteers or paid individuals who patrolled the city's streets and kept an eye out for crime or other problems. They were often armed with weapons such as clubs or swords, and they were responsible for arresting criminals and bringing them before the local authorities for punishment. The city watch was typically organized and supervised by the city's governing body or by the local lord, and they often worked in cooperation with other law enforcement officials, such as the town guard or the constable. 569 ID_STRUC_CITY_WATCH $ID_STRUC_CITY_WATCH 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Wool   Gold Luxury Precious Stones  MBPStockpile Manpower_Output Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Revolt Decrease: 15% Loyalty Bonus: 8
