search: City Council The City Council was typically made up of wealthy and influential members of the city, such as merchants, landowners, and other prominent figures. These individuals were appointed or elected to serve on the council, and they were responsible for making decisions on a wide range of issues, including law and order, trade and commerce, and the administration of city services. The city council would typically meet regularly to discuss and vote on various matters, and they would often consult with other city officials, such as the mayor or the town clerk, before making decisions. 570 ID_STRUC_CITY_COUNCIL $ID_STRUC_CITY_COUNCIL 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Parchment & Vellum   Glassware Dyes Luxury Precious Stones Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Authsucc Penalty: 7% Loyalty Bonus: 16 Stewardship Bonus: 18% Jobprod2 Bonus: 3 Jobprod5 Bonus: 8 Town Council

name: King's Agent
bonus_summary: Money -3, Stewardship 3 | from: Gold, Luxury, Precious Stones
search:
King's Agent Endowed with significant power, the King's Agent is neither truly a spy nor entirely above board. This trusted emissary is dispatched by the monarch to convey crucial messages, edicts, or simply to ensure that orders are executed. Acting as a conduit between the monarch and local lords, he ensures the smooth communication and execution of royal directives. When the King's Agent is stationed in a capital region (including Summer/Winter Palaces), an additional bodyguard is provided for the ruler's protection (EVENT). Though a rare appointment, maintaining a King's Agent can strain the treasury, as he requires funds to hasten the completion of projects and decrees.

Character loyalty bonuses apply only to characters in the same region as the King's Agent. 571 ID_STRUC_KINGS_AGENT $ID_STRUC_KINGS_AGENT 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship     Gold Luxury Precious Stones  Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Ldrloy Bonus: 10 Buildedict Bonus: 10% Jobprod5 Bonus: 5

name: Courthouse
bonus_summary: Money 5, Stewardship -3 | from: Gold, Luxury, Precious Stones
search:
Courthouse The medieval Courthouse wields the king's authority. Comprised of nobles with a sound knowledge of the kingdom's laws, it oversees legal proceedings, ensuring justice for all. The Courthouse hears the pleas of both highborn and commoners, although sentencing may differ based on social class. As a last resort, the victim can call upon a duel or other form of trial that employs the force of arms.

Judges at the Courthouse might be tempted by some bribes if there's an abundance of wealth in the region, which would certainly reduce their fairness and the quality of justice delivered. 572 ID_STRUC_COURTHOUSE $ID_STRUC_COURTHOUSE 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship     Gold Luxury Precious Stones  Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Adminburden Bonus: 1% Maxdemesne Bonus: 1% Authority Bonus: 1%

name: Harem
bonus_summary: Money -4, Stewardship 4 | from: Gold, Luxury, Precious Stones, Marble
search:
Harem The Harem is a secluded area within the royal palace, reserved for the monarch and their consorts. Apart from its role in facilitating the continuation of the lineage, it symbolizes prestige, power, and influence. Adorned with exquisite decorations, the Harem stands as a testament to the grandeur of the royal court.

They are also places of intrigue, which might occasionally play a role in increasing the chance of a coup, albeit minorly.
The Harem guarantees you to have at least one courtesan should you have none (EVENT).
It has a small contingent of guards, although you should not rely too much on them to defend the palace. 573 ID_STRUC_MUS_HAREM $ID_STRUC_MUS_HAREM 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship     Gold Luxury Precious Stones Marble Income_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Newborn Bonus: 40% Lobbyhidden: 0

name: Public Lighting
search:
Public Lighting Public Lighting, a rare occurrence in Medieval times, was a remarkable advancement that heightened safety and fostered community life. While darkness often enshrouded the streets, there were rare instances, such as in Al-Andalus, where public spaces were illuminated, reducing accidents and instilling a sense of security.
Lighting the streets at night will significantly reduce the various incidents that frequently occurred in the winding streets of medieval cities, providing a Health bonus.

Even rarer in Christian land, the non Muslim version will ask for a very large population and Stewardship prerequisite. 574 ID_STRUC_ALD_PUBLIC_LIGHTING $ID_STRUC_ALD_PUBLIC_LIGHTING 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Honey & Wax   Glassware    Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 15 Health Bonus: 5%

name: Summer Palace
bonus_summary: Money -3, Manpower -1, Stewardship 7 | from: Gold, Luxury, Marble
search:
Summer Palace This magnificent Summer Palace serves as a testament to beauty and grandeur, embodying the legacy and prominence of the nation it represents. The palace's allure is found in its intricate decorations, lush gardens, and enchanting fountains, which together create a haven of luxury and elegance.
Designated as a secondary National Capital for the nation, it comes with all the accompanying benefits.
The greater the availability of luxurious resources, the more it enhances the ruler's overall Stewardship. Nevertheless, keeping the palace in its prime state will incur higher costs and necessitate an expanding personnel presence.
Huge and difficult to defend, it will make resisting a siege harder. 576 ID_STRUC_MUS_SUMMERPALACE $ID_STRUC_MUS_SUMMERPALACE 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Precious Stones   Gold Luxury Marble  Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Manpower Bonus_Steward 0 1 0 Legacy Bonus: 5 Capitol: 1 Extra Capital: 1

name: Surface Iron Mining
search:
Surface Iron Mining Surface Iron Mining is a mining method utilized to extract iron ore from shallow deposits near the earth's surface, when other sources such as iron mines or bogs are not viable options. These deposits are typically of lower richness, requiring extensive efforts to achieve a meaningful productivity. However, in certain circumstances, it serves as the sole available source.

This method is highly labor-intensive, placing a heavy burden on the population, but it requires minimal infrastructure, relying solely on men equipped with picks.
The building is restricted to hills. 670 ID_STRUC_SURFACE_IRON_MINING $ID_STRUC_SURFACE_IRON_MINING 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Iron Ore  Tools    Metal_Output Manpower_Output Bonus_Infra Bonus_Metal Bonus_Manpower 1 1 1 Health Penalty: 4%

name: Mercantile Burgh
bonus_summary: Money 2 | from: Wine, Wool, Preserved Food, Flax
search:
Mercantile Burgh This is a Scottish Mercantile Burgh, also known as a 'Toun'. It is a bustling hub for commerce, frequented by merchants, which makes it challenging to defend.
The Burgh can spontaneously emerge in regions with strong Scottish culture and high loyalty levels (75+). 672 ID_STRUC_SCO_MERCANTILE_BURGH $ID_STRUC_SCO_MERCANTILE_BURGH 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce     Wine Wool Preserved Food Flax Food_Output Income_Output Bonus_Income 0 0 0 Simple Taxation Penalty: 20% Commerce Bonus: 10%

name: Baronial Burgh
search:
Baronial Burgh This is a Scottish Baronial Burgh, a hybrid between a military compound or garrison town and a traditional Scottish 'Toun'. It serves as a recruitment center where young men are mustered and enlisted. Additionally, the local authority offers land grants to those brave enough to settle in areas that are still largely untamed and fraught with dangers.
The Burgh can spontaneously emerge in regions with strong Scottish culture and high loyalty levels (75+). 673 ID_STRUC_SCO_BARONIAL_BURGH $ID_STRUC_SCO_BARONIAL_BURGH 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Iron Ore   Weapons Armors   MBPStockpile MBP_Output Manpower_Output Steward_Output Bonus_MBP 0 0 0 Flavor: 0

name: Ecclesiastic Burgh
search: Ecclesiastic Burgh This is a Scottish Ecclesiastic Burgh, also known as a 'Toun'. Led by a skilled priest, the town is not only a center for religious activities but also excels in religious craftsmanship. The community here is particularly strong in its faith, making the town a local pilgrimage site. As a result, a steady stream of pilgrims regularly visit, contributing to rapid population growth and enhancing the town's vitality. 674 ID_STRUC_SCO_ECCLESIASTIC_BURGH $ID_STRUC_SCO_ECCLESIASTIC_BURGH 1 $POP_JOB_PIETY Piety $JOB_KIND_RELIGIOUS Job Kind Religious   Religious Craftsmanship  Brass    Food_Output Piety_Output Bonus_Piety 1 0 0 Foodkept Bonus: 5% Revolt Decrease: 10%

name: Royal Burgh
bonus_summary: Money -2, Stewardship 2 | from: Horses, Weapons, Armors, Luxury
search: Royal Burgh This is a Scottish royal Burgh, also known as a 'Toun'. Under the direct governance of the crown, this settlement serves as a powerful symbol of the King of Scotland's authority over newly acquired territories, leaving no doubt that the Kingdom of Alba is here to stay. Many officials take up residence in the Burgh, which, while enhancing its administrative capabilities, can also strain the state's treasury due to their lavish lifestyles. 675 ID_STRUC_SCO_ROYAL_BURGH $ID_STRUC_SCO_ROYAL_BURGH 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Livestock   Horses Weapons Armors Luxury Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Steward 1 0 0 Flavor: 0

name: Anconan Commercial Enclave
search:
Anconan Commercial Enclave Fondachi (singular Fondacho) are merchant outposts established in foreign lands, this one belongs to Ancona.

They procure amenities for the citizens of the maritime republic in charge of the enclave, and possess a large number of warehouses. Although they compete fiercely with local merchants, they can end up opening new commercial opportunities for the clever ruler (EVENT).

This structure can't be disbanded by the region owner, as strictly speaking, it does not belong to him. 676 ID_STRUC_IMR_ENCLAVE_ACN $ID_STRUC_IMR_ENCLAVE_ACN 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce         Income_Output Steward_Output  0 0 0 Gs Commerce Bonus: 3% Lobbyhidden: 0

name: Clockwise stairs
search:
Clockwise stairs The main stairs of the local fortress will be built clockwise, which will impede assailants when fighting upward, as the majority of them are right-handed and will often have their weapons hit the central column.

This fortification option will provide a single fortification point but is practically costless, as stairs have to be built in any case! 678 ID_STRUC_CLOCKWISE_STAIRS $ID_STRUC_CLOCKWISE_STAIRS 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military           1 1 0

name: Fireclock Maker
search: Fireclock Maker Fire clocks, such as candle clocks, were used to measure time by burning a fuel like a candle or oil. Originating in ancient Asia, they spread to Europe, where they were commonly used in monastic settings to regulate prayer times. For the wealthy, these clocks also served as early personal alarms by attaching weights to the candle, which would drop and make a sound as the candle burned down. 690 ID_STRUC_FIRECLOCK_MAKER $ID_STRUC_FIRECLOCK_MAKER 1 $POP_JOB_COMMERCE Commerce $JOB_KIND_COMMERCE Commerce  Honey & Wax   Brass    Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Income 1 1 0 Loyalty Bonus: 8

name: Public Execution
search:
Public Execution Crimes in nomadic or tribal societies were often punished by death or exile to a hostile land. Executions were almost always public, as the goal was also to convey the message that wrongdoers were punished.

EVENT:<color> In addition to the recurring reduction in revolt risk provided by this building, if the region is experiencing Unrest when it is constructed, the Unrest will be systematically removed. EVENT: Over time, this site will balance the values of piety and stewardship in the region, which is a major asset for generating legacy.

Bronze (made from copper) did not disappear completely during medieval times. With a lower melting point than iron, several nomadic societies found it easier to work with. It was also used for religious ornaments, as it was considered a noble material. name: Underground Fortress
search:
Underground Fortress This is a fortress or castle with an extensive underground network. In addition to the above-ground fortifications, it also consists of tunnels, living quarters, storerooms, and defensive chambers carved directly into the rock, designed to provide shelter and protection during sieges or invasions.
Notable examples include the Château de Brézé in France, featuring vast rock-hewn corridors and defensive galleries beneath the main castle, and the Buda Castle Labyrinth in Hungary, an extensive cave system historically used for defense, storage, and even as a prison. 719 ID_STRUC_UNDERGROUND_FORTRESS $ID_STRUC_UNDERGROUND_FORTRESS 2 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military  Preserved Food   Weapons Armors   MBPStockpile MBP_Output Income_Output Steward_Output Bonus_MBP Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Extra Supply: 15

name: Mandalika Residence
bonus_summary: Food 1, Money 2 | from: Wine, Horses, Salt, Silver
modifiers_note_html: Transverse output: Gain 1.5 stewardship for any building under Stewardship category. Limit 18.
search:
Mandalika Residence This is the residence of the Mandalika, a local chieftain under the authority of a Samanta or Raja. From here, he governs his territory, composed mostly of land-bound peasants and skilled artisans working in village workshops. A few independent farmers, some merchants, and low-ranking scribes may also be found under his jurisdiction.

This structure is a mandatory building to access the second part of Tier I Stewardship structures, which will provide more population management tools. 759 ID_STRUC_IND_MINOR_LORD_MANOR $ID_STRUC_IND_MINOR_LORD_MANOR 1 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Preserved Food   Wine Horses Salt Silver MBP_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Food Bonus_Income 1 0 0 Transverse output: Gain 1.5 stewardship for any building under Stewardship category. Samanta Residence This structure is pivotal in developing Stewardship and National Authority in a region as it is mandatory to access the second part of Tier II Stewardship structures.

This estate belongs to a Samanta, a powerful vassal noble serving under a Raja or Maharaja. Though not as imposing as a royal palace, it is a large compound with multiple halls for administration, worship, and residence, including a great audience chamber, a shrine, and the private quarters of the Samanta. 760 ID_STRUC_IND_LORD_MANOR $ID_STRUC_IND_LORD_MANOR 2 $POP_JOB_STEWARD Stewardship $JOB_KIND_STEWARD Stewardship  Preserved Food   Luxury    MBP_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Transverse output: Gain 2.0 stewardship for any building under Stewardship category. name: Slitherine Chapterhouse
search:
Slitherine Chapterhouse This fortified stronghold is the seat of the Order of Slitherine, founded in ages past by Jawn and Eon Macenail. Born in the rugged lands of Scotland, the Order journeyed south over the centuries - first to the lowlands of England, and then beyond.
Wherever they went, they brought with them the sacred art of military strategy, teaching it to the scattered peoples of the land.
By their wisdom, these folk learned to defend their homes more fiercely or to strategize heroically while others did the dying. 761 ID_STRUC_SPECIAL_FORT $ID_STRUC_SPECIAL_FORT 1 $POP_JOB_MILITARY Military $JOB_KIND_MILITARY Military     Wine Furnitures   MBP_Output Steward_Output Bonus_Manpower 0 0 0 Milexpert Bonus: 20

name: Field Drainage
modifiers_note_html:
Health Bonus: 7%
Transverse output: 10 food from Farm
search:
Field Drainage Networks of ditches and channels carefully engineered to remove excess water from farmland. By reclaiming marshy ground, they expanded cultivable land and protected harvests from floods and stagnation.

(EVENT) There is a 1% chance per turn that the slot usage of this building becomes permanently 0, representing the land's full reclamation and conversion into stable fields. 768 ID_STRUC_FIELD_DRAINAGE $ID_STRUC_FIELD_DRAINAGE 3 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural           1 1 0 Health Bonus: 7% Transverse output: 10 food from Farm

name: Salt Farm Complex
search:
Salt Farm Complex Evaporation ponds and workshops devoted to the extraction and processing of salt, a substance once worth its weight in silver. These complexes sustained food preservation, livestock care, and distant trade networks. Notable medieval examples include Salins-les-Bains in France and Lüneburg in Germany.

EVENT:There is a significant chance to gain a large income from salt taxation, but a small chance to trigger inflation instead. 769 ID_STRUC_SALT_FARM_COMPLEX $ID_STRUC_SALT_FARM_COMPLEX 3 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural   Salt      Food_Output  1 1 0

name: Communal Farms
search: Communal Farms Communal agricultural plots worked collectively by the households of a village or parish. They encouraged cooperation in both labor and resource distribution, shaping the social fabric of medieval rural life. Fountains Abbey in England illustrates the organization and productivity such systems could reach. 770 ID_STRUC_COMMUNAL_FARMS $ID_STRUC_COMMUNAL_FARMS 3 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural   Grain  Legumes    Food_Output Bonus_Manpower 1 1 0 Foodkept Bonus: 8% Peasantgrow Bonus: 25%

name: Monumental Terraces
search: Monumental Terraces Large stepped terraces carved into hillsides to hold both soil and moisture, allowing intensive cultivation where level ground was scarce. These monumental works testify to generations of labor and ingenuity, thus providing extra Legacy Points. The terraced slopes of Cinque Terre in Italy offer an example of this mastery. 771 ID_STRUC_MONUMENTAL_TERRACES $ID_STRUC_MONUMENTAL_TERRACES 3 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural   Grain      Food_Output  1 1 0

name: Dovecote
search:
Dovecote Also known as pigeon lofts, these stone or timber towers sheltered flocks of doves whose meat, eggs, and feathers were valuable commodities. Above all, their droppings provided a potent fertilizer, rich in nitrates and vital for enriching the fields.

This structure is also key to the production of early gunpowder (with Salpeter Works being the only other possible structure), as the guano collected within was an important source of saltpeter. Saltpeter, sulfur and coal are all required to produce gunpowder. 772 ID_STRUC_DOVECOTE $ID_STRUC_DOVECOTE 3 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural         Food_Output  1 1 0 Food Bonus: 12%

name: Saffron Field
search:
Saffron Field Carefully cultivated patches of crocus flowers whose crimson stigmas yielded the rare and precious spice, saffron. Its production demanded patience, delicate hands, and favorable weather. 

If Luxury Goods are present, indicating the existence of a wealthy upper class in the region, Saffron Fields will generate additional Stewardship points. 773 ID_STRUC_SAFFRON_FIELD $ID_STRUC_SAFFRON_FIELD 3 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural   Spices  Luxury    Steward_Output Bonus_Steward 1 1 0

name: Landless Labourers
search:
Landless Labourers Men and women without land or property, compelled to sell their labor to survive. Whether hired seasonally or bound to local estates, these landless laborers formed a fragile yet indispensable pillar of the medieval agrarian economy, ensuring that the fields of others were sown and harvested.
Their working and living conditions will favor unrest and revolts though.
If provided with equipment, this large population of sturdy men can serve as a military levy. 774 ID_STRUC_LANDLESS_LABOURERS $ID_STRUC_LANDLESS_LABOURERS 3 $POP_JOB_AGRI Agriculture $JOB_KIND_AGRI Agricultural     Weapons Flax Horses  Food_Output Infra_Output Bonus_Manpower 1 1 0 Structure Costreduction: 20% (params: $JOB_KIND_AGRI_CONSTEFFECT) Loyalty Penalty: 6 Revolt Increase: 10%

name: Sulfur Mining
search: Sulfur Mining Despite being a difficult and unhealthy task, the extraction of sulfur from volcanic soils or mineral deposits gained importance toward the XIIIth century. It became a critical ingredient in the making of gunpowder and in other early chemical processes. Saltpeter, sulfur and coal are all required to produce gunpowder. 780 ID_STRUC_SULFUR_MINING $ID_STRUC_SULFUR_MINING 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Sulfur      Infra_Output  1 1 0 Health Penalty: 4%

name: Corpse Burners
search:
Corpse Burners Teams assigned (sometimes against their will) to burn contaminated bodies after major epidemics. Their grim work helped slow the spread of deadly diseases, and such crews were often kept apart from the rest of the population.
This building is very cheap to set up, which is one of its quality. 786 ID_STRUC_CORPSE_BURNERS $ID_STRUC_CORPSE_BURNERS 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health     Coal Tar   MBP_Output Manpower_Output Income_Output Bonus_Piety Bonus_Steward 1 1 1 Flavor: 0

name: Mass Grave
search:
Mass Grave These hastily dug burial pits were created to receive large numbers of plague victims. The toll on workers could be high, though providing leather garments for some protection helped mitigate losses. These mass graves were a desperate response to overwhelming mortality and the collapse of normal funerary practices.
This building is very cheap to set up, which is one of its quality. 787 ID_STRUC_MASS_GRAVE $ID_STRUC_MASS_GRAVE 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health     Leather    Metal_Output Manpower_Output Bonus_Manpower 1 1 1 Flavor: 0

name: Yakhtchal
search: Yakhtchal Persian icehouse structures built partly underground with thick walls and domed roofs. Designed to store ice and perishables through scorching summers, they were feats of both engineering and insulation. Similar structures existed across several Muslim regions. Beyond preserving food, they also contributed to better health and were used to keep unguents and medicines cool and effective. 788 ID_STRUC_YAKHTCHAL $ID_STRUC_YAKHTCHAL 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health     Pottery    Food_Output Infra_Output Income_Output Bonus_Food 1 1 1 Health Bonus: 7% Charhealth Bonus: 5%

name: Windmill

This building will provide a significant increase in productivity to peasants and craftsmen. 790 ID_STRUC_WINDMILL $ID_STRUC_WINDMILL 3 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Sails   Grain Flax    Bonus_Food Bonus_Steward 1 1 0 Jobprod0 Bonus: 2 Jobprod1 Bonus: 2

Shaft Furnace These large vertical furnaces were used to smelt iron ore with charcoal and forced air. Such early bloomeries marked a significant step toward producing workable wrought iron on a more reliable scale.
Their metal output greatly aided the rapid equipment of armies. 791 ID_STRUC_SHAFT_FURNACE $ID_STRUC_SHAFT_FURNACE 2 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Iron Ore   Coal    MBP_Output Metal_Output Income_Output Bonus_Infra Bonus_Metal 1 1 0 Health Penalty: 4% Recruit Metal Reduction: 10% Freemengrow Bonus: 5% Bloomery Early Furnace

name: Blast Furnace
search:
Blast Furnace Larger furnaces than the shaft furnace, driven by higher heat and stronger air blasts to smelt iron. Capable of yielding cast iron in quantity, they transformed metallurgy, though the output often required further refining in a finery forge.
Their metal output greatly aided the rapid equipment of armies. 792 ID_STRUC_BLAST_FURNACE $ID_STRUC_BLAST_FURNACE 3 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure  Iron Ore   Coal    MBP_Output Metal_Output Income_Output Bonus_Infra Bonus_Metal 1 1 0 Health Penalty: 4% Recruit Metal Reduction: 20% Freemengrow Bonus: 10%

name: Lazaretto
search: Lazaretto Quarantine stations or plague hospitals designed to confine those suspected of contagion. As some of the earliest institutional defenses against epidemic disease, they reflected a growing awareness of public health. Often tended by clergy, compassion was present, yet these places also served to triage the sick. If they could not be healed, they would be buried or even cremated. 800 ID_STRUC_LAZARETTO $ID_STRUC_LAZARETTO 3 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health  Drugs & Herbs   Coal Frankincense    Bonus_Piety 1 1 0 Piety Bonus: 10% Health Bonus: 3% Flavor: 0

bonus_summary: Money 4, Manpower 2 | from: Drugs & Herbs, Tools
search:
Apothecary Guild A collective of medical practitioners, herbalists, and chemists providing remedies, ointments, and advice. In an age before formal science, apothecaries bridged folk knowledge and emerging medical practice, serving as both healers and suppliers of daily health needs.
This Guild gains an additional powerful ability when at least 6 population units are assigned to Infrastructure.
Thanks to the remedies provided, many men are considered fit for the levy, whereas without the guild they would have remained weak or ill. 801 ID_STRUC_APOTHECARY_GUILD $ID_STRUC_APOTHECARY_GUILD 3 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_HEALTH Job Kind Health     Drugs & Herbs Tools   Manpower_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Manpower 1 1 0 Health Bonus: 12% Flavor: 0 Gs Health Bonus: 4%

name: Surface Gold Mining
search:
Surface Gold Mining Surface Gold Mining is a method used to extract gold from shallow alluvial or near-surface deposits, when deeper gold mines are not available or viable. These deposits are generally dispersed and of limited concentration, requiring sustained manual effort to yield modest but steady returns. In some regions, however, such surface exploitation may represent the only accessible source of gold.

This method is extremely labor-intensive and places a significant strain on the local population, yet it demands little in the way of infrastructure, relying mainly on manpower and simple tools such as pans, shovels, and sluices.
The building requires an adjacent river. 872 ID_STRUC_SURFACE_GOLD_MINING $ID_STRUC_SURFACE_GOLD_MINING 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Gold  Tools    Manpower_Output Income_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Manpower 1 1 1 Health Penalty: 4%

name: Surface Silver Mining
search:
Surface Silver Mining Surface Silver Mining is a method employed to extract silver from shallow deposits close to the earth's surface, typically where deeper silver mines are unavailable or uneconomical. These surface deposits are usually of limited richness and irregular distribution, requiring sustained effort to obtain worthwhile yields, yet in some regions they remain the only exploitable source of silver.

This method is labor-intensive and places a notable burden on the population, while requiring very limited infrastructure, relying primarily on manpower and basic tools.
The building is restricted to hills or rough terrain. 873 ID_STRUC_SURFACE_SILVER_MINING $ID_STRUC_SURFACE_SILVER_MINING 1 $POP_JOB_INFRA Infrastructure $JOB_KIND_INFRA Infrastructure   Silver  Tools    Manpower_Output Income_Output Bonus_Income Bonus_Manpower 1 1 1 Health Penalty: 4%
